## # PROJECT HARBORLIGHT ## Phase 1: Chart the Harbor ### Community Domain Foundat…: C:\Users\kgray\.codex/attachments/d38367f9-d3c4-40c1-b4d7-af3ed96e0680/pasted-text.txt

## My request for Codex:
# PROJECT HARBORLIGHT PHASE 1

## Isolated Branch, Concurrent-Project Compatibility, and Push Addendum

This addendum is authoritative for repository isolation, concurrent-project handling, commit behavior, and final handoff.

Implement Project Harborlight Phase 1 in a completely separate Git branch and worktree.

Do not perform Harborlight work in the canonical checkout.

Do not modify any existing Project One Voyage, Project Wayfarer, Project Sealed Hold, Lanternwake, Universal Language, or convergence branch.

---

# 1. Mandatory Dedicated Branch

Fetch the latest remote state first:

```text
git fetch origin --prune
```

Create a dedicated branch named:

```text
codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor
```

If that exact branch already exists:

1. inspect its ownership, commit history, and worktree;
2. continue it only if it clearly belongs to this Harborlight Phase 1 implementation;
3. otherwise create a non-destructive suffix such as:

```text
codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor-2
```

Never overwrite or force-update an unrelated branch.

---

# 2. Mandatory Dedicated Worktree

Create a separate sibling worktree, using a safe path such as:

```text
../Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1
```

or an equivalent absolute path suitable for the current machine.

Record:

* canonical checkout path;
* Harborlight worktree path;
* branch;
* base commit;
* current `origin/main`;
* upstream;
* active worktrees;
* staged files;
* unstaged files;
* untracked files.

The Harborlight worktree must begin clean.

The canonical checkout must remain untouched.

Do not use a copied directory that is not a registered Git worktree.

If a network-share path causes package-manager or worker-process failures, use a safe mapped drive or a Git-supported worktree location while preserving the same branch. Do not wait until final validation to discover that Node has developed philosophical objections to UNC paths.

---

# 3. Baseline Selection

Branch from the latest accepted integrated baseline available in `origin/main`.

Do not independently merge any unaccepted candidate branch into Harborlight merely because it contains architecture Harborlight will eventually need.

Current known concurrent candidate commits include:

```text
Project One Voyage
integration/lanternwake-phase5-universal-language
4e8f385687b01aa6b1e97452ff76e6e7e3b58b8c

Project Wayfarer
codex/project-wayfarer-phase1-unified-identity
a478d7957e112c9ce6f63fd423746d3df2a697ce

Project Sealed Hold
codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1
4b93b4f4554ba504bec6d6462d39960dd28b4afe
```

Fetch and verify these references, but treat them as read-only architectural context.

Do not:

* merge them;
* rebase onto them;
* cherry-pick them wholesale;
* modify their branches;
* assume they are already accepted;
* reproduce their entire implementations inside Harborlight.

Record the exact Harborlight base commit in:

```text
Development_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Design_Record.md
```

---

# 4. Concurrent-Project Compatibility Rules

Harborlight Phase 1 is running before final cross-project convergence.

Therefore, its implementation must remain deliberately adaptable.

## 4.1 Canonical Chronicle seam

Harborlight must use the existing canonical Tall Tale or Chronicle domain available on its branch.

It must not create another authored-story or runtime architecture.

Do not introduce independent equivalents of:

```text
CommunityCampaign
CommunityChronicleDefinition
CommunityChapter
CommunitySession
CommunityPlayer
```

A Community Listing is catalog identity.

A Community Release is immutable community distribution identity.

Neither is another mutable Chronicle runtime.

Where One Voyage is expected to change canonical domain names or ownership later:

* isolate Chronicle references behind typed service interfaces;
* document the expected convergence seam;
* avoid duplicating canonical business logic;
* retain additive foreign-key compatibility where necessary.

## 4.2 Canonical identity seam

Harborlight must not create another authentication identity.

Community Profile must reference the current canonical person/profile/account identity available on its base.

Because Wayfarer exists on a separate candidate branch:

* isolate identity resolution behind a typed adapter or service;
* avoid embedding `GameMasterUser` assumptions in Harborlight domain logic;
* avoid copying email, credentials, password state, or session state;
* avoid making `PlayerProfile` semantics inseparable from the community domain;
* document how Community Profile will attach to Wayfarer `UserAccount` during convergence.

Do not create a temporary identity model that becomes permanent because everyone became tired.

## 4.3 Private-content seam

Harborlight must not duplicate Sealed Hold’s encrypted private-package system.

Harborlight Phase 1 may create:

* community asset references;
* storage-provider interfaces;
* staging and quarantine concepts;
* immutable release references;
* checksum fields;
* future public package contracts.

It must not copy Sealed Hold’s `.ftprivate` codec or private import system into Harborlight.

Document the distinction:

```text
Sealed Hold:
private encrypted Chronicle ownership and transport

Harborlight:
community publication, immutable public or unlisted releases,
future installation, remixing, and distribution
```

Shared archive, checksum, storage, or validation utilities may be reconciled later during convergence.

## 4.4 Lanternwake seam

Reuse the existing Lanternwake types and resolved-motion authority.

Do not create a separate Community animation director.

Phase 1 may register future Community scene contracts, but must not falsely mark unfinished scenes as production-ready.

---

# 5. Additive Schema Policy

Because One Voyage, Wayfarer, and Sealed Hold all have separate Prisma work, Harborlight schema changes must be additive and conflict-aware.

Before editing `prisma/schema.prisma`:

1. inspect all current models;
2. inspect active worktrees and visible migration branches;
3. inventory likely overlap;
4. freeze the Harborlight data model in the design record;
5. assign one database owner;
6. document every new model, relation, index, and constraint.

Do not delete, rename, or repurpose existing canonical models during Harborlight Phase 1.

Do not alter legacy tables merely to make community relationships prettier.

Prefer nullable or adapter-backed foreign-key seams where later Wayfarer convergence will provide final account ownership.

Every migration must have a unique, convergence-friendly identifier.

Do not reuse migration sequence identifiers already used by Wayfarer or Sealed Hold.

Known candidate MySQL migration identifiers already include duplicate `0005` entries, so Harborlight must inspect current migration history and choose the next safe provisional identifier rather than contributing another `0005` to the archaeological dig.

Create:

```text
Development_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Integration_Manifest.md
```

Include all schema and migration assumptions that future convergence must reconcile.

---

# 6. High-Conflict Files

Before modifying any of the following, inspect their current contents, history, and concurrent ownership:

```text
prisma/schema.prisma
prisma/migrations/**
src/domain/**
src/services/**
src/lib/auth/**
src/lib/session/**
src/domain/visibility.ts
src/components/studio/**
src/app/api/creator/**
src/app/api/community/**
src/animation/**
package.json
package-lock.json
Development_Docs/project-status.json
README.md
SECURITY.md
.env.example
```

Do not mechanically resolve conceptual conflicts by retaining two implementations.

Do not modify unrelated systems merely because the file is already open.

---

# 7. Runtime and Database Isolation

All mutating Harborlight tests must use:

* an isolated database;
* an isolated storage root;
* an isolated staging root;
* an owned server port;
* synthetic community data.

Before mutation, record:

* branch;
* commit;
* process ID;
* port;
* database identity;
* test nonce;
* storage root;
* staging root;
* canonical database identity;
* canonical database metadata.

Prove the running application uses the isolated resources.

After testing, prove the canonical database and canonical asset roots remain unchanged.

Do not assume a separate worktree or alternate port provides database isolation.

Do not use production credentials.

---

# 8. Commit and Push Are Mandatory

A Git commit and push do not mean:

* Phase acceptance;
* integration approval;
* release readiness;
* merge approval;
* production deployment.

They preserve the implementation candidate remotely.

Do not leave completed or partial Harborlight work only in a local worktree.

Commit durable milestones throughout implementation when appropriate.

At the final Phase 1 checkpoint:

1. review the full diff;
2. run secret and private-content scans;
3. stage only Harborlight files;
4. update the integration manifest;
5. update the validation record;
6. commit the current implementation;
7. push the dedicated Harborlight branch;
8. report the exact remote commit;
9. stop for convergence review.

If all Phase 1 gates pass, label the branch as a completed Phase 1 candidate.

If Harborlight repository-local work is complete but an unrelated repository-wide or external environment gate remains, commit and push it as a blocked acceptance candidate.

Do not respond with only a NO-GO report and leave the branch uncommitted.

Do not confuse honest validation reporting with refusing to preserve work.

---

# 9. Required Integration Manifest

At completion, the Harborlight integration manifest must include:

* branch;
* base commit;
* ending commit;
* latest `origin/main`;
* files added;
* files modified;
* Prisma models added;
* Prisma fields added;
* SQLite migrations;
* MySQL migrations;
* package scripts;
* environment variables;
* Community identity assumptions;
* Chronicle-domain assumptions;
* private-content assumptions;
* authorization assumptions;
* storage abstractions;
* outbox and job abstractions;
* Lanternwake registry changes;
* likely conflicts with One Voyage;
* likely conflicts with Wayfarer;
* likely conflicts with Sealed Hold;
* recommended convergence order;
* required combined tests;
* deferred destructive changes;
* retained compatibility code.

Do not claim that future convergence risks are nonexistent merely because Git reports no textual conflict.

---

# 10. Completion Behavior

Continue until all repository-local Harborlight Phase 1 work from the governing prompt is implemented.

Do not stop merely because:

* dependencies are absent;
* a network-share runtime is awkward;
* a focused test initially fails;
* an unrelated repository test fails;
* a later convergence adjustment will be needed;
* production object storage is not yet deployed;
* Wayfarer is not yet merged;
* Sealed Hold is not yet merged;
* One Voyage is not yet merged.

Instead:

1. complete Harborlight’s own Phase 1 scope;
2. fix Harborlight-caused failures;
3. make narrow safe baseline fixes when required;
4. run focused validation;
5. run the strongest available project-specific validation;
6. classify unrelated failures separately;
7. commit and push the candidate;
8. stop before Phase 2.

Do not merge into `main`.

Do not begin Phase 2.

Do not begin cross-project convergence from the Harborlight branch.

Finish **Chart the Harbor**, preserve it remotely, and stop for the dedicated integration phase.

Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 1 of Project Harborlight**, the Community Harbor, Chronicle Exchange, and shared-creation ecosystem for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale platform.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nChart the Harbor\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunity Domain Foundation, Privacy Boundaries, Immutable Releases, and Trust Architecture\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must create the authoritative technical foundation required for all later Harborlight phases.\r\n\r\nIt must establish:\r\n\r\n1. the canonical community domain;\r\n2. the relationship between Community Harbor and the existing Tall Tale platform;\r\n3. the distinction between private content, public listings, immutable releases, Studio drafts, and live Tale Sessions;\r\n4. the community item taxonomy;\r\n5. the publication lifecycle;\r\n6. the visibility model;\r\n7. the spoiler model;\r\n8. the licensing and attribution model;\r\n9. the immutable release identity model;\r\n10. public projection boundaries;\r\n11. community identity and profile foundations;\r\n12. storage-provider abstractions;\r\n13. durable job and transactional-outbox foundations;\r\n14. authorization and abuse-control policy;\r\n15. Lanternwake community-scene architecture;\r\n16. legacy-domain compatibility strategy;\r\n17. migrations, documentation, tests, and acceptance evidence.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is not primarily a public-facing feature phase.\r\n\r\nIt builds the harbor walls, charts the channels, defines which docks are public, and establishes what may enter or leave the system before later phases invite the entire internet to upload models named `Definitely_Not_Malware.glb`.\r\n\r\nThe central Phase 1 rule is:\r\n\r\n> Community Harbor must never expose mutable private Chronicle data directly. Public community content must be represented through explicit, sanitized, versioned, immutable community releases and projections.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Governing Product Intent\r\n\r\nCommunity Harbor is not a generic social feed.\r\n\r\nIt is intended to become:\r\n\r\n> A curated ecosystem for discovering, sharing, installing, remixing, and celebrating Chronicles and their components.\r\n\r\nLater phases will support:\r\n\r\n* Community Chronicles;\r\n* Chronicle templates;\r\n* Story Block presets;\r\n* 2D artifacts;\r\n* 3D artifacts;\r\n* artifact collections and assemblies;\r\n* map packs;\r\n* location packs;\r\n* audio packs;\r\n* reveal presets;\r\n* invitation styles;\r\n* completion styles;\r\n* guides;\r\n* Creator profiles;\r\n* follows;\r\n* favorites;\r\n* collections;\r\n* ratings;\r\n* reviews;\r\n* comments;\r\n* Voyage Keepsakes;\r\n* Voyage Logs;\r\n* moderation;\r\n* installation;\r\n* forks;\r\n* attribution;\r\n* remix lineage.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must model and protect those future capabilities without prematurely implementing all of them.\r\n\r\nDo not create placeholder user interfaces that falsely imply later features are complete.\r\n\r\nDo not create public uploads, reviews, comments, or unrestricted publishing during Phase 1.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Governing Documentation\r\n\r\nBefore editing source, read all available Harborlight and adjacent governing documents completely.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, inspect:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Community_Harbor_Governing_Document.pdf\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Design_Record.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Product_Specification.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Data_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Privacy_and_Spoiler_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Lanternwake_Integration.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/project-status.json\r\nREADME.md\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nSome of these documents may not exist yet.\r\n\r\nIf they do not exist:\r\n\r\n* do not fabricate that they do;\r\n* create only the Phase 1 documents explicitly required by this prompt;\r\n* record missing governing documents in the final report.\r\n\r\nAlso inspect current documentation for:\r\n\r\n* Project Lanternwake;\r\n* universal language and terminology;\r\n* Project Wayfarer or account/profile architecture;\r\n* canonical Tall Tale architecture;\r\n* Creator Studio;\r\n* invitation and Player identity systems;\r\n* current asset storage;\r\n* current audit logging;\r\n* current authorization;\r\n* deployment and database architecture;\r\n* background jobs;\r\n* rate limiting;\r\n* public Tale catalog;\r\n* legacy Campaign compatibility.\r\n\r\nThe current checked-out repository is authoritative for implementation.\r\n\r\nThe governing documents define intended architecture and product constraints, but every path and source-level assumption must be revalidated against current code.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunityCampaign\r\nCommunityChapter\r\nCommunityPlayer\r\nCommunityTaleSession\r\n```\r\n\r\nunless the current architecture proves such a distinct entity is necessary and identify which remain production-active;\r\n* identify which are compatibility-only;\r\n* identify whether public content currently references them;\r\n* define an adapter or migration strategy;\r\n* Community Profile Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate or extend a Community Profile entity.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual shape:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype CommunityProfile = {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  personId: string;\r\n  handle: string;\r\n  displayName: string;\r\n  biography?: string;\r\n  avatarAssetId?: string;\r\n  bannerAssetId?: string;\r\n  visibility: CommunityProfileVisibility;\r\n email address;\r\n* password state;\r\n* session data;\r\n* invitation data;\r\n* private account identifiers;\r\n* private location;\r\n* legal name unless explicitly chosen as display name;\r\n* blocked-user relationships;\r\n* moderator internal notes.\r\n\r\n## 11.4 Phase 1 user interface\r\n\r\nA minimal internal or authenticated profile-foundation surface may be implemented if needed to validate the model.\r\n\r\nDo not build the final public Creator Profile experience in Phase 1.\r\n\r\nA basic profile editor may support:\r\n\r\n* handle;\r\n* display name;\r\n* biography;\r\n* Community Listing Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate a stable Community Listing identity.\r\n\r\nThe listing represents the long-lived community catalog page and release history.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual fields:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nid\r\nslug\r\nitemType\r\nownerProfileId\r\ntitle\r\nshortDescription\r\nlongDescription\r\nvisibility\r\npublicationStatus\r\nmoderationStatus\r\nspoilerLevel\r\nprimaryCategory\r\ntags\r\ncontentWarnings\r\ncoverAssetId\r\ncurrentReleaseId\r\ncreatedAt\r\nupdatedAt\r\npublishedAt\r\nfeaturedAt\r\narchivedAt\r\nremovedAt\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 12.1 Listing rules\r\n\r\nA listing:\r\n\r\n* may be mutable while draft;\r\n* may change public presentation metadata according to policy;\r\n* may have many immutable releases;\r\n* owns the stable public slug;\r\n* has one current release when published;\r\n* may be private, unlisted, community-visible, or featured;\r\n* may be quarantined or removed without rewriting releases;\r\n* retains audit identity when removed.\r\n\r\n## 12.2 Title and description safety\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* length limits;\r\n* normalization;\r\n* safe rich-text or plain-text policy;\r\n* no raw HTML;\r\n* no scripts;\r\n* no unsafe embedded content;\r\n* safe URL handling;\r\n* content-warning metadata.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may use plain text where the rich-text system is not yet settled.\r\n\r\nDo not add unsafe markdown merely because it is convenient.\r\n\r\n## 12.3 Tags and categories\r\n\r\nImplement a bounded baseline:\r\n\r\n* normalized tags;\r\n* tag-count limit;\r\n* tag-length limit;\r\n* canonical category reference or validated category key;\r\n* no unbounded arbitrary tag creation without limits.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need full search indexing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Immutable Community Release Foundation\r\n\r\nA Community Release represents one published, …24 tokens truncated…ve duplicate;\r\n* invalid Unicode or invisible characters;\r\n* reserved names;\r\n* safe rename;\r\n* owner edit;\r\n* non-owner denial;\r\n* suspended-user denial;\r\n* private field exclusion;\r\n* public projection.\r\n\r\n## 39.3 Listing tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* listing creation;\r\n* owner assignment;\r\n* invalid item type;\r\n* invalid visibility;\r\n* invalid status transition;\r\n* owner update;\r\n* non-owner denial;\r\n* archive;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* removed state;\r\n* unlisted enumeration exclusion;\r\n* private enumeration exclusion;\r\n* slug uniqueness.\r\n\r\n## 39.4 Release tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* release from valid immutable source;\r\n* rejection of mutable source;\r\n* version uniqueness;\r\n* semantic ordering;\r\n* manifest checksum;\r\n* immutable fields;\r\n* current release update;\r\n* transaction rollback;\r\n* outbox insertion;\r\n* audit insertion;\r\n* deprecated release;\r\n* replacement release;\r\n* source Tale version linkage.\r\n\r\n## 39.5 Public projection privacy tests\r\n\r\nCreate hostile fixtures containing:\r\n\r\n* accepted answers;\r\n* hidden variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* private story prose;\r\n* finale text;\r\n* exact coordinates;\r\n* invitation token;\r\n* email address;\r\n* session identifier;\r\n* participant identity;\r\n* unpublished asset path;\r\n* internal moderation note.\r\n\r\nProve public projections exclude every prohibited field.\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* listing summary;\r\n* listing detail;\r\n* release summary;\r\n* release detail;\r\n* Community Profile;\r\n* unlisted lookup;\r\n* private lookup;\r\n* removed listing;\r\n* quarantined release.\r\n\r\n## 39.6 Authorization matrix\r\n\r\nFor every sensitive service or route test:\r\n\r\n| Actor              | Expected                              |\r\n| ------------------ | ------------------------------------- |\r\n| Anonymous visitor  | Public read only                      |\r\n| Authenticated user | Allowed according to policy           |\r\n| Creator owner      | Own content management                |\r\n| Creator non-owner  | No private access                     |\r\n| Verified Creator   | Same plus approved capabilities       |\r\n| Moderator          | Moderation-scoped access              |\r\n| Admin              | Authorized administrative access      |\r\n| Suspended user     | No publishing                         |\r\n| Removed profile    | No active management                  |\r\n| Blocked user       | Policy-enforced denial where relevant |\r\n\r\nTest IDOR explicitly.\r\n\r\n## 39.7 Visibility tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* private excluded from public lists;\r\n* private direct lookup denied;\r\n* unlisted excluded from lists;\r\n* unlisted direct access according to policy;\r\n* community included publicly;\r\n* featured cannot be self-assigned;\r\n* quarantined unavailable;\r\n* removed unavailable;\r\n* owner private access;\r\n* moderator access audit.\r\n\r\n## 39.8 Spoiler tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* preview-safe projection;\r\n* minor spoiler exclusion from default public projection;\r\n* chapter spoiler exclusion;\r\n* finale exclusion;\r\n* Creator-only exclusion;\r\n* Captain-only exclusion;\r\n* participant-private exclusion;\r\n* search projection exclusion;\r\n* Open Graph projection exclusion if implemented.\r\n\r\n## 39.9 License and attribution tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* valid built-in policy;\r\n* custom policy;\r\n* missing license;\r\n* license snapshot;\r\n* attribution ordering;\r\n* missing required attribution;\r\n* asset-level exception;\r\n* old release unchanged after default license edit;\r\n* ownership declaration linkage.\r\n\r\n## 39.10 Storage tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* staged path creation;\r\n* immutable path creation;\r\n* quarantine path creation;\r\n* checksum retrieval;\r\n* path traversal;\r\n* absolute path;\r\n* separator abuse;\r\n* duplicate object key;\r\n* immutable overwrite rejection;\r\n* cross-owner access rejection;\r\n* removed marker;\r\n* missing file behavior.\r\n\r\n## 39.11 Outbox and job tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* outbox inserted in same transaction;\r\n* rollback removes outbox event;\r\n* idempotency key;\r\n* duplicate dispatch protection;\r\n* retryable failure;\r\n* terminal failure;\r\n* sanitized payload;\r\n* processed timestamp only after success;\r\n* worker restart simulation where practical.\r\n\r\n## 39.12 Feature-flag tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* Community disabled;\r\n* Community enabled;\r\n* public publishing disabled;\r\n* server rejects public publishing while disabled;\r\n* moderation required;\r\n* missing storage configuration;\r\n* development-safe defaults.\r\n\r\n## 39.13 Migration tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* clean database migration;\r\n* existing database migration;\r\n* no existing data loss;\r\n* Prisma validation;\r\n* supported database targets;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* rollback/restore procedure documentation.\r\n\r\n## 39.14 Lanternwake foundation tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* community scene family registry exists;\r\n* future scenes are not marked production;\r\n* contracts use existing Lanternwake types;\r\n* reduced-motion policy is inherited;\r\n* no duplicate animation framework;\r\n* no local substantial GSAP scene bypasses Lanternwake.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 40. Security Requirements\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must explicitly test or review:\r\n\r\n* IDOR;\r\n* mass assignment;\r\n* invalid status transitions;\r\n* private listing enumeration;\r\n* unlisted enumeration;\r\n* unsafe slug handling;\r\n* stored XSS in profile/listing text;\r\n* unsafe URL schemes in social links;\r\n* open redirect risk;\r\n* public projection leakage;\r\n* raw ORM record leakage;\r\n* SQL injection through filters;\r\n* path traversal in storage;\r\n* feature-flag bypass;\r\n* moderator authorization;\r\n* suspension bypass;\r\n* checksum spoofing;\r\n* source Tale substitution;\r\n* race conditions creating duplicate versions;\r\n* outbox payload leakage.\r\n\r\nUse strict input schemas.\r\n\r\nDo not accept arbitrary object payloads and spread them into database writes.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 41. Accessibility Requirements\r\n\r\nPhase 1 user-facing surfaces are limited, but any created interface must include:\r\n\r\n* semantic labels;\r\n* keyboard operation;\r\n* visible focus;\r\n* clear errors;\r\n* error summary where forms are complex;\r\n* no color-only status;\r\n* reduced-motion compliance;\r\n* accessible loading state;\r\n* live-region confirmation where appropriate;\r\n* 200% zoom usability;\r\n* mobile usability.\r\n\r\nProfile and listing forms must identify:\r\n\r\n* required fields;\r\n* validation errors;\r\n* privacy implications;\r\n* visibility state;\r\n* spoiler state;\r\n* license selection.\r\n\r\nDo not build inaccessible temporary interfaces with the excuse that later phases will replace them.\r\n\r\nTemporary interfaces have a remarkable tendency to become permanent after someone says, â€œWe can polish that later.â€\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 42. Documentation Requirements\r\n\r\nCreate or update:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Design_Record.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Data_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Privacy_and_Spoiler_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Licensing_and_Attribution.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Release_Manifest.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Storage_and_Job_Architecture.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Test_Plan.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nUpdate as applicable:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/project-status.json\r\nDevelopment_Docs/architecture.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/route-inventory.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/environment-variables.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/security.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Lanternwake scene registry documentation\r\nREADME.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nDocumentation must state:\r\n\r\n* what Phase 1 implemented;\r\n* what remains feature-flagged;\r\n* what remains internal;\r\n* what Phase 2 will add;\r\n* current legacy compatibility;\r\n* current storage limitations;\r\n* current worker limitations;\r\n* current rate-limit limitations;\r\n* current database support;\r\n* current security limitations;\r\n* current public publishing status.\r\n\r\nDo not mark the full Community Harbor complete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 43. Validation Commands\r\n\r\nUse repository-supported commands.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, run applicable focused and final commands such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run assets:validate\r\nnpm run build\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso run, where supported:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpx prisma validate\r\nnpx prisma generate\r\nmigration verification\r\nfocused Harborlight tests\r\nauthorization tests\r\nprivacy tests\r\nstorage tests\r\noutbox tests\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun the full repository validation exactly once under one validation owner after focused suites pass.\r\n\r\nDo not run multiple full validation suites concurrently.\r\n\r\nFor every validation command, record:\r\n\r\n* command;\r\n* exit code;\r\n* passed count;\r\n* failed count;\r\n* skipped count;\r\n* duration;\r\n* environment;\r\n* database identity;\r\n* server identity;\r\n* classification of any failure.\r\n\r\nA skipped test is not a pass.\r\n\r\nA retry that later passes must not erase the original failure from the report.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 44. Exact Acceptance Criteria\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is complete only when every applicable criterion below is satisfied.\r\n\r\n## 44.1 Architecture\r\n\r\n* Community Harbor uses the canonical Tall Tale domain.\r\n* No third story architecture exists.\r\n* Community Profile references canonical identity.\r\n* Public catalog, immutable release, Studio draft, and Tale Session remain distinct.\r\n* Legacy compatibility is explicit.\r\n* The Phase 1 design record matches implementation.\r\n\r\n## 44.2 Community taxonomy\r\n\r\n* All planned community item types are represented in a typed taxonomy.\r\n* Every item type declares its release/install/remix capability.\r\n* Type-specific metadata has schemas or explicit extension points.\r\n* There is no unbounded generic JSON dumping ground.\r\n\r\n## 44.3 Profiles\r\n\r\n* Community Profile exists.\r\n* Handle uniqueness is enforced.\r\n* Handle normalization is tested.\r\n* Public projection excludes private account fields.\r\n* Suspended profiles cannot publish.\r\n* Profile edits are authorized.\r\n\r\n## 44.4 Listings\r\n\r\n* Community Listing exists.\r\n* Stable slugs exist.\r\n* Ownership is explicit.\r\n* Publication state is separate from visibility.\r\n* Visibility is centrally authorized.\r\n* Private and unlisted listings are not publicly enumerable.\r\n* Removed and quarantined states block public delivery.\r\n\r\n## 44.5 Releases\r\n\r\n* Community Release exists.\r\n* Releases are immutable after publication.\r\n* Semantic versions are unique within a listing.\r\n* Schema version is explicit.\r\n* Manifest checksum is deterministic.\r\n* Community Chronicles reference immutable published Tale versions.\r\n* Updating requires a new release.\r\n* Old releases remain identifiable.\r\n* Deprecation does not mutate release content.\r\n\r\n## 44.6 Privacy\r\n\r\n* Public projections are explicit.\r\n* Raw ORM records are not returned publicly.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes answers.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes hidden variables.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes Captain notes.\r\n* Private coordinates are excluded.\r\n* Invitation and session data are excluded.\r\n* Unpublished content is excluded.\r\n* Internal moderation data is excluded.\r\n* Hostile privacy fixtures pass.\r\n\r\n## 44.7 Spoilers\r\n\r\n* Spoiler levels are typed.\r\n* Server projections enforce spoiler policy.\r\n* Finale content is excluded by default.\r\n* Creator-only content is excluded.\r\n* Captain-only content is excluded.\r\n* Participant-private content is excluded.\r\n* Search/Open Graph seams are documented.\r\n\r\n## 44.8 Licensing and attribution\r\n\r\n* License policies exist.\r\n* Release license snapshots exist.\r\n* Attribution records exist.\r\n* Required attribution can be represented.\r\n* Ownership declarations are auditable.\r\n* Future remix compatibility is possible.\r\n* Old releases do not change when Creator defaults change.\r\n\r\n## 44.9 Assets and storage\r\n\r\n* Community asset references exist.\r\n* Checksums are represented.\r\n* Scan status is truthful.\r\n* Processing status is explicit.\r\n* Storage provider interface exists.\r\n* Local development adapter exists.\r\n* Staging, immutable, and quarantine namespaces exist.\r\n* Path traversal tests pass.\r\n* Immutable overwrite is blocked.\r\n\r\n## 44.10 Jobs and outbox\r\n\r\n* Typed job foundation exists.\r\n* Transactional outbox exists or an equivalent durable architecture is implemented.\r\n* Outbox insertion occurs with source mutation.\r\n* Idempotency exists.\r\n* Retry states exist.\r\n* Payloads are sanitized.\r\n* The current development/production limitation is documented truthfully.\r\n\r\n## 44.11 Authorization and abuse controls\r\n\r\n* Server-side authorization is centralized.\r\n* Owner/non-owner behavior is tested.\r\n* Moderator permissions are tested.\r\n* Suspended-user behavior is tested.\r\n* IDOR tests pass safely.\r\n* Centralized rate-limit categories exist.\r\n* Feature flags are enforced server-side.\r\n\r\n## 44.12 Lanternwake\r\n\r\n* Community scene family is defined.\r\n* Future Community scenes are registered or documented without false production status.\r\n* Existing Lanternwake contracts are reused.\r\n* Existing reduced-motion authority is reused.\r\n* No second animation framework is introduced.\r\n* No substantial local animation bypass is added.\r\n\r\n## 44.13 Migrations\r\n\r\n* Schema validates.\r\n* Migrations run on isolated databases.\r\n* Existing data is preserved.\r\n* Indexes and unique constraints exist.\r\n* MySQL compatibility is verified where supported.\r\n* Canonical development data remains unchanged.\r\n\r\n## 44.14 Documentation\r\n\r\n* Required documents exist.\r\n* Project status is updated.\r\n* Environment variables are documented.\r\n* Phase 2 handoff is documented.\r\n* Limitations are explicit.\r\n* Full Harborlight is not falsely marked complete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 45. Required Final Report\r\n\r\nAt completion, provide a detailed final report.\r\n\r\n## Repository status\r\n\r\nReport:\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* worktree;\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit;\r\n* upstream;\r\n* ahead/behind state;\r\n* preserved concurrent changes;\r\n* files changed;\r\n* migrations added;\r\n* commits created;\r\n* push result.\r\n\r\n## architecture;\r\n* job/outbox architecture;\r\n* Lanternwake foundation.\r\n\r\n## Implementation totals\r\n\r\nReport:\r\n\r\n* models added;\r\n* models extended;\r\n* enums/types added;\r\n* services added;\r\n* routes/actions added;\r\n* migrations added;\r\n* constraints added;\r\n* indexes added;\r\n* public projections added;\r\n* item types registered;\r\n* license policies registered;\r\n* audit actions added;\r\n* tests added;\r\n* documentation files added.\r\n\r\n## Security and privacy proof\r\n\r\nProvide evidence that public projections exclude:\r\n\r\n* identities;\r\n* internal moderation information.\r\n\r\nProvide IDOR and private-enumeration results.\r\n\r\n## Release proof\r\n\r\nProvide evidence for:\r\n\r\n* listing creation;\r\n* immutable Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 1 of Project Harborlight**, the Community Harbor, Chronicle Exchange, and shared-creation ecosystem for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale platform.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nChart the Harbor\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunity Domain Foundation, Privacy Boundaries, Immutable Releases, and Trust Architecture\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must create the authoritative technical foundation required for all later Harborlight phases.\r\n\r\nIt must establish:\r\n\r\n1. the canonical community domain;\r\n2. the relationship between Community Harbor and the existing Tall Tale platform;\r\n3. the distinction between private content, public listings, immutable releases, Studio drafts, and live Tale Sessions;\r\n4. the community item taxonomy;\r\n5. the publication lifecycle;\r\n6. the visibility model;\r\n7. the spoiler model;\r\n8. the licensing and attribution model;\r\n9. the immutable release identity model;\r\n10. public projection boundaries;\r\n11. community identity and profile foundations;\r\n12. storage-provider abstractions;\r\n13. durable job and transactional-outbox foundations;\r\n14. authorization and abuse-control policy;\r\n15. Lanternwake community-scene architecture;\r\n16. legacy-domain compatibility strategy;\r\n17. migrations, documentation, tests, and acceptance evidence.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is not primarily a public-facing feature phase.\r\n\r\nIt builds the harbor walls, charts the channels, defines which docks are public, and establishes what may enter or leave the system before later phases invite the entire internet to upload models named `Definitely_Not_Malware.glb`.\r\n\r\nThe central Phase 1 rule is:\r\n\r\n> Community Harbor must never expose mutable private Chronicle data directly. Public community content must be represented through explicit, sanitized, versioned, immutable community releases and projections.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Governing Product Intent\r\n\r\nCommunity Harbor is not a generic social feed.\r\n\r\nIt is intended to become:\r\n\r\n> A curated ecosystem for discovering, sharing, installing, remixing, and celebrating Chronicles and their components.\r\n\r\nLater phases will support:\r\n\r\n* Community Chronicles;\r\n* Chronicle templates;\r\n* Story Block presets;\r\n* 2D artifacts;\r\n* 3D artifacts;\r\n* artifact collections and assemblies;\r\n* map packs;\r\n* location packs;\r\n* audio packs;\r\n* reveal presets;\r\n* invitation styles;\r\n* completion styles;\r\n* guides;\r\n* Creator profiles;\r\n* follows;\r\n* favorites;\r\n* collections;\r\n* ratings;\r\n* reviews;\r\n* comments;\r\n* Voyage Keepsakes;\r\n* Voyage Logs;\r\n* moderation;\r\n* installation;\r\n* forks;\r\n* attribution;\r\n* remix lineage.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must model and protect those future capabilities without prematurely implementing all of them.\r\n\r\nDo not create placeholder user interfaces that falsely imply later features are complete.\r\n\r\nDo not create public uploads, reviews, comments, or unrestricted publishing during Phase 1.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Governing Documentation\r\n\r\nBefore editing source, read all available Harborlight and adjacent governing documents completely.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, inspect:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Community_Harbor_Governing_Document.pdf\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Design_Record.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Product_Specification.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Data_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Privacy_and_Spoiler_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Community_Harbor_Lanternwake_Integration.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/project-status.json\r\nREADME.md\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nSome of these documents may not exist yet.\r\n\r\nIf they do not exist:\r\n\r\n* do not fabricate that they do;\r\n* create only the Phase 1 documents explicitly required by this prompt;\r\n* record missing governing documents in the final report.\r\n\r\nAlso inspect current documentation for:\r\n\r\n* Project Lanternwake;\r\n* universal language and terminology;\r\n* Project Wayfarer or account/profile architecture;\r\n* canonical Tall Tale architecture;\r\n* Creator Studio;\r\n* invitation and Player identity systems;\r\n* current asset storage;\r\n* current audit logging;\r\n* current authorization;\r\n* deployment and database architecture;\r\n* background jobs;\r\n* rate limiting;\r\n* public Tale catalog;\r\n* legacy Campaign compatibility.\r\n\r\nThe current checked-out repository is authoritative for implementation.\r\n\r\nThe governing documents define intended architecture and product constraints, but every path and source-level assumption must be revalidated against current code.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Repository Safety and Concurrent Work Protection\r\n\r\nBefore editing anything:\r\n\r\n1. Confirm the repository root.\r\n2. Record:\r\n\r\n   * repository name;\r\n   * current branch;\r\n   * current commit;\r\n   * upstream;\r\n   * ahead/behind state;\r\n   * staged changes;\r\n   * unstaged changes;\r\n   * untracked files;\r\n   * active worktrees.\r\n3. Identify the latest accepted integrated baseline.\r\n4. Determine whether:\r\n\r\n   * Lanternwake Phase 5 is merged;\r\n   * universal-language work is merged;\r\n   * Project Wayfarer work exists;\r\n   * other Harborlight work already exists;\r\n   * domain-consolidation work is active;\r\n   * any current branch modifies Prisma or shared architecture.\r\n5. Search for existing community-related code before creating anything.\r\n6. Preserve all unrelated and concurrent work.\r\n7. Do not reset or clean the repository.\r\n8. Do not overwrite newer files with older baseline copies.\r\n9. Do not broadly stage unrelated files.\r\n10. Do not modify the canonical development database during unsafe tests.\r\n\r\nNever use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngit reset --hard\r\ngit clean\r\ngit checkout -- .\r\ngit restore .\r\ngit restore --staged .\r\ngit stash --all\r\nforce push\r\nbroad git add .\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the canonical checkout contains unrelated changes or another task is active:\r\n\r\n* create a dedicated worktree;\r\n* create a dedicated Phase 1 feature branch;\r\n* base it on the latest accepted integrated commit;\r\n* document the exact base;\r\n* preserve the canonical checkout untouched.\r\n\r\nRecommended branch:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfeature/harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor\r\n```\r\n\r\nRecommended worktree name:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nharborlight-phase1\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not merge unrelated unfinished branches merely to simplify this task.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Required Repository Survey\r\n\r\nBefore implementation, perform a focused architecture survey.\r\n\r\nSearch for and inspect:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTallTale\r\nTaleDraft\r\nPublishedTaleVersion\r\nTaleSession\r\nPlayerProfile\r\nAccount\r\nUser\r\nCreator\r\nCaptain\r\nCampaign\r\nChapter\r\nArtifact\r\nTaleArtifact\r\nStoryBlock\r\nAsset\r\nMediaAsset\r\nInvitation\r\nAuditEvent\r\nProgressEvent\r\nViewedCeremony\r\nLibrary\r\nCollection\r\nFavorite\r\nFollow\r\nReview\r\nComment\r\nReport\r\nModeration\r\nPublication\r\nVisibility\r\nLicense\r\nAttribution\r\nPackage\r\nManifest\r\nChecksum\r\nStorage\r\nObjectStorage\r\nUpload\r\nBackgroundJob\r\nOutbox\r\nRateLimit\r\nAnimationDirector\r\nSceneHost\r\nLanternwake\r\npublic projection\r\nsanitize\r\nvisibility\r\n```\r\n\r\nProduce a written repository inventory in the Phase 1 design record covering:\r\n\r\n* canonical story domain;\r\n* legacy story domain;\r\n* canonical identity domain;\r\n* current public catalog;\r\n* current Studio publication model;\r\n* current asset model;\r\n* current audit model;\r\n* current authorization model;\r\n* current rate-limit model;\r\n* current storage model;\r\n* current background-worker model;\r\n* current Lanternwake model;\r\n* current public/private projection utilities;\r\n* existing reusable abstractions;\r\n* architecture conflicts;\r\n* likely migration risks.\r\n\r\nDo not begin broad implementation until this survey is complete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. Phase 1 Scope\r\n\r\nPhase 1 contains the following mandatory workstreams:\r\n\r\n1. Harborlight architecture survey and design record\r\n2. Canonical domain selection and legacy compatibility strategy\r\n3. Community item taxonomy\r\n4. Community profile foundation\r\n5. Community listing foundation\r\n6. Immutable community release foundation\r\n7. Publication-state model\r\n8. Visibility-state model\r\n9. Spoiler classification model\r\n10. Licensing and attribution foundation\r\n11. Public projection boundary\r\n12. Content ownership and authorization foundation\r\n13. Community asset-reference foundation\r\n14. Storage-provider abstraction\r\n15. Background-job and transactional-outbox foundation\r\n16. Base abuse controls and centralized rate-limit policy\r\n17. Lanternwake community-scene foundation\r\n18. Database migrations\r\n19. Phase 1 API/service foundation\r\n20. Focused security, privacy, authorization, and migration tests\r\n21. Documentation and final reconciliation\r\n\r\nAll mandatory workstreams must be completed before Phase 1 is accepted.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Explicit Non-Goals\r\n\r\nDo not implement the following during Phase 1:\r\n\r\n* unrestricted public community publishing;\r\n* public file uploads;\r\n* public comments;\r\n* direct messages;\r\n* public ratings or reviews;\r\n* helpful voting;\r\n* Creator follows;\r\n* public collections;\r\n* public Community Harbor search;\r\n* public Community Harbor landing page;\r\n* full Chronicle listing pages;\r\n* full Creator profile pages;\r\n* 3D artifact rendering;\r\n* 3D model processing;\r\n* artifact assembly UI;\r\n* package import;\r\n* package export;\r\n* installation into Studio;\r\n* editable community copies;\r\n* forking;\r\n* remix lineage user interfaces;\r\n* license compatibility across installed packages;\r\n* update detection;\r\n* public Voyage Logs;\r\n* Voyage Keepsake generation;\r\n* participant-consent workflows;\r\n* moderation dashboard;\r\n* public reports;\r\n* malware-scanner integration;\r\n* production transcoding;\r\n* production search cluster;\r\n* recommendations;\r\n* marketplace payments;\r\n* subscriptions;\r\n* creator payouts;\r\n* final Community Harbor animation implementation;\r\n* full object-storage deployment;\r\n* full worker deployment;\r\n* broad redesign of existing public navigation;\r\n* unrelated authentication redesign;\r\n* unrelated Tall Tale migration;\r\n* deletion of legacy domains without an approved migration.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may create types, models, policies, provider seams, internal development views, or service contracts required by later phases.\r\n\r\nIt must not present future features as complete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Phase 1 Design Record\r\n\r\nBefore broad source changes, create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Design_Record.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThis document must define and freeze:\r\n\r\n1. canonical Community Harbor domain boundaries;\r\n2. canonical Tall Tale integration;\r\n3. legacy Campaign compatibility strategy;\r\n4. relationship to Project Wayfarer identity;\r\n5. community item taxonomy;\r\n6. listing versus release distinction;\r\n7. immutable-release policy;\r\n8. publication states;\r\n9. visibility states;\r\n10. spoiler classifications;\r\n11. public projection rules;\r\n12. private projection rules;\r\n13. licensing model;\r\n14. attribution model;\r\n15. ownership declarations;\r\n16. asset-reference model;\r\n17. storage-provider interface;\r\n18. job-provider interface;\r\n19. transactional-outbox behavior;\r\n20. authorization roles and permissions;\r\n21. rate-limit categories;\r\n22. audit-event categories;\r\n23. retention and deletion behavior;\r\n24. schema-version policy;\r\n25. compatibility-version policy;\r\n26. Lanternwake community scene-family policy;\r\n27. logging and telemetry privacy;\r\n28. migration plan;\r\n29. rollback and restore expectations;\r\n30. Phase 2 handoff requirements.\r\n\r\nDo not allow parallel implementation lanes to invent these concepts independently.\r\n\r\nThe design record must explicitly answer:\r\n\r\n* What is a Community Listing?\r\n* What is a Community Release?\r\n* What remains mutable?\r\n* What becomes immutable?\r\n* What links a Community Chronicle to `PublishedTaleVersion`?\r\n* What links community identity to the canonical account/person?\r\n* What fields are public?\r\n* What fields are private?\r\n* What fields are never copied into community releases?\r\n* What makes a release installable later?\r\n* How is attribution preserved?\r\n* How are spoilers classified?\r\n* How are private real-world locations prevented from becoming public?\r\n* How is a listing quarantined without mutating a release?\r\n* How can a release be deprecated without changing it?\r\n* How will later public uploads enter staging and quarantine?\r\n* How will jobs be emitted safely?\r\n* What Phase 1 components are temporary?\r\n* What must Phase 2 replace or extend?\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Canonical Domain Selection\r\n\r\n## 9.1 Use the canonical Tall Tale platform\r\n\r\nCommunity Chronicles must integrate with the current canonical equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTallTale\r\nTaleDraft\r\nPublishedTaleVersion\r\nTaleSession\r\nStoryBlock\r\nTaleArtifact\r\nPlayerProfile or canonical Person/Account\r\nCreator Studio\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunityCampaign\r\nCommunityChapter\r\nCommunityPlayer\r\nCommunityTaleSession\r\n```\r\n\r\nunless the current architecture proves such a distinct entity is necessary and the design record explains why.\r\n\r\nIn particular:\r\n\r\n* a Community Chronicle listing is not another story definition;\r\n* a Community Release is not another mutable Tale draft;\r\n* a Community Chronicle release should reference or contain a sanitized immutable representation of an existing `PublishedTaleVersion`;\r\n* a live Tale Session remains separate from community catalog data.\r\n\r\n## 9.2 Legacy Campaign compatibility\r\n\r\nThe repository may contain legacy `Campaign`, `Chapter`, `Artifact`, `ProgressEvent`, or Companion models.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must:\r\n\r\n* identify which remain production-active;\r\n* identify which are compatibility-only;\r\n* identify whether public content currently references them;\r\n* define an adapter or migration strategy;\r\n* prevent new Harborlight tables from binding directly to mutable legacy Campaign content;\r\n* avoid deleting or migrating legacy data during this phase unless separately approved.\r\n\r\nIf a compatibility adapter is needed, make it explicit and isolated.\r\n\r\nDo not scatter legacy checks throughout community services.\r\n\r\n## 9.3 Identity integration\r\n\r\nIf Project Wayfarer or a canonical account/person model exists:\r\n\r\n* Community Profile must extend or reference it;\r\n* do not create another login identity;\r\n* do not duplicate credentials;\r\n* do not duplicate private email or authentication state;\r\n* do not create separate Creator and Player accounts.\r\n\r\nIf `PlayerProfile` remains the canonical identity during Phase 1:\r\n\r\n* reference it explicitly;\r\n* document the future Wayfarer migration seam;\r\n* avoid coupling Community Profile to Player-only assumptions.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Community Item Taxonomy\r\n\r\nCreate a typed community item taxonomy.\r\n\r\nRecommended baseline:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\nexport const CommunityItemType = {\r\n  CHRONICLE: \"CHRONICLE\",\r\n  CHRONICLE_TEMPLATE: \"CHRONICLE_TEMPLATE\",\r\n  STORY_BLOCK_PRESET: \"STORY_BLOCK_PRESET\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_2D: \"ARTIFACT_2D\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_3D: \"ARTIFACT_3D\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_COLLECTION: \"ARTIFACT_COLLECTION\",\r\n  MAP_PACK: \"MAP_PACK\",\r\n  LOCATION_PACK: \"LOCATION_PACK\",\r\n  AUDIO_PACK: \"AUDIO_PACK\",\r\n  REVEAL_PRESET: \"REVEAL_PRESET\",\r\n  INVITATION_STYLE: \"INVITATION_STYLE\",\r\n  COMPLETION_STYLE: \"COMPLETION_STYLE\",\r\n  GUIDE: \"GUIDE\",\r\n  VOYAGE_LOG: \"VOYAGE_LOG\",\r\n} as const;\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact implementation may be an enum, union, registry, or database enum depending on repository conventions.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* item types must be explicit;\r\n* types must be exhaustively handled;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Community Profile Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate or extend a Community Profile entity.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual shape:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype CommunityProfile = {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  personId: string;\r\n  handle: string;\r\n  displayName: string;\r\n  biography?: string;\r\n  avatarAssetId?: string;\r\n  bannerAssetId?: string;\r\n  visibility: CommunityProfileVisibility;\r\n requirements\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* stable profile ID;\r\n* canonical identity foreign key;\r\n* unique normalized handle;\r\n* display handle;\r\n* display name;\r\n* biography;\r\n* optional avatar reference;\r\n* optional banner reference;\r\n* supported language metadata;\r\n* optional safe social links;\r\n* visibility;\r\n* Creator status;\r\n* verification status;\r\n* moderation status;\r\n* timestamps.\r\n\r\n## 11.2 Handle rules\r\n\r\nHandle validation must include:\r\n\r\n* case-insensitive uniqueness;\r\n* normalized storage;\r\n* length limits;\r\n* allowed characters;\r\n* reserved names;\r\n* prohibited impersonation-sensitive values;\r\n* protection against invisible Unicode abuse;\r\n* safe rename behavior;\r\n* deterministic slug or URL behavior.\r\n\r\nDo not let handles collide through capitalization or Unicode lookalikes where practical.\r\n\r\n## 11.3 Privacy\r\n\r\nPublic Community Profile projections must not expose:\r\n\r\n* email address;\r\n* password state;\r\n* session data;\r\n* invitation data;\r\n* private account identifiers;\r\n* private location;\r\n* legal name unless explicitly chosen as display name;\r\n* blocked-user relationships;\r\n* moderator internal notes.\r\n\r\n## 11.4 Phase 1 user interface\r\n\r\nA minimal internal or authenticated profile-foundation surface may be implemented if needed to validate the model.\r\n\r\nDo not build the final public Creator Profile experience in Phase 1.\r\n\r\nA basic profile editor may support:\r\n\r\n* handle;\r\n* display name;\r\n* biography;\r\n* visibility;\r\n* supported languages.\r\n\r\nDo not add placeholder follower counts, ratings, badges, or install totals as fake data.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Community Listing Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate a stable Community Listing identity.\r\n\r\nThe listing represents the long-lived community catalog page and release history.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual fields:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nid\r\nslug\r\nitemType\r\nownerProfileId\r\ntitle\r\nshortDescription\r\nlongDescription\r\nvisibility\r\npublicationStatus\r\nmoderationStatus\r\nspoilerLevel\r\nprimaryCategory\r\ntags\r\ncontentWarnings\r\ncoverAssetId\r\ncurrentReleaseId\r\ncreatedAt\r\nupdatedAt\r\npublishedAt\r\nfeaturedAt\r\narchivedAt\r\nremovedAt\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 12.1 Listing rules\r\n\r\nA listing:\r\n\r\n* may be mutable while draft;\r\n* may change public presentation metadata according to policy;\r\n* may have many immutable releases;\r\n* owns the stable public slug;\r\n* has one current release when published;\r\n* may be private, unlisted, community-visible, or featured;\r\n* may be quarantined or removed without rewriting releases;\r\n* retains audit identity when removed.\r\n\r\n## 12.2 Title and description safety\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* length limits;\r\n* normalization;\r\n* safe rich-text or plain-text policy;\r\n* no raw HTML;\r\n* no scripts;\r\n* no unsafe embedded content;\r\n* safe URL handling;\r\n* content-warning metadata.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may use plain text where the rich-text system is not yet settled.\r\n\r\nDo not add unsafe markdown merely because it is convenient.\r\n\r\n## 12.3 Tags and categories\r\n\r\nImplement a bounded baseline:\r\n\r\n* normalized tags;\r\n* tag-count limit;\r\n* tag-length limit;\r\n* canonical category reference or validated category key;\r\n* no unbounded arbitrary tag creation without limits.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need full search indexing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Immutable Community Release Foundation\r\n\r\nA Community Release represents one published, immutable version of a listing.\r\n\r\nRecommended conceptual shape:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nid\r\nlistingId\r\nsemanticVersion\r\nschemaVersion\r\nminimumPlatformVersion\r\nsourcePublishedTaleVersionId\r\nsourceDraftId nullable\r\nmanifest\r\nmanifestChecksum\r\npackageChecksum nullable until package support\r\nreleaseNotes\r\ncompatibility\r\npublishedByProfileId\r\nmoderationSnapshot\r\ncreatedAt\r\npublishedAt\r\ndeprecatedAt\r\nreplacementReleaseId\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 13.1 Immutability rules\r\n\r\nAfter publication:\r\n\r\n* release content must not mutate;\r\n* release manifest must not mutate;\r\n* referenced immutable assets must not mutate;\r\n* checksums must not mutate;\r\n* source identity must not mutate;\r\n* licensing snapshot must not mutate;\r\n* attribution snapshot must not mutate;\r\n* spoiler classification snapshot must not mutate.\r\n\r\nCorrections require a new release.\r\n\r\nAdministrative moderation may:\r\n\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* hide;\r\n* remove public delivery;\r\n* deprecate;\r\n* designate a replacement.\r\n\r\nModeration must not rewrite the release payload.\r\n\r\n## 13.2 Semantic versions\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must define:\r\n\r\n* semantic version format;\r\n* uniqueness within a listing;\r\n* ordering;\r\n* prerelease policy if allowed;\r\n* whether initial releases must begin at `1.0.0`;\r\n* minimum platform version format;\r\n* package schema version format.\r\n\r\nDo not assume application release versions and content release versions are the same concept.\r\n\r\n## 13.3 Schema version\r\n\r\nEvery release must declare a community manifest schema version.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncommunityManifestSchemaVersion\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not rely on the current application version alone.\r\n\r\n## 13.4 Source Chronicle identity\r\n\r\nA Community Chronicle release must reference:\r\n\r\n* the source immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`;\r\n* source Tale identity;\r\n* source version/checksum where available;\r\n* sanitized public metadata;\r\n* compatibility metadata.\r\n\r\nDo not reference a mutable Tale draft as the authoritative published content.\r\n\r\nA draft may be recorded as provenance only if necessary.\r\n\r\n## 13.5 Release creation service\r\n\r\nCreate a service boundary for future publication.\r\n\r\nRecommended responsibilities:\r\n\r\n* validate listing ownership;\r\n* validate publication transition;\r\n* validate version uniqueness;\r\n* load immutable source;\r\n* create sanitized release manifest;\r\n* snapshot license and attribution;\r\n* calculate manifest checksum;\r\n* create release;\r\n* update listing current release transactionally;\r\n* emit outbox event;\r\n* write audit record.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may keep publication restricted to internal/development use or feature-flagged.\r\n\r\nDo not enable unrestricted public publishing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Publication Lifecycle\r\n\r\nCreate explicit publication states.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype CommunityPublicationStatus =\r\n  | \"DRAFT\"\r\n  | \"VALIDATING\"\r\n  | \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\r\n  | \"IN_REVIEW\"\r\n  | \"PUBLISHED\"\r\n  | \"UPDATE_PENDING\"\r\n  | \"QUARANTINED\"\r\n  | \"REJECTED\"\r\n  | \"ARCHIVED\"\r\n  | \"REMOVED\";\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the repository favors separate listing and release states, use the cleaner architecture.\r\n\r\n## 14.1 Transition rules\r\n\r\nCreate an explicit transition matrix.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, define:\r\n\r\n* DRAFT -> VALIDATING;\r\n* VALIDATING -> DRAFT on validation failure;\r\n* VALIDATING -> READY_FOR_REVIEW;\r\n* READY_FOR_REVIEW -> IN_REVIEW;\r\n* IN_REVIEW -> PUBLISHED;\r\n* IN_REVIEW -> REJECTED;\r\n* PUBLISHED -> UPDATE_PENDING;\r\n* PUBLISHED -> QUARANTINED;\r\n* PUBLISHED -> ARCHIVED;\r\n* QUARANTINED -> PUBLISHED after approved restoration;\r\n* any publicly available state -> REMOVED through authorized moderation;\r\n* REMOVED remains terminal unless a separately authorized restoration policy exists.\r\n\r\nDo not allow arbitrary direct status updates from routes.\r\n\r\nUse a domain service.\r\n\r\n## 14.2 Phase 1 restrictions\r\n\r\nDuring Phase 1:\r\n\r\n* public publishing must remain feature-flagged or restricted;\r\n* moderation-required flows may remain internal;\r\n* status services and tests must still exist;\r\n* no route may bypass transition policy.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Visibility Model\r\n\r\nPublication state and visibility must remain separate.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype CommunityVisibility =\r\n  | \"PRIVATE\"\r\n  | \"CREW_ONLY\"\r\n  | \"UNLISTED\"\r\n  | \"COMMUNITY\"\r\n  | \"FEATURED\";\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 15.1 Visibility semantics\r\n\r\n### PRIVATE\r\n\r\nVisible only to:\r\n\r\n* owner;\r\n* authorized collaborators;\r\n* moderators with appropriate reason.\r\n\r\n### CREW_ONLY\r\n\r\nVisible only to:\r\n\r\n* authorized participants;\r\n* relevant Tale Session members;\r\n* owner;\r\n* moderators with appropriate reason.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may define this behavior without implementing Voyage Logs yet.\r\n\r\n### UNLISTED\r\n\r\nAccessible only through an authorized direct identifier or link.\r\n\r\nMust not appear in:\r\n\r\n* public search;\r\n* public category listings;\r\n* trending;\r\n* featured;\r\n* recommendations;\r\n* public creator publication lists unless policy explicitly allows it.\r\n\r\n### COMMUNITY\r\n\r\nEligible for public catalog display.\r\n\r\n### FEATURED\r\n\r\nCommunity-visible and editorially selected.\r\n\r\nFeatured must not be a user-controlled visibility escalation.\r\n\r\n## 15.2 Visibility authorization service\r\n\r\nCreate one centralized service or policy layer for:\r\n\r\n* can view listing;\r\n* can view release;\r\n* can edit listing;\r\n* can publish;\r\n* can moderate;\r\n* can retrieve private metadata;\r\n* can retrieve public projection.\r\n\r\nDo not scatter visibility conditions across pages and routes.\r\n\r\n## 15.3 Enumeration protection\r\n\r\nPrivate and unlisted records must not be enumerable through:\r\n\r\n* list endpoints;\r\n* sequential IDs;\r\n* slug guessing;\r\n* public creator pages;\r\n* search;\r\n* aggregate endpoints;\r\n* Open Graph metadata;\r\n* error distinctions.\r\n\r\nReturn safe not-found behavior where appropriate.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. Spoiler Model\r\n\r\nCreate typed spoiler classifications.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype SpoilerLevel =\r\n  | \"NONE\"\r\n  | \"PREVIEW_SAFE\"\r\n  | \"MINOR\"\r\n  | \"CHAPTER\"\r\n  | \"FINALE\"\r\n  | \"CREATOR_ONLY\"\r\n  | \"CAPTAIN_ONLY\"\r\n  | \"PARTICIPANT_PRIVATE\";\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 16.1 Spoiler principles\r\n\r\nSpoiler protection must exist in data projection, not merely in presentation.\r\n\r\nDo not send hidden spoiler content to unauthorized clients.\r\n\r\nDo not rely on:\r\n\r\n* CSS blur;\r\n* hidden DOM;\r\n* collapsed accordions;\r\n* client-side filtering.\r\n\r\n## 16.2 Public projection policy\r\n\r\nDefine which fields may appear for each spoiler level.\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n* `NONE`: public;\r\n* `PREVIEW_SAFE`: public preview;\r\n* `MINOR`: public only behind explicit spoiler projection;\r\n* `CHAPTER`: authorized or explicitly revealed;\r\n* `FINALE`: excluded from default public projection;\r\n* `CREATOR_ONLY`: Creator/owner only;\r\n* `CAPTAIN_ONLY`: Captain-authorized only;\r\n* `PARTICIPANT_PRIVATE`: approved participant context only.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need the complete spoiler-reveal UI.\r\n\r\nIt must implement:\r\n\r\n* classification;\r\n* server-side projection;\r\n* safe tests;\r\n* audit behavior.\r\n\r\n## 16.3 Search and metadata\r\n\r\nPrepare projection rules ensuring future search and Open Graph data exclude:\r\n\r\n* chapter spoilers;\r\n* finale spoilers;\r\n* hidden artifact names;\r\n* private route details;\r\n* secret variables;\r\n* answers;\r\n* Captain notes.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. Licensing Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate a first-class licensing model.\r\n\r\nSupported policies should include at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nALL_RIGHTS_RESERVED\r\nUSE_IN_CHRONICLES_WITH_ATTRIBUTION\r\nREMIX_WITH_ATTRIBUTION\r\nNONCOMMERCIAL_REMIX_WITH_ATTRIBUTION\r\nREDISTRIBUTE_UNMODIFIED_WITH_ATTRIBUTION\r\nPUBLIC_DOMAIN_OR_CC0_STYLE\r\nCUSTOM\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not falsely claim official Creative Commons compatibility unless the exact license is stored and represented correctly.\r\n\r\n## 17.1 License policy fields\r\n\r\nA license policy must express:\r\n\r\n* display name;\r\n* stable key;\r\n* use in private Chronicles;\r\n* use in public Chronicles;\r\n* modification permission;\r\n* redistribution permission;\r\n* commercial-use permission;\r\n* attribution requirement;\r\n* source-link requirement;\r\n* share-alike requirement if supported;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Attribution Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate attribution records representing:\r\n\r\n* credited profile;\r\n* credited display name;\r\n* source listing;\r\n* source release;\r\n* source URL or internal route;\r\n* contribution type;\r\n* required credit text;\r\n* asset reference if applicable;\r\n* license reference;\r\n* ordering;\r\n* immutable snapshot.\r\n\r\nRecommended contribution types:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nORIGINAL_CREATOR\r\nCO_CREATOR\r\nARTIST\r\nMODEL_CREATOR\r\nWRITER\r\nTRANSLATOR\r\nMUSICIAN\r\nVOICE_ACTOR\r\nPHOTOGRAPHER\r\nSOURCE_ASSET\r\nADAPTATION\r\nOTHER\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 18.1 Requirements\r\n\r\nAttribution must be:\r\n\r\n* explicit;\r\n* ordered;\r\n* snapshot into immutable releases;\r\n* retrievable through private and public projections as appropriate;\r\n* protected from silent deletion;\r\n* ready for later package manifests;\r\n* ready for later remix lineage.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need the complete automatic attribution generator.\r\n\r\nIt must provide the authoritative data structures and validation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Ownership Declarations\r\n\r\nCreate a publication ownership-declaration foundation.\r\n\r\nBefore later public publishing, Creators must be able to declare:\r\n\r\n* I created this content;\r\n* I have permission to publish this content;\r\n* I have permission to redistribute included assets;\r\n* I have listed required attribution;\r\n* I understand public visibility;\r\n* I understand the selected license.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 should model:\r\n\r\n* declaration version;\r\n* declarant profile;\r\n* timestamp;\r\n* listing/release;\r\n* accepted statements;\r\n* optional evidence references;\r\n* audit record.\r\n\r\nDo not add legal claims beyond platform policy.\r\n\r\nDo not present this as legal verification.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Public Projection Boundary\r\n\r\nThis is one of the most important Phase 1 requirements.\r\n\r\nCreate explicit public projection functions or services.\r\n\r\nDo not return raw ORM records directly from public routes.\r\n\r\nRecommended projections:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPublicCommunityProfile\r\nPublicCommunityListingSummary\r\nPublicCommunityListingDetail\r\nPublicCommunityReleaseSummary\r\nPublicCommunityReleaseDetail\r\nPrivateCommunityListing\r\nModeratorCommunityListing\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 20.1 Public projection must exclude\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n* passwords;\r\n* account email;\r\n* session tokens;\r\n* invitation details;\r\n* private profile identifiers;\r\n* raw source drafts;\r\n* unpublished Tale content;\r\n* accepted answers;\r\n* hidden variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* Player progress;\r\n* exact private coordinates;\r\n* participant legal identities;\r\n* unapproved media;\r\n* internal moderation notes;\r\n* storage paths;\r\n* scanner internals;\r\n* raw audit payloads;\r\n* secrets;\r\n* credentials.\r\n\r\n## 20.2 Chronicle sanitization\r\n\r\nFor Community Chronicle releases, create an explicit sanitization process.\r\n\r\nIt must define which parts of `PublishedTaleVersion` can enter:\r\n\r\n* public listing metadata;\r\n* installable release manifest;\r\n* private Creator preview;\r\n* future package content.\r\n\r\nPublic listing projection and installable package data are not the same thing.\r\n\r\nAn installable Chronicle release may later contain story content that public listing pages must not reveal.\r\n\r\n## 20.3 Projection tests\r\n\r\nUse hostile fixtures containing:\r\n\r\n* answers;\r\n* private notes;\r\n* finale text;\r\n* coordinates;\r\n* invitation data;\r\n* session data;\r\n* internal IDs;\r\n* hidden artifacts.\r\n\r\nProve the public projection excludes them.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Real-World Location Safety Foundation\r\n\r\nCommunity content may contain real-world treasure-hunt locations.\r\n\r\nCreate a typed location classification:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype CommunityLocationClassification =\r\n  | \"FICTIONAL\"\r\n  | \"GENERIC\"\r\n  | \"PUBLIC_REAL_WORLD\"\r\n  | \"APPROXIMATE_REAL_WORLD\"\r\n  | \"PRIVATE_REAL_WORLD\";\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 21.1 Phase 1 rules\r\n\r\nDefine and enforce:\r\n\r\n* private real-world locations cannot be projected publicly in exact form;\r\n* exact coordinates require `PUBLIC_REAL_WORLD`;\r\n* approximate locations must be generalized;\r\n* private homes must default to private;\r\n* school, workplace, child-related, or recurring personal locations receive stricter handling;\r\n* public projection must omit private route geometry;\r\n* future public media processing must strip EXIF GPS metadata.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need the full location publishing wizard.\r\n\r\nIt must create the classification and projection policy.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. Community Asset Reference Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate or extend an asset-reference model suitable for community releases.\r\n\r\nDo not duplicate existing asset storage if the current asset domain can be extended cleanly.\r\n\r\nTrack or plan for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nasset identity\r\nowner\r\ncontent checksum\r\ndeclared MIME\r\ndetected MIME\r\nfile size\r\ndimensions\r\nduration\r\ntriangle count\r\ntexture count\r\ntexture dimensions\r\nscan status\r\nprocessing status\r\nstorage provider\r\nstorage key\r\nvisibility\r\nimmutable release usage\r\naccessibility metadata\r\nlicense\r\nattribution\r\ncreatedAt\r\nupdatedAt\r\nremovedAt\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 22.1 Phase 1 boundaries\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must implement:\r\n\r\n* asset reference;\r\n* ownership;\r\n* checksum field;\r\n* storage-provider key;\r\n* processing status;\r\n* scan status;\r\n* public/private visibility;\r\n* accessibility metadata foundation;\r\n* immutable release relationship.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 does not need:\r\n\r\n* actual model parsing;\r\n* video transcoding;\r\n* thumbnail generation;\r\n* malware-scanner integration;\r\n* public uploads.\r\n\r\n## 22.2 Status truth\r\n\r\nRecommended scan states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nNOT_REQUIRED\r\nPENDING\r\nSCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED\r\nSCANNING\r\nCLEAN\r\nSUSPICIOUS\r\nMALICIOUS\r\nFAILED\r\n```\r\n\r\nRecommended processing states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nUPLOADED\r\nVALIDATING\r\nSCANNING\r\nPROCESSING\r\nREADY\r\nFAILED\r\nQUARANTINED\r\nREMOVED\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not treat `SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED` as `CLEAN`.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. Storage Provider Abstraction\r\n\r\nCreate or formalize a storage-provider interface.\r\n\r\nThe application must not permanently bind community assets to a single local filesystem root.\r\n\r\nRecommended capabilities:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ninterface CommunityAssetStorageProvider {\r\n  putStagedObject(...): Promise<StoredObjectReference>;\r\n  readObject(...): Promise<ReadableStream | Buffer>;\r\n  objectExists(...): Promise<boolean>;\r\n  copyToImmutableRelease(...): Promise<StoredObjectReference>;\r\n  moveToQuarantine(...): Promise<StoredObjectReference>;\r\n  markRemoved(...): Promise<void>;\r\n  getChecksum(...): Promise<string>;\r\n  getMetadata(...): Promise<StoredObjectMetadata>;\r\n  deleteDevelopmentObject?(...): Promise<void>;\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse repository conventions and avoid unnecessary custom abstractions if one already exists.\r\n\r\n## 23.1 Required adapters\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must provide:\r\n\r\n* local-development adapter;\r\n* provider interface for future object storage;\r\n* staging namespace;\r\n* immutable release namespace;\r\n* quarantine namespace;\r\n* checksum behavior;\r\n* safe path construction.\r\n\r\nProduction object storage does not need to be deployed in Phase 1.\r\n\r\n## 23.2 Path safety\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* path traversal;\r\n* absolute paths;\r\n* separator tricks;\r\n* Unicode path confusion where practical;\r\n* duplicate keys;\r\n* cross-profile access;\r\n* immutable overwrite attempts.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. Background Job Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate or extend a durable job abstraction.\r\n\r\nFuture jobs include:\r\n\r\n* asset scanning;\r\n* image processing;\r\n* model processing;\r\n* thumbnail generation;\r\n* package construction;\r\n* checksum generation;\r\n* search indexing;\r\n* publication finalization;\r\n* moderation notifications;\r\n* aggregate reconciliation;\r\n* abandoned-upload cleanup;\r\n* Keepsake generation;\r\n* dependency updates.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must not implement all processors.\r\n\r\nIt must create a foundation that supports:\r\n\r\n* typed job name;\r\n* typed payload schema;\r\n* idempotency key;\r\n* attempt count;\r\n* state;\r\n* scheduled time;\r\n* started time;\r\n* completed time;\r\n* failure code;\r\n* bounded retry;\r\n* dead-letter or terminal-failure state;\r\n* audit linkage;\r\n* safe payload logging.\r\n\r\nRecommended states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPENDING\r\nRUNNING\r\nSUCCEEDED\r\nRETRYABLE_FAILURE\r\nFAILED\r\nCANCELLED\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 24.1 Development provider\r\n\r\nA safe development provider may execute inline or through a local worker if clearly documented.\r\n\r\nIt must not disguise synchronous route execution as a durable production worker.\r\n\r\n## 24.2 Provider seam\r\n\r\nProvide a clean seam for future:\r\n\r\n* database queue;\r\n* Redis-backed queue;\r\n* dedicated worker;\r\n* external job service.\r\n\r\nDo not introduce unnecessary infrastructure in Phase 1 if the repository has no worker yet.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Transactional Outbox Foundation\r\n\r\nWhere a database mutation must reliably trigger later work, create a transactional outbox foundation.\r\n\r\nEvents may include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCOMMUNITY_PROFILE_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_LISTING_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_LISTING_UPDATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_REQUESTED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_PUBLISHED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_REGISTERED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_QUARANTINED\r\nCOMMUNITY_SEARCH_REINDEX_REQUESTED\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 25.1 Requirements\r\n\r\nOutbox records must include:\r\n\r\n* stable ID;\r\n* event type;\r\n* aggregate type;\r\n* aggregate ID;\r\n* payload schema version;\r\n* sanitized payload;\r\n* idempotency key;\r\n* created time;\r\n* available time;\r\n* claimed time;\r\n* processed time;\r\n* attempt count;\r\n* terminal failure state.\r\n\r\nThe mutation and outbox insertion must occur in the same transaction.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may implement a minimal dispatcher.\r\n\r\nDo not mark an outbox event processed before its side effect succeeds.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Authorization Foundation\r\n\r\nDefine explicit community roles and capabilities.\r\n\r\nRecommended roles:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nVISITOR\r\nAUTHENTICATED_USER\r\nCREATOR\r\nVERIFIED_CREATOR\r\nMODERATOR\r\nADMIN\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use hidden UI as authorization.\r\n\r\n## 26.1 Required permissions\r\n\r\nCreate centralized checks for:\r\n\r\n* view public listing;\r\n* view unlisted listing;\r\n* view private listing;\r\n* create Community Profile;\r\n* edit own Community Profile;\r\n* create listing;\r\n* edit own listing;\r\n* create release;\r\n* request publication;\r\n* publish under development policy;\r\n* archive own listing;\r\n* moderate listing;\r\n* quarantine release;\r\n* restore release;\r\n* view private moderation data;\r\n* view public projection;\r\n* view owner projection.\r\n\r\n## 26.2 Suspended and blocked state\r\n\r\nModel or integrate:\r\n\r\n* account suspension;\r\n* profile suspension;\r\n* publishing suspension;\r\n* content quarantine;\r\n* blocked relationships where an existing system supports them.\r\n\r\nA suspended Creator must not publish.\r\n\r\nA removed listing must not become public through a stale route.\r\n\r\n## 26.3 IDOR testing\r\n\r\nEvery private resource route must be tested with:\r\n\r\n* owner;\r\n* different authenticated user;\r\n* Creator;\r\n* moderator;\r\n* administrator;\r\n* anonymous user;\r\n* suspended user.\r\n\r\nDo not rely on unguessable IDs as authorization.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Base Rate Limiting and Abuse Controls\r\n\r\nUse the repositoryâ€™s centralized rate-limit abstraction.\r\n\r\nIf the current implementation uses process-local memory, document that as a Phase 4 production limitation, but do not create separate one-off maps.\r\n\r\nDefine Phase 1 rate-limit categories for:\r\n\r\n* profile creation;\r\n* profile edits;\r\n* listing creation;\r\n* listing edits;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
