Audit Events\r\n\r\nIntegrate with the canonical audit system.\r\n\r\nDo not create a third unrelated audit table if one can be extended.\r\n\r\nRequired audit categories include:\r\n\r\n* profile created;\r\n* profile updated;\r\n* handle changed;\r\n* listing created;\r\n* listing updated;\r\n* visibility changed;\r\n* publication state changed;\r\n* release created;\r\n* release deprecated;\r\n* release replacement set;\r\n* ownership declaration accepted;\r\n* license changed before release;\r\n* moderation status changed;\r\n* asset quarantined;\r\n* release quarantined;\r\n* release restored;\r\n* authorization denied for sensitive action.\r\n\r\nAudit records must:\r\n\r\n* identify actor;\r\n* identify subject;\r\n* identify action;\r\n* include timestamp;\r\n* include correlation ID;\r\n* avoid secrets and private story payloads;\r\n* preserve immutable historical meaning.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Lanternwake Community Foundation\r\n\r\nDo not implement the complete Community Harbor animation suite in Phase 1.\r\n\r\nCreate the architectural foundation.\r\n\r\n## 29.1 Community scene family\r\n\r\nRegister or reserve a community scene family compatible with existing Lanternwake conventions.\r\n\r\nRecommended future scenes:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncommunity-harbor-arrival\r\ncommunity-featured-reveal\r\ncommunity-card-collection-enter\r\ncommunity-listing-open\r\ncommunity-chronicle-preview\r\ncommunity-artifact-inspection\r\ncommunity-artifact-assembly-preview\r\ncommunity-profile-arrival\r\ncommunity-voyage-log-unfurl\r\ncommunity-save-to-collection\r\ncommunity-install-confirmation\r\ncommunity-fork-confirmation\r\ncommunity-publish-launch\r\ncommunity-release-update\r\ncommunity-report-submitted\r\ncommunity-moderation-state-change\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1 should not falsely mark all of these production-ready.\r\n\r\nUse reachability such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfuture-contract\r\ndevelopment-only\r\nproduction\r\ndeprecated\r\n```\r\n\r\n## 29.2 Contract requirements\r\n\r\nFreeze Community Harbor scene requirements:\r\n\r\n* scene family;\r\n* host naming;\r\n* target naming;\r\n* runtime ownership;\r\n* semantic final-state requirements;\r\n* reduced-motion policy;\r\n* interruption policy;\r\n* cleanup;\r\n* presentation receipt;\r\n* publication-success truth;\r\n* installation-success truth;\r\n* model fallback behavior;\r\n* focus restoration.\r\n\r\n## 29.3 No local animation hacks\r\n\r\nAny minimal Phase 1 community-development interface must:\r\n\r\n* use existing CSS state patterns for trivial interactions;\r\n* use Lanternwake for substantial presentations;\r\n* not add ad hoc GSAP timelines directly inside components;\r\n* not create a competing motion preference.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Feature Flags and Environment Configuration\r\n\r\nAdd or document Phase 1 configuration.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCOMMUNITY_ENABLED\r\nCOMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED\r\nCOMMUNITY_MODERATION_REQUIRED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_STORAGE_PROVIDER\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_ROOT\r\nCOMMUNITY_WORKER_ENABLED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RATE_LIMIT_PROFILE\r\nCOMMUNITY_PUBLIC_BASE_URL\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1 defaults should be safe.\r\n\r\nRecommended development defaults:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCOMMUNITY_ENABLED=true\r\nCOMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED=false\r\nCOMMUNITY_MODERATION_REQUIRED=true\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_STORAGE_PROVIDER=local\r\nCOMMUNITY_WORKER_ENABLED=false\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not commit credentials.\r\n\r\nFeature flags must not bypass authorization.\r\n\r\n`COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED=false` must prevent public publishing server-side, not merely hide a button.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Required Phase 1 Service Boundaries\r\n\r\nImplement clear service boundaries rather than placing domain logic in route handlers.\r\n\r\nRecommended services or equivalent:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunityProfileService\r\nCommunityListingService\r\nCommunityReleaseService\r\nCommunityPublicationPolicy\r\nCommunityVisibilityPolicy\r\nCommunityProjectionService\r\nCommunityLicenseService\r\nCommunityAttributionService\r\nCommunityAssetRegistry\r\nCommunityStorageService\r\nCommunityOutboxService\r\nCommunityAuthorizationService\r\nCommunityAuditService\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact number may differ.\r\n\r\nAvoid both extremes:\r\n\r\n* one 3,000-line `CommunityService`;\r\n* dozens of one-function abstractions with no coherent ownership.\r\n\r\nRoutes should primarily:\r\n\r\n1. authenticate;\r\n2. validate input;\r\n3. call a domain service;\r\n4. project response;\r\n5. map typed errors.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Phase 1 API Foundation\r\n\r\nImplement only the endpoints needed to validate the Phase 1 foundation.\r\n\r\nPotential routes:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nGET    /api/community/profile/me\r\nPOST   /api/community/profile\r\nPATCH  /api/community/profile/me\r\n\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings\r\nGET    /api/community/listings/:id/private\r\nPATCH  /api/community/listings/:id\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings/:id/validate\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings/:id/releases\r\nGET    /api/community/listings/:slug/public\r\nGET    /api/community/releases/:id/public\r\n```\r\n\r\nRevalidate routing conventions.\r\n\r\nDo not expose internal routes publicly if repository architecture uses server actions or another pattern.\r\n\r\n## 32.1 Input validation\r\n\r\nAll inputs must use strict schemas.\r\n\r\nReject:\r\n\r\n* unknown fields where appropriate;\r\n* oversized strings;\r\n* invalid handles;\r\n* invalid status transitions;\r\n* invalid visibility;\r\n* unsupported item types;\r\n* malformed semantic versions;\r\n* invalid spoiler levels;\r\n* invalid license references;\r\n* unauthorized source Tale versions.\r\n\r\n## 32.2 Error model\r\n\r\nUse typed errors such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCOMMUNITY_NOT_ENABLED\r\nCOMMUNITY_PROFILE_REQUIRED\r\nCOMMUNITY_HANDLE_TAKEN\r\nCOMMUNITY_LISTING_NOT_FOUND\r\nCOMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\r\nCOMMUNITY_INVALID_TRANSITION\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_VERSION_CONFLICT\r\nCOMMUNITY_SOURCE_NOT_IMMUTABLE\r\nCOMMUNITY_PUBLICATION_DISABLED\r\nCOMMUNITY_LICENSE_REQUIRED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ATTRIBUTION_INVALID\r\nCOMMUNITY_PRIVATE_LOCATION_BLOCKED\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not leak:\r\n\r\n* raw database errors;\r\n* storage paths;\r\n* account existence;\r\n* hidden listing existence;\r\n* private IDs;\r\n* internal moderation data.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Database Migration Requirements\r\n\r\nOne database owner must control all schema changes.\r\n\r\nBefore editing Prisma or migrations:\r\n\r\n1. inspect current schema;\r\n2. identify existing reusable models;\r\n3. identify naming conventions;\r\n4. identify database targets;\r\n5. identify current migration tooling;\r\n6. identify SQLite/MySQL compatibility rules;\r\n7. document proposed tables and relationships;\r\n8. confirm no concurrent schema owner is active.\r\n\r\nPotential models may include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCommunityProfile\r\nCommunityListing\r\nCommunityRelease\r\nCommunityReleaseAttribution\r\nCommunityLicensePolicy\r\nCommunityOwnershipDeclaration\r\nCommunityAssetReference\r\nCommunityOutboxEvent\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not create future tables such as reviews, follows, comments, and collections unless:\r\n\r\n* required for a clean foreign-key strategy now;\r\n* they are minimal;\r\n* their inclusion is documented;\r\n* no user-facing feature is falsely claimed.\r\n\r\nPrefer not to create unused speculative tables.\r\n\r\n## 33.1 Migration requirements\r\n\r\n* deterministic migration;\r\n* rollback/restore documentation;\r\n* Prisma validation;\r\n* SQLite compatibility if supported;\r\n* MySQL compatibility if supported;\r\n* indexes for ownership, slug, handle, listing state, visibility, release version;\r\n* unique constraints;\r\n* foreign keys;\r\n* check constraints where supported;\r\n* no destructive migration of existing data;\r\n* no mutation of canonical production data.\r\n\r\n## 33.2 Suggested uniqueness constraints\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n* normalized Community Profile handle;\r\n* Community Listing slug;\r\n* listing + semantic version;\r\n* release manifest checksum if policy requires;\r\n* outbox idempotency key;\r\n* ownership declaration version per release where appropriate.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 34. Data Retention and Deletion Policy\r\n\r\nDefine Phase 1 behavior for:\r\n\r\n* profile deletion request;\r\n* profile suspension;\r\n* listing archival;\r\n* listing removal;\r\n* release deprecation;\r\n* release quarantine;\r\n* audit retention;\r\n* attribution retention;\r\n* ownership declaration retention;\r\n* removed assets;\r\n* legal/copyright retention.\r\n\r\nA deleted or deactivated Creator must not silently erase required attribution from historical releases.\r\n\r\nA removed listing may retain:\r\n\r\n* stable ID;\r\n* moderation record;\r\n* release checksum;\r\n* attribution identity;\r\n* legal audit information.\r\n\r\nPublic content delivery must stop according to policy.\r\n\r\nDo not implement irreversible hard deletion without a documented requirement.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 35. Logging and Telemetry Privacy\r\n\r\nPhase 1 logs may include:\r\n\r\n* request/correlation ID;\r\n* profile ID;\r\n* listing ID;\r\n* release ID;\r\n* action;\r\n* transition;\r\n* authorization result;\r\n* projection type;\r\n* item type;\r\n* manifest schema version;\r\n* checksum prefix if safe;\r\n* duration;\r\n* error code.\r\n\r\nDo not log:\r\n\r\n* passwords;\r\n* credentials;\r\n* email addresses unless required and protected;\r\n* story prose;\r\n* accepted answers;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* invitation secrets;\r\n* exact private coordinates;\r\n* raw ownership evidence;\r\n* raw moderation evidence;\r\n* full manifests containing private content;\r\n* unpublished Tale payloads.\r\n\r\nAdd sanitized structured logging to sensitive services.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 36. Required Implementation Order\r\n\r\nFollow this sequence.\r\n\r\n## Step 1: Repository preflight\r\n\r\n* record Git state;\r\n* identify current baseline;\r\n* inspect worktrees;\r\n* identify concurrent changes;\r\n* choose safe branch/worktree;\r\n* identify database owner.\r\n\r\n## Step 2: Architecture survey\r\n\r\n* inspect canonical domains;\r\n* inspect legacy domains;\r\n* inspect identity;\r\n* inspect assets;\r\n* inspect authorization;\r\n* inspect storage;\r\n* inspect jobs;\r\n* inspect Lanternwake;\r\n* create repository inventory.\r\n\r\n## Step 3: Phase 1 design record\r\n\r\nFreeze:\r\n\r\n* taxonomy;\r\n* models;\r\n* projection policy;\r\n* release policy;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* license;\r\n* spoiler;\r\n* storage;\r\n* jobs;\r\n* migration plan.\r\n\r\nDo not parallelize high-conflict implementation before this is stable.\r\n\r\n## Step 4: Core domain types and validation\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* item types;\r\n* publication states;\r\n* visibility;\r\n* spoiler levels;\r\n* profile states;\r\n* license types;\r\n* attribution types;\r\n* release manifest schema;\r\n* typed errors.\r\n\r\nRun type checking.\r\n\r\n## Step Security Requirements\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must explicitly test or review:\r\n\r\n* IDOR;\r\n* mass assignment;\r\n* invalid status transitions;\r\n* private listing enumeration;\r\n* unlisted enumeration;\r\n* unsafe slug handling;\r\n* stored XSS in profile/listing text;\r\n* unsafe URL schemes in social links;\r\n* open redirect risk;\r\n* public projection leakage;\r\n* raw ORM record leakage;\r\n* SQL injection through filters;\r\n* path traversal in storage;\r\n* feature-flag bypass;\r\n* moderator authorization;\r\n* suspension bypass;\r\n* checksum spoofing;\r\n* source Tale substitution;\r\n* race conditions creating duplicate versions;\r\n* outbox payload leakage.\r\n\r\nUse strict input schemas.\r\n\r\nDo not accept arbitrary object payloads and spread them into database writes.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 41. Accessibility Requirements\r\n\r\nPhase 1 user-facing surfaces are limited, but any created interface must include:\r\n\r\n* semantic labels;\r\n* keyboard operation;\r\n* visible focus;\r\n* clear errors;\r\n* error summary where forms are complex;\r\n* no color-only status;\r\n* reduced-motion compliance;\r\n* accessible loading state;\r\n* live-region confirmation where appropriate;\r\n* 200% zoom usability;\r\n* mobile usability.\r\n\r\nProfile and listing forms must identify:\r\n\r\n* required fields;\r\n* validation errors;\r\n* privacy implications;\r\n* visibility state;\r\n* spoiler state;\r\n* license selection.\r\n\r\nDo not build inaccessible temporary interfaces with the excuse that later phases will replace them.\r\n\r\nTemporary interfaces have a remarkable tendency to become permanent after someone says, â€œWe can polish that later.â€\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 42. domain.\r\n* No third story architecture exists.\r\n* Community Profile references canonical identity.\r\n* Public catalog, immutable release, Studio draft, and Tale Session remain distinct.\r\n* taxonomy.\r\n* Every item type declares its release/install/remix capability.\r\n* Type-specific metadata has schemas or explicit extension points.\r\n* There is no unbounded generic JSON dumping ground.\r\n\r\n## 44.3 Profiles\r\n\r\n* Community Profile exists.\r\n* Handle uniqueness is enforced.\r\n* Handle normalization is tested.\r\n* Public projection excludes private account fields.\r\n* Suspended profiles cannot publish.\r\n* Profile edits are authorized.\r\n\r\n## 44.4 Listings\r\n\r\n* Community Listing exists.\r\n* Stable slugs exist.\r\n* Ownership is explicit.\r\n* Publication state is separate from visibility.\r\n* Visibility is centrally authorized.\r\n* Private and unlisted listings are not publicly enumerable.\r\n* Removed and quarantined states block public delivery.\r\n\r\n## 44.5 Releases\r\n\r\n* Community Release exists.\r\n* Releases are immutable after publication.\r\n* Semantic versions are unique within a listing.\r\n* Schema version is explicit.\r\n* Manifest checksum is deterministic.\r\n* Community Chronicles reference immutable published Tale versions.\r\n* Updating requires a new release.\r\n* Old releases remain identifiable.\r\n* Deprecation does not mutate release content.\r\n\r\n## 44.6 Privacy\r\n\r\n* Public projections are explicit.\r\n* Raw ORM records are not returned publicly.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes answers.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes hidden variables.\r\n* Public Chronicle metadata excludes Captain notes.\r\n* Private coordinates are excluded.\r\n* Invitation and typed.\r\n* Server projections enforce spoiler policy.\r\n* Finale content is excluded by default.\r\n* Creator-only content is excluded.\r\n* Captain-only content is excluded.\r\n*        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  fingerprint String\n  succeeded   Boolean\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n\n  @@index([fingerprint, createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel TallTale {\n  id                       String                 @id @default(cuid())\n  slug                     String                 @unique\n  title                    String\n  subtitle                 String?\n             String\n  currentDraftRevisionId   String?\n  latestPublishedVersionId String?\n  forkedFromTaleId         String?\n  forkedFromVersionId      String?\n  playerCountMin           Int Shared development documentation\n\n`Development_Docs` is the canonical repository location for shared development references, requirements, architecture notes, design decisions, implementation plans, testing notes, meeting notes, and other planning material that should remain available across Codex sessions and computers.\n\nFiles in this directory are normal Git-tracked project files. The repository's end-of-task synchronization workflow inspects additions, modifications, renames, moves, and deletions here and, after safety checks, can commit and push them through the same approved branch workflow used for `Codex_Chats`. The files are not copied into the conversation archive or packaged into a second store.\n\n## Working from another computer\n\nBefore relying on or editing these documents:\n\n1. Confirm that this is the intended repository and branch with `git remote -v`, `git branch --show-current`, and `git status --short --branch`.\n2. Preserve and review any local modifications. Do not reset, clean, restore, or force-push them away.\n3. Run `git fetch --prune origin`, then compare the branch with its upstream using `git rev-list --left-right --count \"HEAD...@{upstream}\"`.\n4. If the branch is only behind and the working tree is clean, update it with `git pull --ff-only`. If it is ahead, diverged, or locally modified, reconcile the work deliberately before editing these documents.\n5. If Git reports a conflict, preserve both versions and resolve it visibly. Do not push unresolved conflict markers. For an unmergeable binary document, retain clearly named local and remote variants for review when repository conventions permit.\n\nThis pull-before-edit check matters: a Codex session on a different computer must synchronize the latest repository state before treating local `Development_Docs` content as current.\n\n## Safety and change behavior\n\nDo not store secrets, credentials, private keys, access or API tokens, production database exports, private authentication caches, or sensitive company information here unless both the repository and the specific contents have been explicitly approved for that purpose. This repository may be publicly visible. Temporary, recovery, credential-shaped, and obvious secret files are ignored, and the synchronizer also checks changed file names, bounded textual content, and file sizes before staging.\n\nDeleting a tracked file locally and committing that deletion also removes it from the GitHub version after the commit is pushed. Git history can still preserve earlier versions of documents that were changed or deleted, subject to the repository's retention and history policies.\n\nPreserve authored formatting and native file types. Do not rewrite a document merely to normalize it, and do not use this directory as a dumping ground for terminal output, build artifacts, dependency archives, database dumps, application installers, or routine generated test results.\n\nGit tracks files rather than empty directories. A new subdirectory is synchronized automatically when it contains an eligible file; use an intentional `.gitkeep` only when preserving an otherwise empty directory is genuinely necessary.\n\r\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\n\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\n\n## Current phase\n\nThe application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Players have durable identities, invitation acceptance, a multi-voyage library, live waiting/runtime state, and exact-version archives. Captains can create version-pinned crew voyages, deliver individually hashed link/QR/code/PIN invitations, manage invitation lifecycle, preview the Player-safe surface, and launch accepted crews. Creator Studio retains immutable publishing and now adds structured version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork provenance.\n\nThe original companion and expanded Game Master Command Center remain integrated compatibility surfaces. A shared animation director coordinates the harbor, journal, chart, artifact, gateway, and finale experiences without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\n\n## Stack\n\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\n- Prisma 6.19.3 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\n- Vitest and Playwright\n\n## Reproducible setup\n\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\n\n### Fastest Windows demo\n\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\n\n```powershell\nnpm run dev:full\n```\n\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, creates generic development data only when the voyage is missing, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Existing campaign progress is preserved across normal stop/start cycles. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\n\n- Role gateway: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n- Player library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/player`\n- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\n- Returning Player development login: `sera` / the configured `PLAYER_PASSWORD` (or `PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE` fallback)\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n- Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\n\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\n\n### Portable manual setup\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\ncd treasurehuntSoT\nnpm ci\ncp .env.example .env\nnpm run db:generate\nnpm run db:migrate\nnpm run db:seed\nnpm run dev\n```\n\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\n\n## Commands\n\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\n\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\n\n## Repository workflow\n\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\n\n## Important limitations\n\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\n\n## Next steps\n\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\n\r\nHISTORY_SHARED\r\nb5b5d6351 merge(language): integrate Universal Language onto Lanternwake\nfb6c3c8e9 fix(lanternwake): preserve immutable asset contracts\n3c2122cec feat(lanternwake): freeze Phase 5 animation contracts\nca5d3bd2b feat: migrate Captain's Console language\n70b87e04f feat: enforce Voyagewright product language\nec19f2115 feat: complete Project Lanternwake Phase 4\n7521afa04 feat: bring Phase 4 harbor surfaces alive\n44c00db7a feat: implement Lanternwake Phase 3 player cinematics\nd529b59e0 feat: implement Project Lanternwake Phase 2 runtime ownership\ne469b1205 feat: implement unified Tall Tale platform\nCANDIDATE_SCHEMA_FILES\r\nREF=integration/lanternwake-phase5-universal-language\r\nM\tpackage.json\nA\tprisma/migrations/20260721113000_project_one_voyage/migration.sql\nR100\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0003_tall_tale_studio_phase1/migration.sql\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0003_chronicle_studio_phase1/migration.sql\nR100\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0004_tall_tale_platform/migration.sql\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0004_chronicle_platform/migration.sql\nA\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0005_project_one_voyage/migration.sql\nM\tprisma/schema.prisma\nREF=codex/project-wayfarer-phase1-unified-identity\r\nM\tpackage.json\nA\tprisma/migrations/20260721120000_wayfarer_unified_identity/migration.sql\nA\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0005_wayfarer_unified_identity/migration.sql\nM\tprisma/schema.prisma\nREF=codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1\r\nM\tpackage.json\nA\tprisma/migrations/20260721130000_project_sealed_hold_phase1/migration.sql\nA\tprisma/mysql-migrations/0005_project_sealed_hold_phase1/migration.sql\nM\tprisma/schema.prisma\n", "type": "input_text"}]

revisionNumber])\n  @@index([taleId, updatedAt])\n}\n\nmodel TaleChapter {\n  id                String       @id @default(cuid())\n  draftRevisionId   String\n  title             String\n  subtitle onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  @@index([assetId, …25 tokens truncated…ough and carries invitation/ready/active/completed/declined/removed state plus per-Player pin/hide preferences. `Invitation` stores only token/code/PIN hashes and safe prefixes; `InvitationEvent` records lifecycle. `RevealState` records content actually revealed. `PlatformRoleAssignment` supplements existing staff capabilities. `PlatformAuditEvent` records resource actions with correlation IDs and secret-filtered metadata.\n\nSupported playthrough progression is `DRAFT_SETUP -> INVITING -> READY/SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE <-> PAUSED -> COMPLETED`, with cancellation before launch and abandonment after launch. Memberships move through invited, accepted, ready, active, and completed or terminate as declined/removed. Invitation links and codes move through created/sent/copied/viewed into ready/consumed, or terminate as declined/expired/revoked/replaced. Terminal transitions do not reopen in place.\n\n## Invitations and crew launch\n\nThe Captain wizard selects an exact published version, configures progression/hints/side quests/schedule/accessibility, adds one or more Players, chooses PIN/account/lifetime policy, reviews Player-safe content, and creates the playthrough plus all membership/invitation records in one transaction. Each crew member receives a distinct high-entropy link, QR code, human short code, and copyable message. Full secrets are returned only by creation or replacement responses and are never listed again.\n\nLink and code resolution is rate-limited. The pending invitation is held in a short-lived HttpOnly SameSite cookie with its own CSRF token. Acceptance atomically claims one redemption, binds or creates the Player identity, readies membership, records invitation/audit events, and readies the playthrough only when no invitation remains pending. Safe retries of an already accepted credential return the existing membership. Expired, declined, revoked, and replaced credentials are rejected before protected content is rendered. Account-required invitations can target only an existing claimed Player and must be accepted while signed into that exact profile.\n\nLaunch requires an assigned Captain, a ready/scheduled playthrough, at least one ready Player, the expected concurrency version, and a playable entry block. It promotes ready memberships, consumes accepted invitations, appends ordered launch/block events, records reveal state, and pushes sanitized realtime progression. The waiting room polls canonical state as a fallback and opens the runtime only after persisted launch.\n\n## Projection and authorization rules\n\nPlayer projections are allowlists. They contain current Player-safe Tale copy, revealed blocks/assets, crew readiness, and exact edition metadata; accepted answers, Captain instructions, Creator notes, future branches, private variables, storage keys, and raw event payloads are removed. Completed archives are rebuilt from the pinned published snapshot plus visited events/reveal state, not from the current draft or latest release.\n\nThe journal projection extends that allowlist to all released readable blocks, typed presentation metadata, released hints, visited-choice summaries, current-objective indexes, and completion/edition data. Recursive sanitization runs before any page is built. Logic-only blocks and unreleased future content are omitted. Player reading position is stored separately in profile preferences and cannot advance, rewind, or otherwise mutate `TaleSession` progression.\n\nMedia reads require one of four explicit scopes: authenticated Creator asset management; Player membership in the exact version-bound playthrough plus reveal/role inclusion; pending invitation cover access; or the current public catalog cover. Original downloads require asset-management permission. SSE authenticates before streaming and periodically rechecks membership, emitting an access-revoked close signal when authorization changes.\n\nAll cookie-authenticated staff and Player mutations require CSRF, except legacy compatibility runtime cookies whose SameSite boundary and opaque session token are retained. Login, invitation-code lookup, Player actions, uploads, and helper endpoints are bounded by rate limits. Identity and invitation secrets are cryptographically random, stored only as hashes, sent through HttpOnly Secure-in-production cookies where applicable, and rotated/revoked on session replacement or logout. Audit metadata rejects secret-, answer-, snapshot-, payload-, and private-note-bearing fields.\n\n## Migration, backfill, and rollback\n\nSQLite migration `20260718020000_tall_tale_platform` and MySQL migration `0004_tall_tale_platform` are additive. They add platform fields and tables without rebuilding `TaleSession`, so existing IDs, version bindings, event sequences, access hashes, inventory, variables, and timestamps remain intact. The progress-preserving seed path creates missing development Player/staff assignments and backfills only non-preview sessions without memberships. Each gets a placeholder Player membership mapped from the existing session state, a voyage/launch value derived from existing fields, and block reveal records derived from historical `blockEntered` events. It does not reset campaign or Tall Tale progress.\n\n`scripts/verify-platform-backfill.ts` creates a legacy-shaped proof row in the disposable validation database, runs normal `--ensure`, then proves identity, event, version, membership, reveal, and timestamp preservation. `scripts/verify-database.ts` additionally checks published checksums, scoped development roles, membership coverage, exact version relations, and audit metadata.\n\nThere is no destructive down migration. Before production migration, back up the database and asset volume and verify restore. Rollback is application rollback plus restoration of that matched backup. The MySQL SQL is maintained for production parity, but a live MySQL integration environment is still required before production rollout.\n\n## Validation\n\nUnit tests cover playthrough/membership/invitation state rules, scoped authorization, and audit redaction. The Chromium mutation journey forks and publishes a Tale, creates and accepts an invitation, denies cross-role access and missing CSRF, launches, publishes a newer edition, proves the live session remains pinned, completes the Tale, validates the exact-version archive, persists pin/hide preferences, proves idempotent acceptance, and verifies replacement/revocation. The reduced-motion responsive gateway and authorization denials also run as a read-only mobile WebKit check. The repository release gate remains `npm run validate`.\n\r\n# Tall Tale Studio Phase 1\n\n## Delivered workflow\n\nTall Tale Studio is an additive authoring and play system. It does not replace the original single-campaign companion.\n\n- `/studio` lists drafts, validation state, release state, assets, and sessions.\n- `/studio/tales/new` creates a private tale and first chapter.\n- `/studio/tales/[id]` edits a vertical story graph with optimistic autosave, undo/redo, dnd-kit pointer/keyboard sorting, and explicit add/move controls.\n- Settings, assets, locations, artifacts, and version history have dedicated nested routes.\n- `/tales` is the dynamic public catalog. `/play/[taleSlug]` starts a version-pinned session.\n- `/captain` lists live sessions; `/captain/sessions/[id]` controls progression and exposes the development verification simulator.\n\nCreator and publisher operations require a server-side GM session, explicit capabilities, and CSRF. Player access tokens are random, stored only as hashes, and retained in a strict HTTP-only browser cookie rather than the session URL. Media storage keys are opaque and validated before filesystem access.\n\nThe editor includes searchable Block Library, Chapters, and Story Outline tabs; collapsible chapters; visible flow/drop indicators; and responsive inspector behavior. A selected block can be rendered in an isolated desktop or mobile viewport with reduced-motion and replay controls, while Full Tale Preview and Play From Here use expiring draft sessions. The More menu exposes settings, version history, duplicate, and guarded archive actions.\n\n## Authoring model\n\nThe registry in `src/tall-tale/block-registry.ts` is the source of truth for all Phase 1 blocks:\n\n1. Narrative\n2. Captain's Note\n3. Riddle\n4. Information\n5. Travel Direction\n6. Location\n7. Arrival Check\n8. Image\n9. Image Transformation\n10. Cinematic\n11. Audio\n12. Artifact Reveal\n13. Hidden Message Reveal\n14. Collection Update\n15. Confirmation\n16. Choice\n17. Text Answer\n18. Captain Approval\n19. Wait\n20. Condition\n21. Set Variable\n22. Chapter Complete\n23. Tale Complete\n\nEach entry owns a stable type ID, category, defaults, inspector metadata, Zod configuration schema, asset-field declarations, and verification-provider dispatch. Unknown types render a safe diagnostic card and do not execute arbitrary behavior.\n\nThe editor sends the complete normalized draft with its `autosaveVersion`. The server claims that exact token before replacing chapter and block rows. A stale browser receives `DRAFT_CONFLICT`; its local state is not discarded. Choice and condition targets become explicit `BlockConnection` records. Duplication assigns new chapter/block IDs and remaps branch targets.\n\n## Publishing and sessions\n\nPublishing runs server-side graph, configuration, asset, and reachability validation. Blocking errors prevent a release; warnings remain visible. A successful publish serializes tale metadata, chapters, blocks, connections, locations, artifacts, and exact asset variants into a checksummed immutable snapshot. The transaction advances `latestPublishedVersionId` and marks the previous release non-current.\n\nThe catalog lists only current public releases; current unlisted releases remain available by their direct address. Starting a game records the exact `publishedVersionId`; later releases cannot alter that session. Preview sessions instead carry a draft snapshot and are visibly labeled. Every session event has a monotonic sequence and globally unique idempotency key.\n\nVersion History can preview any immutable release, compare two releases with structured added/removed/moved/renamed/access-scope changes, copy a release into a newly numbered draft, or fork it as a separate private Tall Tale with explicit provenance. None of those operations mutate a published snapshot or silently retarget active playthroughs. The catalog reports New, In Progress, or Completed/Replayable for compatibility browser sessions; the canonical Player library groups durable identity memberships by invitation, waiting, active, completed, replay/new-edition, and closed state.\n\nThe progression engine advances automatic blocks, evaluates variables/conditions, grants artifacts once, and opens standardized verification requests. Text answers, player confirmations, timers, Captain decisions, helper submissions, and the simulator converge on that engine. It rejects duplicate, stale, wrong-session, wrong-version, wrong-block, and wrong-request submissions.\n\n## Assets and reusable libraries\n\nUploads validate MIME allowlists, size, and magic bytes. Originals are retained; images receive WebP thumbnail, preview, optimized, and mobile derivatives with checksums. Duplicate content returns the existing logical asset. Replacement adds new variants to the same asset ID, preserving existing draft references and older version snapshots. Published media requests are restricted to variants captured by that version.\n\nAssets support display metadata, tags, semantic roles, collections, search/filtering by media/context/role/tag/collection/usage, recent and unused views, lazy thumbnails, paged expansion, drag-to-field assignment, usage lookup, original download, replacement, and guarded archive. A local `TaleAssetStorage` implementation isolates storage operations behind a replaceable interface. Locations support searchable/sortable records, duplicate/edit/archive, used-by counts, player copy, private Captain notes, map/display assets, a reference collection, and a future verification profile. Artifacts provide the parallel searchable/duplicable workflow for lore, ordinary-object labels, artwork/reveal/model assets, inventory categories, and persistence.\n\nInteractive player, Captain, upload, and helper routes apply bounded per-process rate limits in addition to authorization, CSRF, idempotency, and optimistic concurrency. Structured logs cover publish, asset ingestion, session starts, accepted/rejected verification, and sanitized API failures without recording answers, credentials, bearer tokens, or raw evidence.\n\n## Data and migration\n\nSQLite migration `20260717213000_tall_tale_studio_phase1` and MySQL migration `0003_tall_tale_studio_phase1` add the authoring domain. The subsequent platform migrations extend that same model with identity, membership, invitation, reveal, role, and audit data. The seed is progress-safe under `--ensure`; an explicit preset remains the only development reset path.\n\nFor local SQLite:\n\n```powershell\nnpm run db:generate\nnpm run db:migrate\nnpm run db:seed\n```\n\nFor a fresh MySQL database, apply the platform script after the three prior domain scripts in the exact order documented in `docs/deployment.md`.\n\n## Operational checklist\n\nBefore release, run `npm run validate`. Manually confirm narrow/mobile editor navigation, draft conflict messaging with two windows, published-session pinning after publishing a newer release, asset archive protection, Captain disconnect/reconnect state, and helper rejection cases. Keep `.data/tall-tale-assets` or the configured durable asset root backed up alongside the database.\n\r\n# Animation architecture\n\nThe cinematic layer is an orchestration system, not a set of component timers. `AnimationProvider` owns one `AnimationDirector`; every meaningful scene is registered by name and built as a GSAP timeline inside a scoped `gsap.context`. Motion handles component presence and direct interaction, StPageFlip handles the journal's physical page surface, Rive owns stateful vector objects, and Lottie owns ambient or illustrative loops.\n\n## Phase 3 Player progression orchestration\n\n`ProgressionPresentationController` is the compatibility Player adapter above the Director. Its pure deterministic queue admits immutable Player-safe requests, deduplicates event identity, rejects stale work, and orders authoritative live/reconnect requests before replay. Sequence, policy priority, and stable request identity break ties. Replay always receives a fresh playback identity, stays permanently acknowledgment-ineligible, and may be interrupted by authoritative work outside a semantic commit boundary.\n\nOne persistent `SceneHost kind=\"player-progression\"` supplies the global registered targets. Unrelated event-family target layers stay imperceptible and ineligible while the selected event family is active. A relevant section may contribute one exact registered handle only after the global semantic commit; the local handoff is optional, nonblocking, owns no acknowledgment or fallback, and is revoked on target/status cleanup. Hidden or unrelated sections are never mounted to manufacture targets.\n\nThe wrapper receipt is intentionally stricter than visual completion. Acknowledgment requires an authoritative source, a successful readable outcome, the Director's acknowledgment permission, final-state reconciliation or verified fallback, and successful restoration. `finalStateRuntime` commits or reconciles the settled UI, verifies the readable heading/summary, renders fallback on target/runtime failure, and performs idempotent cleanup. Skip converges through that same boundary rather than bypassing it.\n\n## Server-synchronized sequence\n\nFor an authenticated mutation, the director starts the real request immediately and plays only the non-authoritative opening while the request is pending. A slow request enters a bounded idle loop. Success content cannot start until the server accepts the mutation. A rejection runs the scene's failure branch and leaves the existing public snapshot in place.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n  participant UI\n  participant Director\n  participant API\n  UI->>Director: play(scene, operation)\n  Director->>API: start operation immediately\n  Director->>Director: opening timeline\n  alt operation is still pending\n    Director->>Director: await-server idle loop\n  end\n  API-->>Director: success or failure\n  alt success\n    Director->>Director: success timeline\n    Director-->>UI: reconcile public snapshot\n  else failure\n    Director->>Director: failure/reversal timeline\n    Director-->>UI: preserve state and report error\n  end\n```\n\nThe player consumes one SSE connection. Events are sorted through one promise queue, mapped to a registered scene, presented, receipt-checked, conditionally acknowledged, and then reconciled from a fresh server-filtered snapshot. Navigation, page turns, and decorative loops do not create additional event streams.\n\n## Lifecycle and control contract\n\nThe director exposes play, queue, pause, resume, seek by progress or label, speed from 0.25x to 2x, skip, supported reverse, cancel, and kill. It cancels outstanding waits, kills current timelines, reverses cleanup registration order, reverts SplitText/GSAP contexts, and releases property ownership on completion or unmount. Document visibility pauses an active timeline; a user-paused timeline does not resume merely because the tab becomes visible.\n\nEvery scene reports a live snapshot: scene, phase, label, progress, speed, mode, queue depth, pause state, and error. The development showcase and compact runtime controls consume this same state instead of maintaining a second playback model.\n\n## Motion modes\n\n`full`, `gentle`, and `reduced` are product modes. The browser's `prefers-reduced-motion` always resolves to `reduced`, even when the saved product preference says otherwise.\n\n- Full preserves authored distance, duration, ambience, particles, page curl, and camera-like framing.\n- Gentle uses 58% duration, 42% distance, fewer decorative effects, and shorter page turns while preserving narrative order.\n- Reduced uses immediate state changes, no spatial travel or ambient loops, a representative Lottie frame, stable Rive poses, and an accessible paged journal in place of page curl.\n\nModes do not change server state, content order, or acknowledgement rules. Skip also preserves those invariants: it renders the final visual state and then reconciles truth.\n\n## Adding a journal page type\n\nAdd the discriminated `kind` and safe fields to `JournalPage` in `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts`, generate a stable ID from public snapshot keys rather than array position, render that kind once in `JournalWorkspace`, and include it in the semantic/reduced page path. Never pass raw database records into the model. Extend the page-model tests for locked-content filtering, stable indices after a snapshot refresh, even spread padding, and chapter navigation; StPageFlip should receive the resulting React HTML through `updateFromHtml`, not a replacement instance.\n\n## Failure boundaries\n\nRive, Lottie, and StPageFlip are dynamically imported. Each has a local SVG or React fallback. Failed visual assets are recorded for the development metrics panel but do not block authentication, progression, reading, navigation, or GM commands. No animation failure is allowed to roll back a committed server transaction.\n\nPageFlip exposes `turn-start`, `turn-commit`, `turn-settle`, `turn-cancel`, and `turn-failed` with book/mount identity and current boundary generation. Queued turns rebase against the current page and generation when dispatched; a same-page request settles as a truthful cancel/no-op. `forceReadableFallback(reason)` abandons the failed runtime, preserves the current semantic page and focus where possible, and makes the static reader ready. Journal interactivity is not declared until the opening policy and PageFlip/static readiness produce a verified `JOURNAL_READY` receipt.\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

definitions.map((definition) => [\n    definition.name,\n    { ...definition, contract: sceneContracts[definition.name] } satisfies RegisteredSceneDefinition,\n  ]),\n) as Record<AnimationSceneName, RegisteredSceneDefinition>;\n\nexport function getSceneDefinition(name: AnimationSceneName) {\n  return sceneRegistry[name];\n}\n\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\nimport { createStudioTale, listStudioTales } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Sign in with a creator account to continue.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  return NextResponse.json({ csrfToken: session.csrfToken, tales: await listStudioTales() });\n}\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Sign in with a creator account to continue.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Your creator session has expired. Reload the page and try again.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    return NextResponse.json(await createStudioTale({ ...(await request.json()), creatorId: session.userId }), {\n      status: 201,\n    });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\r\nsrc/tall-tale\\assets.ts:40:export interface TaleAssetStorage {\nsrc/tall-tale\\assets.ts:45:class LocalTaleAssetStorage implements TaleAssetStorage {\nsrc/tall-tale\\assets.ts:57:const assetStorage: TaleAssetStorage = new LocalTaleAssetStorage();\nsrc/app/api/studio\\assets\\[assetId]\\route.ts:6:import { consumeRateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\nsrc/app/api/studio\\assets\\[assetId]\\route.ts:53:        { status: 429, headers: rateLimitHeaders(rate) },\nsrc/lib\\events.ts:9:export function publishCampaignEvent(campaignId: string, event: ClientProgressEvent) {\nsrc/lib\\events.ts:13:export function publishTaleSessionEvent(\nsrc/lib\\rate-limit.test.ts:2:import { consumeRateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\nsrc/lib\\rate-limit.test.ts:12:    expect(Number(rateLimitHeaders(rejected)[\"Retry-After\"])).toBeGreaterThan(0);\nsrc/lib\\rate-limit.ts:46:export function rateLimitHeaders(result: RateLimitResult) {\nsrc/app/api/studio\\tales\\[taleId]\\assets\\route.ts:6:import { consumeRateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\nsrc/app/api/studio\\tales\\[taleId]\\assets\\route.ts:39:        { status: 429, headers: rateLimitHeaders(rate) },\nsrc/tall-tale\\publishing.ts:92:export async function publishTale(\n", "type": "input_text"}]

 @unique\n+  normalizedHandle    String   @unique\n+  handle              String\n+  displayName         String\n+  biography           String?\n+  avatarAssetId       String?\n+  bannerAssetId       String?\n+  visibility          String   @default(\"COMMUNITY\")\n+  creatorStatus       String   @default(\"ACTIVE\")\n+  moderationStatus    String   @default(\"ACTIVE\")\n+  verificationStatus  String   @default(\"UNVERIFIED\")\n+  supportedLanguages  String   @default(\"[]\")\n+  socialLinks         String   @default(\"[]\")\n+  lastPublishedAt     DateTime?\n+  createdAt           DateTime @default(now())\n+  updatedAt           DateTime @updatedAt\n+  playerProfile       PlayerProfile? @relation(fields: [playerProfileId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\n+  listings            CommunityListing[]\n+  releasesPublished   CommunityRelease[] @relation(\"CommunityReleasePublisher\")\n+  attributions        CommunityReleaseAttribution[]\n+  declarations        CommunityOwnershipDeclaration[]\n+  assets              CommunityAssetReference[]\n+\n+  @@index([moderationStatus, visibility])\n+}\n+\n+model CommunityListing {\n+  id                String   @id @default(cuid())\n+  slug              String   @unique\n+  itemType          String\n+  ownerProfileId    String\n+  title             String\n+  shortDescription  String?\n+  longDescription   String?\n+  visibility        String   @default(\"PRIVATE\")\n+  publicationStatus String   @default(\"DRAFT\")\n+  moderationStatus  String   @default(\"ACTIVE\")\n+  spoilerLevel      String   @default(\"PREVIEW_SAFE\")\n+  locationClass     String   @default(\"FICTIONAL\")\n+  primaryCategory   String?\n+  tags              String   @default(\"[]\")\n+  contentWarnings   String   @default(\"[]\")\n+  coverAssetId      String?\n+  currentReleaseId  String?  @unique\n+  archivedAt        DateTime?\n+  removedAt         DateTime?\n+  publishedAt       DateTime?\n+  featuredAt        DateTime?\n+  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())\n+  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt\n+  owner             CommunityProfile @relation(fields: [ownerProfileId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)\n+  releases          CommunityRelease[]\n+  declarations      CommunityOwnershipDeclaration[]\n+\n+  @@index([ownerProfileId, updatedAt])\n+  @@index([publicationStatus, visibility])\n+}\n+\n+model CommunityRelease {\n+  id                           String   @id @default(cuid())\n+  listingId                    String\n+  semanticVersion              String\n+  manifestSchemaVersion        Int      @default(1)\n+  minimumPlatformVersion       String?\n+  sourcePublishedTaleVersionId String?\n+  manifest                     String\n+  manifestChecksum             String\n+  packageChecksum              String?\n+  releaseNotes                 String?\n+  compatibility                String   @default(\"{}\")\n+  licenseSnapshot              String\n+  attributionSnapshot          String   @default(\"[]\")\n+  spoilerSnapshot              String   @default(\"{}\")\n+  moderationStatus             String   @default(\"ACTIVE\")\n+  publishedByProfileId         String\n+  publishedAt                  DateTime @default(now())\n+  deprecatedAt                 DateTime?\n+  replacementReleaseId         String?\n+  createdAt                    DateTime @default(now())\n+  listing                      CommunityListing @relation(fields: [listingId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)\n+  sourcePublishedTaleVersion   PublishedTaleVersion? @relation(fields: [sourcePublishedTaleVersionId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)\n+  publishedBy                  CommunityProfile @relation(\"CommunityReleasePublisher\", fields: [publishedByProfileId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)\n+  attributions                 CommunityReleaseAttribution[]\n+  assetReferences              CommunityAssetReference[]\n+  declarations                 CommunityOwnershipDeclaration[]\n+\n+  @@unique([listingId, semanticVersion])\n+  @@index([listingId, publishedAt])\n+  @@index([sourcePublishedTaleVersionId])\n+}\n+\n+model CommunityReleaseAttribution {\n+  id                  String   @id @default(cuid())\n+  releaseId           String\n+  creditedProfileId   String?\n+  creditedDisplayName String\n+  sourceListingId     String?\n+  sourceReleaseId     String?\n+  sourceUrl           String?\n+  contributionType    String\n+  requiredCreditText  String?\n+  licenseReference    String?\n+  sortOrder           Int\n+  createdAt           DateTime @default(now())\n+  release             CommunityRelease @relation(fields: [releaseId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+  creditedProfile     CommunityProfile? This work does not merge One Voyage (`4e8f385`), Wayfarer (`a478d79`), or Sealed Hold (`4b93b4f`).\n+\n+## Repository inventory and boundaries\n+\n+`TallTale` is the canonical authored Chronicle; `TaleDraft` is mutable authoring state; `PublishedTaleVersion` is immutable source content; `TaleSession` is a live voyage; old `Campaign` tables remain production compatibility surfaces. `PlayerProfile` is the currently available canonical person/profile; `PlatformAuditEvent`, `TaleAssetStorage`, `consumeRateLimit`, and the Lanternwake scene registry are existing seams. Harborlight creates no alternate Chronicle, chapter, session, or login identity.\n+\n+`CommunityListing` is mutable catalog identity and release history. `CommunityRelease` is immutable distribution identity, bound by scalar source ID to an immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`; its manifest, checksum, attribution, license, and spoiler snapshots never change. A later correction creates a new release; listing quarantine/removal controls delivery without rewriting the release.\n+\n+## Convergence seams\n+\n+`CommunityProfile.identityKey` plus optional `playerProfileId` represents current Player Profile identity without copying credentials, email, sessions, or Player-only state. During Wayfarer convergence, the adapter maps `identityKey` to `UserAccount` and migrates only the optional compatibility pointer. Chronicle references are source-version IDs, keeping One Voyage terminology/domain ownership isolated. Sealed Hold remains the private encrypted transport authority; Harborlight owns community staging, immutable public/unlisted releases, checksums, and future distribution only.\n+\n+## Frozen policy\n+\n+Typed taxonomy has 14 item types with declared release/install/remix/search capabilities. Publication transitions are centralized; visibility is independent; public delivery allows only published COMMUNITY/FEATURED records. Unlisted and private records are never enumerable. Spoilers and private real-world locations are enforced at projection time, not CSS. Public projections are strict allowlists and exclude answers, hidden variables, Captain notes, invitation/session data, private coordinates, email, storage paths, raw drafts, and moderation notes.\n+\n+Release manifests use schema version 1, canonical JSON SHA-256, semantic versions, required immutable source IDs, license snapshot, and ordered attribution snapshot. License policies support reserved, attribution, remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom keys. Ownership declarations are auditable. Releases retain attribution through profile deletion/deactivation.\n+\n+Community assets use explicit scan/processing states; `SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED` is never clean. The local provider separates `staging`, `releases`, and `quarantine`, rejects traversal/absolute/separator tricks, and refuses immutable overwrite. A typed outbox model carries sanitized schema-versioned payloads and idempotency; a future durable dispatcher must set `processedAt` only after success.\n+\n+Community scenes remain future contracts under the existing Lanternwake Director and inherited reduced-motion/cleanup policies; no scene is production-ready in Phase 1.\n+\n+## Migration and rollback\n+\n+Schema changes are additive and use new, unique SQLite/MySQL migration identifiers. No legacy table is renamed, deleted, or repurposed. Rollback is application rollback plus restore of a matched database/asset backup; no destructive down migration is supplied. Phase 2 must add public publishing/review workflow, Wayfarer account relation, durable dispatcher/object storage, and combined convergence tests.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Integration_Manifest.md\n+# Harborlight Phase 1 Integration Manifest\n+\n+## Candidate\n+\n+- Branch: `codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor`\n+- Base/latest `origin/main`: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n+- Ending commit: recorded after final validation and push.\n+\n+## Additive surface\n+\n+Models: CommunityProfile, CommunityListing, CommunityRelease, CommunityReleaseAttribution, CommunityLicensePolicy, CommunityOwnershipDeclaration, CommunityAssetReference, CommunityOutboxEvent. Unique constraints: normalized handle, listing slug, listing/version, storage key, owner/checksum, outbox idempotency. Indexes cover profile moderation/visibility, listing ownership/state, release source/version, attribution order, asset release/visibility, and outbox dispatch.\n+\n+SQLite migration: `20260721140000_project_harborlight_phase1`; MySQL migration: `0006_project_harborlight_phase1`. Environment: `COMMUNITY_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_MODERATION_REQUIRED`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_STORAGE_PROVIDER`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_ROOT`, `COMMUNITY_WORKER_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_RATE_LIMIT_PROFILE`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_BASE_URL`.\n+\n+## Convergence risks and order\n+\n+One Voyage owns canonical Chronicle terminology/domain consolidation; merge it first, then retarget scalar source seams. Wayfarer owns `UserAccount`; next migrate `identityKey` adapter without copying identity secrets. Sealed Hold remains private encrypted package transport and should reconcile only shared checksum/storage utilities. Then run schema migration, public-projection hostile fixtures, IDOR/visibility tests, exact-version release tests, and storage/outbox integration tests on both SQLite and MySQL.\n+\n+Deferred destructive work: no legacy migration/deletion, no auth replacement, no package import/export, no public upload/publishing, no production object storage/worker. Retained compatibility: Campaign, PlayerProfile, TallTale, and current Lanternwake registry.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Data_Model.md\n+# Community Harbor data model\n+\n+The eight additive models are catalog/profile, release, attribution/license/declaration, asset, and outbox foundations. `CommunityRelease` points to the immutable `PublishedTaleVersion` by ID and never to a mutable draft. JSON columns hold only versioned snapshots or bounded arrays; item taxonomy and release manifests are Zod-validated TypeScript contracts rather than an unbounded type-specific payload.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Privacy_and_Spoiler_Model.md\n+# Community Harbor privacy and spoiler model\n+\n+Public projections are allowlists. They exclude raw source snapshots, answers, variables, Captain notes, finale/chapter spoiler fields, invitations, sessions, participant identity, email, storage keys, moderation notes, and exact private locations. Private/unlisted records are non-enumerable; private real-world locations have no public projection.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Licensing_and_Attribution.md\n+# Community Harbor licensing and attribution\n+\n+Every release snapshots a policy key/version and ordered attribution. Policies cover all-rights-reserved, attribution use/remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom terms. Ownership declarations record accepted statements and evidence references; they are declarations, not legal verification.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Release_Manifest.md\n+# Community Harbor release manifest\n+\n+Manifest schema 1 requires listing ID, semantic version, immutable source `PublishedTaleVersion` ID, public metadata, license snapshot, and ordered attribution. Canonical key-sorted JSON is SHA-256 checksummed. A release is immutable; deprecation/replacement changes delivery metadata only.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Storage_and_Job_Architecture.md\n+# Community Harbor storage and job architecture\n+\n+The local adapter isolates `staging`, immutable `releases`, and `quarantine`, with path safety and immutable-write protection. It is a development adapter only. CommunityOutboxEvent is the transactional dispatch record with sanitized payload, idempotency, availability, claim, attempts, processed time, and terminal failure. No production object store or worker is enabled.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Test_Plan.md\n+# Community Harbor Phase 1 test plan\n+\n+Focused tests cover taxonomy, handles, publication transitions, server flags, checksum determinism, hostile projection leakage, private-location denial, Chronicle metadata sanitization, namespace separation, traversal rejection, and immutable overwrite rejection. Required convergence tests add Prisma migration on isolated SQLite/MySQL databases, source-version transaction/outbox/audit behavior, authorization actors, and public enumeration behavior.\n*** End Patch";
const result = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof result === "string" ? result : JSON.stringify(result));

Added additive Prisma schema/migrations, typed community taxonomy/policy, safe public projection primitives, deterministic release manifest checksums, local staging/release/quarantine storage, docs, integration manifest, and focused tests. Key records are documented in [design record](<\\gwplastics.com\VT\Users\kgray\My Documents\treasurehunt\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\Development_Docs\Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Design_Record.md>) and [integration manifest](<\\gwplastics.com\VT\Users\kgray\My Documents\treasurehunt\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\Development_Docs\Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Integration_Manifest.md>).

Validation:

- Focused Harborlight: 8/8 tests passed.
- Full unit suite: 87 files, 912 tests passed.
- TypeScript, Prisma SQLite schema validation, focused lint, and format passed.
- All SQLite migrations, including `20260721140000_project_harborlight_phase1`, applied successfully to a disposable local SQLite DB.
- Prisma’s `migrate deploy` command was blocked by an unavailable bundled schema-engine binary; raw SQLite migration execution succeeded instead.

This is a pushed **blocked acceptance candidate**, not a claim that all Chart the Harbor acceptance criteria are complete. Material remaining work includes DB-backed profile/listing/release services and API routes, transactionally coupled outbox/audit creation, centralized community authorization/rate-limit integration, Lanternwake registry contracts, MySQL execution against an isolated server, and the full hostile privacy/IDOR integration matrix. No Phase 2 or convergence merge was started.
