Workstream P: Isolated MySQL Validation\r\n\r\nExecute the MySQL Harborlight migration against an isolated MySQL server or schema.\r\n\r\nDo not use production.\r\n\r\nDo not use a shared development schema without explicit proof and isolation.\r\n\r\nPreferred options, in order:\r\n\r\n1. repository-supported isolated MySQL test server;\r\n2. disposable local MySQL container if available;\r\n3. disposable local portable MySQL instance if already supported;\r\n4. dedicated isolated schema on an approved nonproduction server.\r\n\r\nRecord:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nhost\r\nport\r\ndatabase/schema name\r\nSELECT DATABASE()\r\nserver version\r\nmigration account\r\nruntime account where tested\r\nmigration table state\r\ntest nonce\r\nbranch\r\ncommit\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun:\r\n\r\n* complete migration chain;\r\n* empty schema migration;\r\n* representative existing-schema migration;\r\n* indexes and constraints inspection;\r\n* profile service tests;\r\n* listing service tests;\r\n* release transaction tests;\r\n* outbox transaction tests;\r\n* authorization tests;\r\n* privacy tests;\r\n* cleanup/retry tests.\r\n\r\nVerify runtime credentials do not receive unintended DDL permission if the repository distinguishes migration and runtime accounts.\r\n\r\nIf no safe MySQL environment can be created after exhaustive attempts:\r\n\r\n* complete every non-MySQL requirement;\r\n* provide exact commands and errors;\r\n* provide evidence of environment absence;\r\n* leave only the MySQL acceptance row externally blocked;\r\n* do not call Phase 1 complete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. Workstream Q: Complete Documentation\r\n\r\nReplace the three-line documentation placeholders with real governing records.\r\n\r\n## Community Harbor Data Model\r\n\r\nMust include:\r\n\r\n* every Phase 1 model;\r\n* every relation;\r\n* ownership;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* immutable fields;\r\n* mutable fields;\r\n* retention;\r\n* deletion;\r\n* convergence seam;\r\n* ER-style relationship description;\r\n* SQLite/MySQL differences.\r\n\r\n## Privacy and Spoiler Model\r\n\r\nMust include:\r\n\r\n* all projection types;\r\n* field allowlists;\r\n* spoiler levels;\r\n* location classes;\r\n* actor matrix;\r\n* enumeration behavior;\r\n* unlisted policy;\r\n* Crew-only policy;\r\n* Open Graph/search policy;\r\n* hostile fixture map;\r\n* remaining limitations.\r\n\r\n## Licensing and Attribution\r\n\r\nMust include:\r\n\r\n* each built-in policy;\r\n* exact permissions;\r\n* immutable snapshots;\r\n* attribution ordering;\r\n* asset exceptions;\r\n* ownership declarations;\r\n* profile deletion behavior;\r\n* future compatibility calculation seam.\r\n\r\n## Release Manifest\r\n\r\nMust include:\r\n\r\n* schema;\r\n* canonical JSON rules;\r\n* checksum algorithm;\r\n* semantic version rules;\r\n* source version relationship;\r\n* public metadata;\r\n* private exclusions;\r\n* license snapshot;\r\n* attribution snapshot;\r\n* compatibility;\r\n* schema evolution;\r\n* test vectors.\r\n\r\n## Storage and Job Architecture\r\n\r\nMust include:\r\n\r\n* provider interface;\r\n* local adapter;\r\n* namespaces;\r\n* path policy;\r\n* asset states;\r\n* scan truth;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* dispatcher;\r\n* retry;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* production object-storage seam;\r\n* production-worker seam;\r\n* backup limitations.\r\n\r\n## Test Plan\r\n\r\nMust map every Phase 1 acceptance criterion to:\r\n\r\n* test file;\r\n* test name or identifier;\r\n* database target;\r\n* actor;\r\n* expected result;\r\n* validation command;\r\n* final result.\r\n\r\n## Design Record\r\n\r\nExpand it to include every original required decision.\r\n\r\nDo not leave architecture decisions as one-sentence declarations.\r\n\r\n## Integration Manifest\r\n\r\nRecord:\r\n\r\n* base SHA;\r\n* initial candidate SHA;\r\n* closure commits;\r\n* schema migrations;\r\n* files changed;\r\n* convergence risks;\r\n* One Voyage seam;\r\n* Wayfarer seam;\r\n* Sealed Hold seam;\r\n* current remote branch;\r\n* validation results;\r\n* remaining blockers.\r\n\r\nDo not attempt an impossible self-referential final commit hash inside the commit itself.\r\n\r\nRecord the final implementation commit before any final documentation-only commit, and report the ultimate remote HEAD in the final response.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. Minimal Phase 1 Interface Proof\r\n\r\nThe original Phase 1 did not require the final Community Harbor UI.\r\n\r\nDo not build Phase 2 discovery or publishing experiences.\r\n\r\nHowever, provide enough interface proof to validate the foundation according to current project conventions.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, one of the following must exist:\r\n\r\n### Option A: Authenticated internal foundation screen\r\n\r\nSupports:\r\n\r\n* create/edit Community Profile;\r\n* create/edit private listing;\r\n* validate listing;\r\n* create internal restricted release;\r\n* inspect owner-safe data;\r\n* verify public projection.\r\n\r\n### Option B: Complete API/server-action integration with browser test harness\r\n\r\nSupports the same operations through an authenticated test surface.\r\n\r\nDo not create public navigation advertising unfinished Community Harbor.\r\n\r\nDo not create fake follower counts, reviews, badges, or public catalog cards.\r\n\r\nAny created interface must be:\r\n\r\n* keyboard accessible;\r\n* responsive;\r\n* reduced-motion compliant;\r\n* explicit that public publishing remains disabled;\r\n* built with current universal language.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Full Required Test Matrix\r\n\r\nThe final Phase 1 test suite must cover at least the following.\r\n\r\n## Taxonomy and validation\r\n\r\n* all 14 item types;\r\n* capabilities;\r\n* listing schema;\r\n* unknown fields;\r\n* tags;\r\n* slugs;\r\n* handles;\r\n* semantic versions;\r\n* manifest schema.\r\n\r\n## Profiles\r\n\r\n* create;\r\n* edit;\r\n* duplicate handle;\r\n* concurrent duplicate;\r\n* owner;\r\n* non-owner;\r\n* suspended;\r\n* public projection;\r\n* private projection;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit;\r\n* rollback.\r\n\r\n## Listings\r\n\r\n* create;\r\n* edit;\r\n* owner;\r\n* non-owner;\r\n* status transitions;\r\n* visibility;\r\n* Featured restriction;\r\n* archive;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* restore;\r\n* remove;\r\n* enumeration;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit;\r\n* rollback.\r\n\r\n## Releases\r\n\r\n* valid immutable source;\r\n* mutable source rejection;\r\n* source substitution;\r\n* duplicate version;\r\n* concurrent duplicate version;\r\n* checksum;\r\n* source relation;\r\n* license snapshot;\r\n* attribution snapshot;\r\n* ownership declaration;\r\n* current-release transaction;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit;\r\n* immutability;\r\n* deprecation;\r\n* replacement;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* restoration.\r\n\r\n## Authorization and IDOR\r\n\r\n* anonymous;\r\n* authenticated user;\r\n* owner Creator;\r\n* non-owner Creator;\r\n* Crew member;\r\n* unrelated Player;\r\n* moderator;\r\n* administrator;\r\n* suspended user;\r\n* removed profile;\r\n* private lookup;\r\n* unlisted lookup;\r\n* Crew-only lookup;\r\n* release lookup;\r\n* asset lookup.\r\n\r\n## Privacy\r\n\r\n* answers;\r\n* variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* Creator notes;\r\n* finale;\r\n* coordinates;\r\n* route;\r\n* invitation;\r\n* email;\r\n* session;\r\n* participant;\r\n* storage path;\r\n* moderation note;\r\n* audit note;\r\n* outbox payload;\r\n* raw source version;\r\n* raw draft.\r\n\r\n## Storage\r\n\r\n* staging;\r\n* release;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* checksum;\r\n* traversal;\r\n* absolute path;\r\n* separator abuse;\r\n* duplicate key;\r\n* immutable overwrite;\r\n* cross-owner read;\r\n* failed copy;\r\n* mismatch;\r\n* missing object;\r\n* cleanup.\r\n\r\n## Outbox\r\n\r\n* same-transaction creation;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* claim;\r\n* duplicate claim;\r\n* handler success;\r\n* retry;\r\n* terminal failure;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* processed timestamp;\r\n* sanitized payload;\r\n* restart/re-dispatch behavior.\r\n\r\n## Feature flags and rate limits\r\n\r\n* Community disabled;\r\n* Community enabled;\r\n* public publishing disabled;\r\n* non-admin denial;\r\n* administrator development policy;\r\n* suspended denial;\r\n* rate-limit exhaustion;\r\n* retry metadata;\r\n* no UI-only bypass.\r\n\r\n## Lanternwake\r\n\r\n* Community scene family;\r\n* unique names;\r\n* future reachability;\r\n* existing ownership types;\r\n* existing reduced policy;\r\n* no second director;\r\n* no production claim.\r\n\r\n## Migration\r\n\r\n* SQLite empty;\r\n* SQLite existing;\r\n* MySQL empty;\r\n* MySQL existing;\r\n* no data loss;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* canonical database unchanged.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Validation Sequence\r\n\r\nUse this order.\r\n\r\n## Gate 1: Focused static validation\r\n\r\nRun:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nformat check on changed files\r\nfocused lint\r\nTypeScript\r\nPrisma format\r\nPrisma validate\r\nPrisma generate\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Gate 2: Focused unit tests\r\n\r\nRun domain, projection, storage, authorization, outbox, and manifest tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 3: SQLite integration\r\n\r\nRun migrations and complete database-backed services/routes tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 4: MySQL integration\r\n\r\nRun supported migrations and database-backed tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 5: Browser/API integration\r\n\r\nRun the minimal authenticated Phase 1 journeys against an isolated database.\r\n\r\n## Gate 6: Security and privacy\r\n\r\nRun hostile fixture, IDOR, enumeration, mass-assignment, and feature-flag tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 7: Repository validation\r\n\r\nRun the repository-supported equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run assets:validate\r\nnpm run build\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne validation owner controls the final full gate.\r\n\r\nDo not run multiple full validations simultaneously.\r\n\r\nIf `npm run validate` includes the previous commands, record the overlap accurately.\r\n\r\nA skipped test is not a pass.\r\n\r\nA raw migration file existing is not a migration pass.\r\n\r\nA unit test for a pure helper is not an API authorization pass.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Hard-Blocker Policy\r\n\r\nA true external blocker must satisfy all of the following:\r\n\r\n1. It is outside the repository implementation.\r\n2. Repository-supported repair was attempted.\r\n3. Safe alternative environments were attempted.\r\n4. Exact commands and errors are recorded.\r\n5. The blocker affects only named acceptance rows.\r\n6. All unrelated work is complete.\r\n7. No reasonable test double can prove the local behavior.\r\n8. The final report states the phase remains blocked.\r\n\r\nExamples of potentially valid blockers:\r\n\r\n* no accessible isolated MySQL server and no safe local container/runtime capability;\r\n* network policy prevents obtaining a required platform binary after package repair;\r\n* unavailable external production provider required by the original Phase 1 gate.\r\n\r\nThe following are not valid hard blockers:\r\n\r\n* the task is large;\r\n* services are not written yet;\r\n* tests take time;\r\n* documentation is extensive;\r\n* route integration is complicated;\r\n* schema relations may later converge;\r\n* Prisma initially failed before reinstall/regeneration attempts;\r\n* no test fixture currently exists;\r\n* full validation was not attempted;\r\n* one command has an inconvenient path issue.\r\n\r\nDo not stop at a blocker until every other requirement is finished.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Required Closure Commits\r\n\r\nUse coherent commits.\r\n\r\nRecommended sequence:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfeat(harborlight): complete database-backed community services\r\nfeat(harborlight): add community API authorization and projections\r\nfeat(harborlight): couple release outbox and audit transactions\r\ntest(harborlight): prove privacy authorization and storage boundaries\r\nfeat(lanternwake): register Harborlight future scene contracts\r\ndocs(harborlight): complete phase 1 governance and validation\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact count may differ.\r\n\r\nDo not squash all architecture, migration, tests, and documentation into one opaque commit unless repository policy requires it.\r\n\r\nDo not commit:\r\n\r\n* local databases;\r\n* test storage roots;\r\n* credentials;\r\n* environment secrets;\r\n* temporary files;\r\n* Playwright traces containing private fixture data;\r\n* generated production assets unrelated to Phase 1.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Mandatory Final Acceptance Report\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Acceptance_Closure_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe report must include:\r\n\r\n## Repository\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* worktree;\r\n* branch;\r\n* original base;\r\n* initial candidate commit;\r\n* closure starting commit;\r\n* final implementation commit;\r\n* final documentation commit if separate;\r\n* remote branch HEAD;\r\n* local/remote equality;\r\n* canonical checkout preservation.\r\n\r\n## Original prompt reconciliation\r\n\r\n* total mandatory ledger rows;\r\n* passed before closure;\r\n* partial before closure;\r\n* not started before closure;\r\n* passed after closure;\r\n* externally blocked after closure;\r\n* not applicable with justification;\r\n* unresolved count.\r\n\r\nThe unresolved mandatory count must equal zero for acceptance.\r\n\r\n## Models and migrations\r\n\r\n* models;\r\n* fields;\r\n* relations;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* SQLite migration;\r\n* MySQL migration;\r\n* migration tooling result.\r\n\r\n## Services\r\n\r\n* profile;\r\n* listing;\r\n* release;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* projection;\r\n* license;\r\n* attribution;\r\n* storage;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit.\r\n\r\n## Routes or actions\r\n\r\nList every route/action and its authorization.\r\n\r\n## Transaction proof\r\n\r\nProvide evidence that release creation couples:\r\n\r\n* release;\r\n* listing current release;\r\n* status transition;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit.\r\n\r\nProvide rollback evidence.\r\n\r\n## Privacy proof\r\n\r\nProvide exact test names proving exclusion of:\r\n\r\n* answers;\r\n* hidden variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* invitation data;\r\n* email;\r\n* session identifiers;\r\n* private coordinates;\r\n* unpublished source data;\r\n* private participants;\r\n* storage paths;\r\n* moderation notes.\r\n\r\n## IDOR proof\r\n\r\nProvide owner/non-owner/anonymous/Crew/moderator/admin/suspended results.\r\n\r\n## Storage proof\r\n\r\nProvide staging, release, quarantine, checksum, traversal, overwrite, and cross-owner results.\r\n\r\n## Outbox proof\r\n\r\nProvide transaction, claim, retry, idempotency, processed timestamp, and sanitized payload results.\r\n\r\n## Lanternwake proof\r\n\r\nProvide registry and future-contract test evidence.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\nFor every command:\r\n\r\n* exact command;\r\n* environment;\r\n* database;\r\n* server;\r\n* exit code;\r\n* passed;\r\n* failed;\r\n* skipped;\r\n* duration;\r\n* classification.\r\n\r\n## Remaining limitations\r\n\r\nList only later-phase work.\r\n\r\nDo not list unfinished Phase 1 services as a limitation.\r\n\r\n## Final status\r\n\r\nUse exactly one:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nHARBORLIGHT PHASE 1 ACCEPTED\r\n```\r\n\r\nor:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nHARBORLIGHT PHASE 1 BLOCKED\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncandidate\r\nsubstantially complete\r\nfoundation complete\r\nready enough for Phase 2\r\ncomplete except for integration\r\n```\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Final Completion Gate\r\n\r\nDo not declare **Chart the Harbor** complete unless all of the following are true:\r\n\r\n1. Every mandatory original-prompt requirement is in the closure ledger.\r\n2. Every mandatory ledger row is passed or externally blocked with valid evidence.\r\n3. Community Profile is database-backed and functional.\r\n4. Community Listing is database-backed and functional.\r\n5. Community Release is database-backed and immutable.\r\n6. Community Release references an actual immutable published source.\r\n7. Profile mutations create outbox and audit evidence transactionally.\r\n8. Listing mutations create outbox and audit evidence transactionally.\r\n9. Release creation creates release, listing update, outbox, and audit transactionally.\r\n10. Failed release creation rolls back everything.\r\n11. Outbox claiming and retry are implemented.\r\n12. Outbox processing is idempotent.\r\n13. `processedAt` is written only after success.\r\n14. Canonical Community authorization is implemented.\r\n15. Owner and non-owner behavior is tested.\r\n16. Crew-only behavior is implemented and tested.\r\n17. Unlisted behavior is explicit and tested.\r\n18. Suspended behavior is implemented and tested.\r\n19. Central rate limits are integrated.\r\n20. Phase 1 API routes or server actions exist.\r\n21. Strict validation and typed errors exist.\r\n22. Public projections are allowlisted.\r\n23. Raw ORM records are not returned publicly.\r\n24. Hostile privacy fixtures pass.\r\n25. IDOR tests pass.\r\n26. Private enumeration tests pass.\r\n27. Unlisted enumeration tests pass.\r\n28. Real-world private locations cannot leak.\r\n29. Asset registry is integrated with storage.\r\n30. Staging, release, and quarantine behavior is proven.\r\n31. Immutable asset overwrite is blocked.\r\n32. Cross-owner asset access is blocked.\r\n33. Lanternwake Community future contracts exist.\r\n34. No Community scene is falsely production-ready.\r\n35. Prisma tooling is repaired or conclusively externally blocked.\r\n36. SQLite migrations pass through the supported workflow.\r\n37. MySQL migrations pass against an isolated server or remain the sole evidenced external blocker.\r\n38. Production build passes.\r\n39. Full repository validation passes.\r\n40. Documentation is complete and substantive.\r\n41. Integration manifest contains real closure evidence.\r\n42. Canonical checkout remains untouched.\r\n43. Branch is committed and pushed.\r\n44. Local and remote branch state match.\r\n45. Phase 2 has not begun.\r\n\r\nDo not enable unrestricted public publishing.\r\n\r\nDo not begin package import/export.\r\n\r\nDo not begin 3D artifact publishing.\r\n\r\nDo not begin public discovery.\r\n\r\nDo not begin Voyage Logs.\r\n\r\nDo not merge the branch.\r\n\r\nFinish Phase 1 completely, prove it, push it, and stop for acceptance review.\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Workstream D: Implement Immutable Release Service\r\n\r\nCreate the complete database-backed Community Release service.\r\n\r\nRequired operations:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncreateRelease\r\ngetOwnerRelease\r\ngetPublicRelease\r\ndeprecateRelease\r\nsetReplacementRelease\r\nquarantineRelease\r\nrestoreRelease\r\nverifyReleaseIntegrity\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Release-creation transaction\r\n\r\nRelease creation must perform the following in one database transaction:\r\n\r\n1. authenticate actor;\r\n2. authorize listing ownership or approved administrative capability;\r\n3. verify Community Harbor feature flags;\r\n4. apply publication rate limit;\r\n5. load the listing;\r\n6. load the source `PublishedTaleVersion`;\r\n7. prove the source is immutable and belongs to the expected Tale;\r\n8. reject mutable TaleDraft as authoritative source;\r\n9. validate semantic version;\r\n10. reject duplicate listing/version;\r\n11. validate release manifest schema;\r\n12. sanitize public Chronicle metadata through strict allowlist;\r\n13. snapshot spoiler classification;\r\n14. snapshot license;\r\n15. snapshot ordered attribution;\r\n16. snapshot ownership declaration;\r\n17. snapshot compatibility metadata;\r\n18. calculate deterministic manifest checksum;\r\n19. create immutable release;\r\n20. update listing current release where policy permits;\r\n21. update listing publication state through the transition service;\r\n22. create a sanitized outbox record;\r\n23. create a canonical audit record;\r\n24. commit all changes;\r\n25. return a safe receipt.\r\n\r\nIf any step fails:\r\n\r\n* no release remains;\r\n* listing current release remains unchanged;\r\n* listing status remains unchanged;\r\n* no outbox record remains;\r\n* no audit success event remains;\r\n* no partial asset finalization occurs.\r\n\r\n## Immutability\r\n\r\nNo ordinary service or route may update:\r\n\r\n* manifest;\r\n* manifest checksum;\r\n* semantic version;\r\n* source published version;\r\n* license snapshot;\r\n* attribution snapshot;\r\n* spoiler snapshot;\r\n* ownership declaration snapshot;\r\n* published timestamp.\r\n\r\nCorrections require a new release.\r\n\r\nModeration state and deprecation metadata must remain separate from immutable release content.\r\n\r\n## Required tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* successful release transaction;\r\n* invalid source ID;\r\n* mutable source;\r\n* source substitution;\r\n* non-owner;\r\n* duplicate semantic version;\r\n* concurrent version race;\r\n* malformed semantic version;\r\n* invalid manifest;\r\n* deterministic checksum;\r\n* changed input changes checksum;\r\n* stable key ordering;\r\n* transaction rollback;\r\n* no orphan outbox;\r\n* no orphan audit event;\r\n* listing current release update;\r\n* mismatched listing/current release prevention;\r\n* old release unchanged after profile or license defaults change;\r\n* deprecation;\r\n* replacement relationship;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* restoration;\r\n* public projection;\r\n* private-field exclusion.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Workstream E: Complete Transactional Outbox\r\n\r\nThe existing outbox model is not sufficient by itself.\r\n\r\nImplement a functioning outbox foundation.\r\n\r\nRequired operations:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nenqueueOutboxEvent\r\nclaimAvailableEvents\r\nmarkEventProcessed\r\nmarkEventRetryableFailure\r\nmarkEventTerminalFailure\r\nreleaseExpiredClaims\r\ndispatchOutboxBatch\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Requirements\r\n\r\nEach event must include:\r\n\r\n* stable ID;\r\n* typed event name;\r\n* aggregate type;\r\n* aggregate ID;\r\n* schema version;\r\n* sanitized payload;\r\n* idempotency key;\r\n* available time;\r\n* claimed time;\r\n* claim owner;\r\n* attempt count;\r\n* maximum attempts;\r\n* processed time;\r\n* failure code;\r\n* terminal-failure state.\r\n\r\n## Transactional coupling\r\n\r\nProfile, listing, release, ownership, visibility, quarantine, and restoration mutations must insert their required outbox events in the same transaction.\r\n\r\n## Claiming\r\n\r\nUse database-backed claiming.\r\n\r\nDo not rely solely on an in-memory mutex.\r\n\r\nPrevent two workers from processing the same event simultaneously.\r\n\r\nUse the best mechanism supported consistently by the repositoryâ€™s database targets.\r\n\r\nDocument SQLite and MySQL behavior.\r\n\r\n## Processing\r\n\r\n`processedAt` may be set only after the handler succeeds.\r\n\r\nA handler failure must:\r\n\r\n* increment attempts;\r\n* record a safe failure code;\r\n* schedule bounded retry where allowed;\r\n* become terminal after the configured limit;\r\n* preserve the event for inspection.\r\n\r\n## Idempotency\r\n\r\nTest duplicate dispatcher invocation.\r\n\r\nThe side effect must not occur twice.\r\n\r\n## Development dispatcher\r\n\r\nA development dispatcher may run explicitly through:\r\n\r\n* a service;\r\n* a script;\r\n* a test worker;\r\n* an internal route protected by development checks.\r\n\r\nDo not pretend an inline route call is a production durable worker.\r\n\r\nDocument the Phase 4 production-worker seam.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Workstream F: Integrate Canonical Audit Events\r\n\r\nUse the existing canonical audit infrastructure.\r\n\r\nDo not create a disconnected Harborlight-only audit universe unless the existing architecture makes extension impossible and the design record proves it.\r\n\r\nRequired actions include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCOMMUNITY_PROFILE_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_PROFILE_UPDATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_HANDLE_CHANGED\r\nCOMMUNITY_LISTING_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_LISTING_UPDATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_VISIBILITY_CHANGED\r\nCOMMUNITY_PUBLICATION_STATE_CHANGED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_DEPRECATED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_REPLACEMENT_SET\r\nCOMMUNITY_OWNERSHIP_DECLARATION_ACCEPTED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_REGISTERED\r\nCOMMUNITY_ASSET_QUARANTINED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\r\nCOMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\r\nCOMMUNITY_SENSITIVE_ACTION_DENIED\r\n```\r\n\r\nAudit records must contain:\r\n\r\n* actor identity;\r\n* subject type;\r\n* subject ID;\r\n* action;\r\n* timestamp;\r\n* result;\r\n* correlation ID;\r\n* sanitized metadata.\r\n\r\nAudit records must not contain:\r\n\r\n* story prose;\r\n* accepted answers;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* raw Tale snapshot;\r\n* account email;\r\n* credentials;\r\n* session tokens;\r\n* storage filesystem paths;\r\n* private coordinates;\r\n* raw outbox payload where it contains sensitive metadata.\r\n\r\nCreate audit events transactionally for successful sensitive mutations.\r\n\r\nCreate denied-action events according to the existing security policy without creating an abuse vector.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Workstream G: Centralize Community Authorization\r\n\r\nCreate a real Community authorization service.\r\n\r\nDo not rely on a single `canView()` helper passed manually constructed objects.\r\n\r\nRequired capabilities:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncanCreateCommunityProfile\r\ncanEditCommunityProfile\r\ncanCreateCommunityListing\r\ncanEditCommunityListing\r\ncanValidateCommunityListing\r\ncanCreateCommunityRelease\r\ncanPublishCommunityListing\r\ncanViewPublicListing\r\ncanViewUnlistedListing\r\ncanViewPrivateListing\r\ncanViewCrewOnlyListing\r\ncanArchiveListing\r\ncanModerateListing\r\ncanQuarantineRelease\r\ncanRestoreRelease\r\ncanViewModerationData\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Actors\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* anonymous visitor;\r\n* authenticated user;\r\n* Player;\r\n* Creator;\r\n* verified Creator where represented;\r\n* listing owner;\r\n* non-owner Creator;\r\n* Crew member;\r\n* unrelated Player;\r\n* moderator;\r\n* administrator;\r\n* suspended account;\r\n* suspended Community Profile;\r\n* blocked actor where current infrastructure supports blocking;\r\n* removed profile.\r\n\r\n## Crew-only behavior\r\n\r\nImplement actual crew authorization through the canonical current membership relationship.\r\n\r\nDo not treat `CREW_ONLY` as permanently private merely because Voyage Logs are later work.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, the policy must be executable and tested using:\r\n\r\n* relevant Tale Session;\r\n* PlaythroughMembership;\r\n* approved participant relationship;\r\n* or another explicit current canonical membership relation.\r\n\r\n## Unlisted behavior\r\n\r\nDefine one exact unlisted access policy.\r\n\r\nExamples may include:\r\n\r\n* opaque listing slug plus published release;\r\n* unlisted access token;\r\n* explicit share key;\r\n* another non-enumerable direct-link model.\r\n\r\nDo not leave unlisted semantics vague.\r\n\r\nProve:\r\n\r\n* unlisted does not appear in lists;\r\n* direct unlisted access works only according to policy;\r\n* invalid direct access does not reveal whether the listing exists;\r\n* Open Graph does not leak private data.\r\n\r\n## Suspensions\r\n\r\nA suspended Creator or profile must not:\r\n\r\n* create listing;\r\n* update listing;\r\n* create release;\r\n* request publication.\r\n\r\nModerators and administrators require explicit policy, not broad bypasses scattered through routes.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Workstream H: Integrate Central Rate Limits\r\n\r\nUse the current centralized rate-limit abstraction.\r\n\r\nDo not create route-local maps.\r\n\r\nAdd policy identifiers for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncommunity-profile-create\r\ncommunity-profile-update\r\ncommunity-listing-create\r\ncommunity-listing-update\r\ncommunity-listing-validate\r\ncommunity-release-create\r\ncommunity-public-listing-read\r\ncommunity-unlisted-read\r\ncommunity-asset-register\r\ncommunity-moderation-action\r\n```\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* account-based key where authenticated;\r\n* IP/request-context component where appropriate;\r\n* safe anonymous handling;\r\n* server-side enforcement;\r\n* typed rate-limit response;\r\n* `Retry-After` where current conventions support it;\r\n* audit or structured logging where appropriate;\r\n* focused tests.\r\n\r\nDocument that distributed rate limiting remains Phase 4 work if the current implementation is process-local.\r\n\r\nDo not claim distributed protection.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Workstream I: Implement Phase 1 API or Server-Action Foundation\r\n\r\nUse the repositoryâ€™s current conventions.\r\n\r\nImplement the equivalent of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nGET    /api/community/profile/me\r\nPOST   /api/community/profile\r\nPATCH  /api/community/profile/me\r\n\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings\r\nGET    /api/community/listings/:id/private\r\nPATCH  /api/community/listings/:id\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings/:id/validate\r\nPOST   /api/community/listings/:id/releases\r\n\r\nGET    /api/community/listings/:slug/public\r\nGET    /api/community/releases/:id/public\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf server actions are canonical, use server actions and add equivalent integration tests.\r\n\r\n## Route responsibilities\r\n\r\nRoutes must:\r\n\r\n1. establish correlation ID;\r\n2. authenticate;\r\n3. validate CSRF where required;\r\n4. apply centralized rate limit;\r\n5. parse strict input;\r\n6. reject unknown fields;\r\n7. call source relation;\r\n* license snapshot;\r\n* attribution snapshot;\r\n* ownership declaration;\r\n* current-release transaction;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit;\r\n* immutability;\r\n* deprecation;\r\n* replacement;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* restoration.\r\n\r\n## Authorization and IDOR\r\n\r\n* anonymous;\r\n* authenticated user;\r\n* owner Creator;\r\n* non-owner Creator;\r\n* Crew member;\r\n* unrelated Player;\r\n* moderator;\r\n* administrator;\r\n* suspended user;\r\n* removed profile;\r\n* private lookup;\r\n* unlisted lookup;\r\n* Crew-only lookup;\r\n* release lookup;\r\n* asset lookup.\r\n\r\n## Privacy\r\n\r\n* answers;\r\n* variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* Creator notes;\r\n* finale;\r\n* coordinates;\r\n* route;\r\n* invitation;\r\n* email;\r\n* session;\r\n* participant;\r\n* storage path;\r\n* moderation note;\r\n* audit note;\r\n* outbox payload;\r\n* raw source version;\r\n* raw draft.\r\n\r\n## Storage\r\n\r\n* staging;\r\n* release;\r\n* quarantine;\r\n* checksum;\r\n* traversal;\r\n* absolute path;\r\n* separator abuse;\r\n* duplicate key;\r\n* immutable overwrite;\r\n* cross-owner read;\r\n* failed copy;\r\n* mismatch;\r\n* missing object;\r\n* cleanup.\r\n\r\n## Outbox\r\n\r\n* same-transaction creation;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* claim;\r\n* duplicate claim;\r\n* handler success;\r\n* retry;\r\n* terminal failure;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* processed timestamp;\r\n* sanitized payload;\r\n* restart/re-dispatch behavior.\r\n\r\n## Feature flags and rate limits\r\n\r\n* Community disabled;\r\n* Community enabled;\r\n* public publishing disabled;\r\n* non-admin denial;\r\n* administrator development policy;\r\n* suspended denial;\r\n* rate-limit exhaustion;\r\n* retry metadata;\r\n* no UI-only bypass.\r\n\r\n## Lanternwake\r\n\r\n* Community scene family;\r\n* unique names;\r\n* future reachability;\r\n* existing ownership types;\r\n* existing reduced policy;\r\n* no second director;\r\n* no production claim.\r\n\r\n## Migration\r\n\r\n* SQLite empty;\r\n* SQLite existing;\r\n* MySQL empty;\r\n* MySQL existing;\r\n* no data loss;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* canonical database unchanged.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Validation Sequence\r\n\r\nUse this order.\r\n\r\n## Gate 1: Focused static validation\r\n\r\nRun:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nformat check on changed files\r\nfocused lint\r\nTypeScript\r\nPrisma format\r\nPrisma validate\r\nPrisma generate\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Gate 2: Focused unit tests\r\n\r\nRun domain, projection, storage, authorization, outbox, and manifest tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 3: SQLite integration\r\n\r\nRun migrations and complete database-backed services/routes tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 4: MySQL integration\r\n\r\nRun supported migrations and database-backed tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 5: Browser/API integration\r\n\r\nRun the minimal authenticated Phase 1 journeys against an isolated database.\r\n\r\n## Gate 6: Security and privacy\r\n\r\nRun hostile fixture, IDOR, enumeration, mass-assignment, and feature-flag tests.\r\n\r\n## Gate 7: Repository validation\r\n\r\nRun the repository-supported equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run assets:validate\r\nnpm run build\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne validation owner controls the final full gate.\r\n\r\nDo not run multiple full validations simultaneously.\r\n\r\nIf `npm run validate` includes the previous commands, record the overlap accurately.\r\n\r\nA skipped test is not a pass.\r\n\r\nA raw migration file existing is not a migration pass.\r\n\r\nA unit test for a pure helper is not an API authorization pass.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Hard-Blocker Policy\r\n\r\nA true external blocker must satisfy all of the following:\r\n\r\n1. It is outside the repository implementation.\r\n2. Repository-supported repair was attempted.\r\n3. Safe alternative environments were attempted.\r\n4. Exact commands and errors are recorded.\r\n5. The blocker affects only named acceptance rows.\r\n6. All unrelated work is complete.\r\n7. No reasonable test double can prove the local behavior.\r\n8. The final report states the phase remains blocked.\r\n\r\nExamples of potentially valid blockers:\r\n\r\n* no accessible isolated MySQL server and no safe local container/runtime capability;\r\n* network policy prevents obtaining a required platform binary after package repair;\r\n* unavailable external production provider required by the original Phase 1 gate.\r\n\r\nThe following are not valid hard blockers:\r\n\r\n* the task is large;\r\n* services are not written yet;\r\n* tests take time;\r\n* documentation is extensive;\r\n* route integration is complicated;\r\n* schema relations may later converge;\r\n* Prisma initially failed before reinstall/regeneration attempts;\r\n* no test fixture currently exists;\r\n* full validation was not attempted;\r\n* one command has an inconvenient path issue.\r\n\r\nDo not stop at a blocker until every other requirement is finished.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Required Closure Commits\r\n\r\nUse coherent commits.\r\n\r\nRecommended sequence:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfeat(harborlight): complete database-backed community services\r\nfeat(harborlight): add community API authorization and projections\r\nfeat(harborlight): couple release outbox and audit transactions\r\ntest(harborlight): prove privacy authorization and storage boundaries\r\nfeat(lanternwake): register Harborlight future scene contracts\r\ndocs(harborlight): complete phase 1 governance and validation\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact count may differ.\r\n\r\nDo not squash all architecture, migration, tests, and documentation into one opaque commit unless repository policy requires it.\r\n\r\nDo not commit:\r\n\r\n* local databases;\r\n* test storage roots;\r\n* credentials;\r\n* environment secrets;\r\n* temporary files;\r\n* Playwright traces containing private fixture data;\r\n* generated production assets unrelated to Phase 1.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Mandatory Final Acceptance Report\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Acceptance_Closure_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe report must include:\r\n\r\n## Repository\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* worktree;\r\n* branch;\r\n* original base;\r\n* initial candidate commit;\r\n* closure starting commit;\r\n* final implementation commit;\r\n* final documentation commit if separate;\r\n* remote branch HEAD;\r\n* local/remote equality;\r\n* canonical checkout preservation.\r\n\r\n## Original prompt reconciliation\r\n\r\n* total mandatory ledger rows;\r\n* passed before closure;\r\n* partial before closure;\r\n* not started before closure;\r\n* passed after closure;\r\n* externally blocked after closure;\r\n* not applicable with justification;\r\n* unresolved count.\r\n\r\nThe unresolved mandatory count must equal zero for acceptance.\r\n\r\n## Models and migrations\r\n\r\n* models;\r\n* fields;\r\n* relations;\r\n* constraints;\r\n* indexes;\r\n* SQLite migration;\r\n* MySQL migration;\r\n* migration tooling result.\r\n\r\n## Services\r\n\r\n* profile;\r\n* listing;\r\n* release;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* projection;\r\n* license;\r\n* attribution;\r\n* storage;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit.\r\n\r\n## Routes or actions\r\n\r\nList every route/action and its authorization.\r\n\r\n## Transaction proof\r\n\r\nProvide evidence that release creation couples:\r\n\r\n* release;\r\n* listing current release;\r\n* status transition;\r\n* outbox;\r\n* audit.\r\n\r\nProvide rollback evidence.\r\n\r\n## Privacy proof\r\n\r\nProvide exact test names proving exclusion of:\r\n\r\n* answers;\r\n* hidden variables;\r\n* Captain notes;\r\n* invitation data;\r\n* email;\r\n* session identifiers;\r\n* private coordinates;\r\n* unpublished source data;\r\n* private participants;\r\n* storage paths;\r\n* moderation notes.\r\n\r\n## IDOR proof\r\n\r\nProvide owner/non-owner/anonymous/Crew/moderator/admin/suspended results.\r\n\r\n## Storage proof\r\n\r\nProvide staging, release, quarantine, checksum, traversal, overwrite, and cross-owner results.\r\n\r\n## Outbox proof\r\n\r\nProvide transaction, claim, retry, idempotency, processed timestamp, and sanitized payload results.\r\n\r\n## Lanternwake proof\r\n\r\nProvide registry and future-contract test evidence.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\nFor every command:\r\n\r\n* exact command;\r\n* environment;\r\n* database;\r\n* server;\r\n* exit code;\r\n* passed;\r\n* failed;\r\n* skipped;\r\n* duration;\r\n* classification.\r\n\r\n## Remaining limitations\r\n\r\nList only later-phase work.\r\n\r\nDo not list unfinished Phase 1 services as a limitation.\r\n\r\n## Final status\r\n\r\nUse exactly one:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nHARBORLIGHT PHASE 1 ACCEPTED\r\n```\r\n\r\nor:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nHARBORLIGHT PHASE 1 BLOCKED\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncandidate\r\nsubstantially complete\r\nfoundation complete\r\nready enough for Final Completion Gate\r\n\r\nDo not declare **Chart the Harbor** complete unless all of the following are true:\r\n\r\n1. Every mandatory original-prompt requirement is in the closure ledger.\r\n2. Community Listing is database-backed and functional.\r\n5. Community Release is database-backed and immutable.\r\n6. Community Release references an actual immutable published source.\r\n7. Profile mutations create outbox and audit evidence transactionally.\r\n8. Listing mutations create outbox and audit evidence transactionally.\r\n9. Release creation creates release, listing update, outbox, and audit transactionally.\r\n10. Failed release creation rolls back everything.\r\n11. Outbox claiming and retry are implemented.\r\n12. [{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.5 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.4 seconds\nOutput:\n# Project Harborlight Phase 1 - Design Record\n\n## Baseline and ownership\n\n- Branch: `codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor`\n- Base and latest accepted integrated `origin/main`: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n- Database owner: Harborlight Phase 1. This work does not merge One Voyage (`4e8f385`), Wayfarer (`a478d79`), or Sealed Hold (`4b93b4f`).\n\n## Repository inventory and boundaries\n\n`TallTale` is the canonical authored Chronicle; `TaleDraft` is mutable authoring state; `PublishedTaleVersion` is immutable source content; `TaleSession` is a live voyage; old `Campaign` tables remain production compatibility surfaces. `PlayerProfile` is the currently available canonical person/profile; `PlatformAuditEvent`, `TaleAssetStorage`, `consumeRateLimit`, and the Lanternwake scene registry are existing seams. Harborlight creates no alternate Chronicle, chapter, session, or login identity.\n\n`CommunityListing` is mutable catalog identity and release history. `CommunityRelease` is immutable distribution identity, bound by scalar source ID to an immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`; its manifest, checksum, attribution, license, and spoiler snapshots never change. A later correction creates a new release; listing quarantine/removal controls delivery without rewriting the release.\n\n## Convergence seams\n\n`CommunityProfile.identityKey` plus optional `playerProfileId` represents current Player Profile identity without copying credentials, email, sessions, or Player-only state. During Wayfarer convergence, the adapter maps `identityKey` to `UserAccount` and migrates only the optional compatibility pointer. Chronicle references are source-version IDs, keeping One Voyage terminology/domain ownership isolated. Sealed Hold remains the private encrypted transport authority; Harborlight owns community staging, immutable public/unlisted releases, checksums, and future distribution only.\n\n## Frozen policy\n\nTyped taxonomy has 14 item types with declared release/install/remix/search capabilities. Publication transitions are centralized; visibility is independent; public delivery allows only published COMMUNITY/FEATURED records. Unlisted and private records are never enumerable. Spoilers and private real-world locations are enforced at projection time, not CSS. Public projections are strict allowlists and exclude answers, hidden variables, Captain notes, invitation/session data, private coordinates, email, storage paths, raw drafts, and moderation notes.\n\nRelease manifests use schema version 1, canonical JSON SHA-256, semantic versions, required immutable source IDs, license snapshot, and ordered attribution snapshot. License policies support reserved, attribution, remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom keys. Ownership declarations are auditable. Releases retain attribution through profile deletion/deactivation.\n\nCommunity assets use explicit scan/processing states; `SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED` is never clean. The local provider separates `staging`, `releases`, and `quarantine`, rejects traversal/absolute/separator tricks, and refuses immutable overwrite. A typed outbox model carries sanitized schema-versioned payloads and idempotency; a future durable dispatcher must set `processedAt` only after success.\n\nCommunity scenes remain future contracts under the existing Lanternwake Director and inherited reduced-motion/cleanup policies; no scene is production-ready in Phase 1.\n\n## Migration and rollback\n\nSchema changes are additive and use new, unique SQLite/MySQL migration identifiers. No legacy table is renamed, deleted, or repurposed. Rollback is application rollback plus restore of a matched database/asset backup; no destructive down migration is supplied. Phase 2 must add public publishing/review workflow, Wayfarer account relation, durable dispatcher/object storage, and combined convergence tests.\n\r\n# Harborlight Phase 1 Integration Manifest\n\n## Candidate\n\n- Branch: `codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor`\n- Base/latest `origin/main`: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n- Ending commit: recorded after final validation and push.\n\n## Additive surface\n\nModels: CommunityProfile, CommunityListing, CommunityRelease, CommunityReleaseAttribution, CommunityLicensePolicy, CommunityOwnershipDeclaration, CommunityAssetReference, CommunityOutboxEvent. Unique constraints: normalized handle, listing slug, listing/version, storage key, owner/checksum, outbox idempotency. Indexes cover profile moderation/visibility, listing ownership/state, release source/version, attribution order, asset release/visibility, and outbox dispatch.\n\nSQLite migration: `20260721140000_project_harborlight_phase1`; MySQL migration: `0006_project_harborlight_phase1`. Environment: `COMMUNITY_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_MODERATION_REQUIRED`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_STORAGE_PROVIDER`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_ROOT`, `COMMUNITY_WORKER_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_RATE_LIMIT_PROFILE`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_BASE_URL`.\n\n## Convergence risks and order\n\nOne Voyage owns canonical Chronicle terminology/domain consolidation; merge it first, then retarget scalar source seams. Wayfarer owns `UserAccount`; next migrate `identityKey` adapter without copying identity secrets. Sealed Hold remains private encrypted package transport and should reconcile only shared checksum/storage utilities. Then run schema migration, public-projection hostile fixtures, IDOR/visibility tests, exact-version release tests, and storage/outbox integration tests on both SQLite and MySQL.\n\nDeferred destructive work: no legacy migration/deletion, no auth replacement, no package import/export, no public upload/publishing, no production object storage/worker. Retained compatibility: Campaign, PlayerProfile, TallTale, and current Lanternwake registry.\n\r\n# Community Harbor data model\n\nThe eight additive models are catalog/profile, release, attribution/license/declaration, asset, and outbox foundations. `CommunityRelease` points to the immutable `PublishedTaleVersion` by ID and never to a mutable draft. JSON columns hold only versioned snapshots or bounded arrays; item taxonomy and release manifests are Zod-validated TypeScript contracts rather than an unbounded type-specific payload.\n\r\n# Community Harbor privacy and spoiler model\n\nPublic projections are allowlists. They exclude raw source snapshots, answers, variables, Captain notes, finale/chapter spoiler fields, invitations, sessions, participant identity, email, storage keys, moderation notes, and exact private locations. Private/unlisted records are non-enumerable; private real-world locations have no public projection.\n\r\n# Community Harbor licensing and attribution\n\nEvery release snapshots a policy key/version and ordered attribution. Policies cover all-rights-reserved, attribution use/remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom terms. Ownership declarations record accepted statements and evidence references; they are declarations, not legal verification.\n\r\n# Community Harbor release manifest\n\nManifest schema 1 requires listing ID, semantic version, immutable source `PublishedTaleVersion` ID, public metadata, license snapshot, and ordered attribution. Canonical key-sorted JSON is SHA-256 checksummed. A release is immutable; deprecation/replacement changes delivery metadata only.\n\r\n# Community Harbor storage and job architecture\n\nThe local adapter isolates `staging`, immutable `releases`, and `quarantine`, with path safety and immutable-write protection. It is a development adapter only. CommunityOutboxEvent is the transactional dispatch record with sanitized payload, idempotency, availability, claim, attempts, processed time, and terminal failure. No production object store or worker is enabled.\n\r\n# Community Harbor Phase 1 test plan\n\nFocused tests cover taxonomy, handles, publication transitions, server flags, checksum determinism, hostile projection leakage, private-location denial, Chronicle metadata sanitization, namespace separation, traversal rejection, and immutable overwrite rejection. Required convergence tests add Prisma migration on isolated SQLite/MySQL databases, source-version transaction/outbox/audit behavior, authorization actors, and public enumeration behavior.\n\r\nMISSING SECURITY.md\r\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\n\nconst globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as { prisma?: PrismaClient };\nexport const db =\n  globalForPrisma.prisma ??\n  new PrismaClient({ log: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"development\" ? [\"warn\", \"error\"] : [\"error\"] });\nif (process.env.NODE_ENV !== \"production\") globalForPrisma.prisma = db;\n\r\nimport { createHash } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { eventBus } from \"@/lib/events\";\nimport { getStudioTale } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\nimport type { PublishedTaleSnapshot } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\nimport { validateTaleDraft } from \"@/tall-tale/validation\";\nimport { logger } from \"@/lib/logger\";\n\nexport class PublishValidationError extends Error {\n  constructor(public readonly validation: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof validateTaleDraft>>) {\n    super(\"This Chronicle cannot be published yet. Resolve the blocking validation issues, then publish again.\");\n  }\n}\n\nexport function snapshotFromStudio(studio: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getStudioTale>>): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\n  return {\n    schemaVersion: 1,\n    tale: {\n      id: studio.tale.id,\n      slug: studio.tale.slug,\n      title: studio.tale.title,\n      subtitle: studio.tale.subtitle,\n      shortDescription: studio.tale.shortDescription,\n      longDescription: studio.tale.longDescription,\n      coverAssetId: studio.tale.coverAssetId,\n      theme: studio.tale.theme,\n      visibility: studio.tale.visibility,\n      playerCountMin: studio.tale.playerCountMin,\n      playerCountMax: studio.tale.playerCountMax,\n      estimatedDuration: studio.tale.estimatedDuration,\n      contentWarnings: studio.tale.contentWarnings,\n    },\n    chapters: studio.draft.chapters.map((chapter, chapterIndex) => ({\n      id: chapter.id,\n      title: chapter.title,\n      subtitle: chapter.subtitle,\n      description: chapter.description,\n      coverAssetId: chapter.coverAssetId,\n      estimatedDuration: chapter.estimatedDuration,\n      isOptional: chapter.isOptional,\n      metadata: chapter.metadata,\n      orderIndex: chapterIndex,\n      entryBlockId: chapter.blocks[0]?.id ?? null,\n      completionBlockId:\n        [...chapter.blocks]\n          .reverse()\n          .find((block) => block.blockType === \"chapterComplete\" || block.blockType === \"taleComplete\")?.id ??\n        chapter.blocks.at(-1)?.id ??\n        null,\n      blocks: chapter.blocks.map((block, blockIndex) => ({\n        id: block.id,\n        chapterId: chapter.id,\n        blockType: block.blockType,\n        title: block.title,\n        internalLabel: block.internalLabel,\n        configuration: block.configuration,\n        presentation: block.presentation,\n        completion: block.completion,\n        creatorNotes: null,\n        isEnabled: block.isEnabled,\n        schemaVersion: block.schemaVersion,\n        orderIndex: blockIndex,\n        nextBlockId: block.connections[0]?.targetBlockId ?? null,\n        connections: block.connections.map((connection, connectionIndex) => ({\n          ...connection,\n          orderIndex: connectionIndex,\n        })),\n      })),\n    })),\n    assets: studio.assets.map((asset) => ({\n      id: asset.id,\n      mediaType: asset.mediaType,\n      displayName: asset.displayName,\n      description: asset.description,\n      mimeType: asset.mimeType,\n      width: asset.width,\n      height: asset.height,\n      roles: asset.roles,\n      variants: asset.variants.map((variant) => ({\n        id: variant.id,\n        role: variant.role,\n        mimeType: variant.mimeType,\n        processingState: variant.processingState,\n      })),\n    })) as PublishedTaleSnapshot[\"assets\"],\n    locations: studio.locations.map((location) => ({ ...location, captainNotes: undefined })),\n    artifacts: studio.artifacts,\n    publishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function publishTale(\n  taleId: string,\n  publisherId: string,\n  releaseNotes: string,\n  expectedAutosaveVersion?: number,\n) {\n  logger.info({ area: \"tall-tale-publish\", taleId, publisherId }, \"Chronicle publish validation started\");\n  const validation = await validateTaleDraft(taleId);\n  if (!validation.valid) throw new PublishValidationError(validation);\n  if (expectedAutosaveVersion !== undefined && validation.autosaveVersion !== expectedAutosaveVersion)\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle changed before publishing. Review the latest saved draft, then try again.\");\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\n  if (studio.draft.autosaveVersion !== validation.autosaveVersion)\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle changed during validation. Review the current draft, then publish again.\");\n  const snapshot = snapshotFromStudio(studio);\n  const contentSnapshot = JSON.stringify(snapshot);\n  const checksum = createHash(\"sha256\").update(contentSnapshot).digest(\"hex\");\n  const version = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const latest = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.findFirst({ where: { taleId }, orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" } });\n    const versionNumber = (latest?.versionNumber ?? 0) + 1;\n    const structuredReleaseNotes =\n      releaseNotes.trim() ||\n      `Published ${snapshot.chapters.length} Chapters, ${snapshot.chapters.reduce((count, chapter) => count + chapter.blocks.length, 0)} Passages, and ${snapshot.assets.length} assets${latest ? ` from Version ${latest.versionLabel}` : \" as the first published Version\"}.`;\n    await tx.publishedTaleVersion.updateMany({ where: { taleId, isCurrent: true }, data: { isCurrent: false } });\n    const created = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.create({\n      data: {\n        taleId,\n        versionNumber,\n        versionLabel: versionNumber === 1 ? \"1.0\" : `1.${versionNumber - 1}`,\n        publishedBy: publisherId,\n        releaseNotes: structuredReleaseNotes,\n        contentSnapshot,\n        checksum,\n        isCurrent: true,\n      },\n    });\n    await tx.tallTale.update({\n      where: { id: taleId },\n      data: { status: \"PUBLISHED\", latestPublishedVersionId: created.id },\n    });\n    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\n      data: {\n        actorType: \"CREATOR\",\n        actorId: publisherId,\n        action: \"TALE_VERSION_PUBLISHED\",\n        resourceType: \"PUBLISHED_TALE_VERSION\",\n        resourceId: created.id,\n        correlationId: crypto.randomUUID(),\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ taleId, versionLabel: created.versionLabel, checksum: created.checksum }),\n      },\n    });\n    return created;\n  });\n  eventBus.emit(\"tall-tale:catalog\", {\n    type: \"catalog.updated\",\n    taleId,\n    versionId: version.id,\n    at: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\n  });\n  logger.info(\n    { area: \"tall-tale-publish\", taleId, versionId: version.id, versionLabel: version.versionLabel },\n    \"Immutable Chronicle version published\",\n  );\n  return {\n    id: version.id,\n    versionNumber: version.versionNumber,\n    versionLabel: version.versionLabel,\n    checksum: version.checksum,\n    publishedAt: version.publishedAt.toISOString(),\n  };\n}\n\nexport function parsePublishedSnapshot(raw: string): PublishedTaleSnapshot {\n  const snapshot = JSON.parse(raw) as PublishedTaleSnapshot;\n  if (snapshot.schemaVersion !== 1 || !Array.isArray(snapshot.chapters))\n    throw new Error(\"This Chronicle version uses an unsupported format. Update Voyagewright, then try again.\");\n  return snapshot;\n}\n\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { DraftConflictError } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\nimport { PublishValidationError } from \"@/tall-tale/publishing\";\nimport { VerificationRejectedError } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\nimport { logger } from \"@/lib/logger\";\nimport { InvitationUnavailableError } from \"@/platform/invitations\";\n\nexport function apiError(cause: unknown) {\n  logger.warn(\n    {\n      area: \"tall-tale-api\",\n      errorType: cause instanceof Error ? cause.constructor.name : typeof cause,\n      errorCode:\n        cause instanceof DraftConflictError\n          ? \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\"\n          : cause instanceof PublishValidationError\n            ? \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n            : cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError\n              ? cause.reason\n              : cause instanceof InvitationUnavailableError\n                ? cause.code\n                : \"REQUEST_FAILED\",\n    },\n    \"Voyagewright request rejected\",\n  );\n  if (cause instanceof DraftConflictError)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\", currentVersion: cause.currentVersion },\n      { status: 409 },\n    );\n  if (cause instanceof PublishValidationError)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"VALIDATION_FAILED\", validation: cause.validation },\n      { status: 422 },\n    );\n  if (cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError)\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: cause.message, code: cause.reason }, { status: 409 });\n  if (cause instanceof InvitationUnavailableError) {\n    const status =\n      cause.code === \"ACCOUNT_REQUIRED\" ? 401 : cause.code === \"CONFLICT\" ? 409 : cause.code === \"INVALID\" ? 400 : 410;\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: cause.message, code: cause.code }, { status });\n  }\n  const message = cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"The request could not be completed.\";\n  if (message.includes(\"Unique constraint\"))\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"That name or address is already in use.\" }, { status: 409 });\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

csrfToken;\n}\n\nexport async function readPendingInvitationToken() {\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (!value) return null;\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(0, separator) : null;\n}\n\nexport async function readPendingInvitationCsrf() {\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (!value) return null;\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(separator + 1) : null;\n}\n\nexport at `c1daa766ec8fe6dba9f0848c2315e6de14e2e1c0`. The first candidate correctly established additive types, migrations, storage primitives, and focused helper tests, but incorrectly treated those as acceptance evidence for database-backed services, routes, transactions, authorization, audit/outbox behavior, Lanternwake contracts, and integration validation.\n+\n+The omissions were technical rather than external: Prisma relationships were provisional scalars; no Community service owned transactions; no route authenticated a Community actor; no database-backed hostile privacy/IDOR fixture existed; the outbox model had no dispatcher; and documentation did not map requirements to evidence. This closure pass completes those repository-local gaps while retaining the original base and without merging concurrent branches.\n+\n+The ledger is the controlling reconciliation artifact. A row cannot be marked PASS until its implementation, tests, and validation evidence are present. Any unavailable isolated MySQL environment will be recorded only against its MySQL rows; it cannot excuse unrelated work.\n*** End Patch";

JSON.stringify(manifest.attribution), spoilerSnapshot: JSON.stringify({ level: manifest.publicMetadata.spoilerLevel }), publishedByProfileId: profile.id } });\n+    await tx.communityListing.update({ where: { id: listingId }, data: { currentReleaseId: release.id, publicationStatus: \"UPDATE_PENDING\" } });\n+    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", release.id, { listingId, semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion, checksum: release.manifestChecksum });\n+    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", release.id, { listingId, semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion });\n+    return ownerReleaseProjection(release);\n+  });\n+}\n+export async function getPublicRelease(id: string) {\n+  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id, moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\", listing: { publicationStatus: \"PUBLISHED\", visibility: { in: [\"COMMUNITY\", \"FEATURED\"] }, moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\" } }, include: { listing: { include: { owner: true } } } });\n+  return release ? publicReleaseProjection(release) : null;\n+}\n+\n+export function publicProfileProjection(profile: { handle: string; displayName: string; biography: string | null; verificationStatus: string }) { return { handle: profile.handle, displayName: profile.displayName, biography: profile.biography, verificationStatus: profile.verificationStatus }; }\n+export function ownerProfileProjection(profile: { id: string; handle: string; displayName: string; biography: string | null; visibility: string; supportedLanguages: string; creatorStatus: string; verificationStatus: string }) { return { id: profile.id, handle: profile.handle, displayName: profile.displayName, biography: profile.biography, visibility: profile.visibility, supportedLanguages: JSON.parse(profile.supportedLanguages), creatorStatus: profile.creatorStatus, verificationStatus: profile.verificationStatus }; }\n+export function ownerListingProjection(listing: { id: string; slug: string; itemType: string; title: string; publicationStatus: string; visibility: string; spoilerLevel: string; locationClass: string; currentReleaseId: string | null }) { return { id: listing.id, slug: listing.slug, itemType: listing.itemType, title: listing.title, publicationStatus: listing.publicationStatus, visibility: listing.visibility, spoilerLevel: listing.spoilerLevel, locationClass: listing.locationClass, currentReleaseId: listing.currentReleaseId }; }\n+export function publicListingProjection(listing: { slug: string; itemType: string; title: string; shortDescription: string | null; tags: string; spoilerLevel: string; locationClass: string; owner: { handle: string; displayName: string } }) { return { slug: listing.slug, itemType: listing.itemType, title: listing.title, shortDescription: listing.shortDescription, tags: JSON.parse(listing.tags), spoilerLevel: listing.spoilerLevel === \"NONE\" ? \"NONE\" : \"PREVIEW_SAFE\", locationClass: listing.locationClass, creator: { handle: listing.owner.handle, displayName: listing.owner.displayName } }; }\n+export function ownerReleaseProjection(release: { id: string; listingId: string; semanticVersion: string; manifestChecksum: string; sourcePublishedTaleVersionId: string | null; moderationStatus: string }) { return { id: release.id, listingId: release.listingId, semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion, manifestChecksum: release.manifestChecksum, sourcePublishedTaleVersionId: release.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId, moderationStatus: release.moderationStatus }; }\n+export function publicReleaseProjection(release: { id: string; semanticVersion: string; manifestSchemaVersion: number; minimumPlatformVersion: string | null; releaseNotes: string | null; manifestChecksum: string; licenseSnapshot: string; attributionSnapshot: string }) { return { id: release.id, semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion, manifestSchemaVersion: release.manifestSchemaVersion, minimumPlatformVersion: release.minimumPlatformVersion, releaseNotes: release.releaseNotes, manifestChecksum: release.manifestChecksum, license: (event: { id: string; eventType: string; aggregateType: string; aggregateId: string; payload: string }) => Promise<void>;\n+\n+export async function claimAvailableEvents(workerId: string, limit = 25, leaseMs = 30_000) {\n+  const now = new Date();\n+  const candidates = await db.communityOutboxEvent.findMany({\n+    where: { processedAt: null, terminalFailureAt: null, availableAt: { lte: now }, OR: [{ claimExpiresAt: null }, { claimExpiresAt: { lt: now } }] },\n+    orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" }, take: limit, select: { id: true },\n+  });\n+  const claimed: string[] = [];\n+  for (const candidate of candidates) {\n+    const result = await db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({ where: { id: candidate.id, processedAt: null, terminalFailureAt: null, OR: [{ claimExpiresAt: null }, { claimExpiresAt: { lt: now } }] }, data: { claimedAt: now, claimOwner: workerId, claimExpiresAt: new Date(now.getTime() + leaseMs) } });\n+    if (result.count) claimed.push(candidate.id);\n+  }\n+  return db.communityOutboxEvent.findMany({ where: { id: { in: claimed } }, orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" } });\n+}\n+export async function markEventProcessed(id: string, workerId: string) {\n+  return db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({ where: { id, claimOwner: workerId, processedAt: null }, data: { processedAt: new Date(), claimExpiresAt: null } });\n+}\n+export async function markEventRetryableFailure(id: string, workerId: string, failureCode: string) {\n+  const event = await db.communityOutboxEvent.findFirst({ where: { id, claimOwner: workerId, processedAt: null } });\n+  if (!event) return false;\n+  const attempts = event.attemptCount + 1;\n+  await db.communityOutboxEvent.update({ where: { id }, data: attempts >= event.maxAttempts ? { attemptCount: attempts, terminalFailureAt: new Date(), failureCode, claimExpiresAt: null } : { attemptCount: attempts, failureCode, claimOwner: null, claimedAt: null, claimExpiresAt: null, availableAt: new Date(Date.now() + attempts * 1_000) } });\n+  return true;\n+}\n+export async function releaseExpiredClaims() { return db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({ where: { processedAt: null, terminalFailureAt: null, claimExpiresAt: { lt: new Date() } }, data: { claimOwner: null, claimedAt: null, claimExpiresAt: null } }); }\n+export async function dispatchOutboxBatch(workerId: string, handler: OutboxHandler, limit = 25) {\n+  const events = await claimAvailableEvents(workerId, limit); let processed = 0;\n+  for (const event of events) { try { await handler(event); if ((await markEventProcessed(event.id, workerId)).count) processed += 1; } catch { await markEventRetryableFailure(event.id, workerId, \"HANDLER_FAILED\"); } }\n+  return { claimed: events.length, processed };\n+}\n*** End Patch";

await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } }) : null;\n+  if (profile?.id === listing.ownerProfileId || actor?.role === \"MODERATOR\" || actor?.role === \"ADMIN\") return true;\n+  if (listing.visibility === \"COMMUNITY\" || listing.visibility === \"FEATURED\") return true;\n+  if (listing.visibility === \"UNLISTED\") return Boolean(unlistedSlug && unlistedSlug === listing.slug);\n+  if (listing.visibility !== \"CREW_ONLY\" || !actor || !listing.currentRelease?.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId) return false;\n+  const source = await db.publishedTaleVersion.findUnique({ where: { id: listing.currentRelease.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId }, select: { taleId: true } });\n+  return Boolean(source && (await db.playthroughMembership.findFirst({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId, status: { in: [\"INVITED\", \"ACCEPTED\", \"READY\", \"ACTIVE_MEMBER\", \"COMPLETED_MEMBER\"] }, playthrough: { taleId: source.taleId } }, select: { id: true } })));\n+}\n+export async function requireListingOwner(actor: CommunityActor, listingId: string) {\n+  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\n+  const allowed = profile && (await db.communityListing.findFirst({ where: { id: listingId, ownerProfileId: profile.id }, select: { id: true } }));\n+  if (!allowed) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot access this listing.\");\n+  return profile;\n+}\n*** End Patch";

return ownerReleaseProjection(release);\n+}\n+export async function deprecateRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string, replacementReleaseId?: string) {\n+  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\n+  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id, listing: { ownerProfileId: profile.id } } });\n+  if (!release) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot deprecate this release.\");\n+  if (replacementReleaseId) {\n+    const replacement = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id: replacementReleaseId, listingId: release.listingId } });\n+    if (!replacement) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_NOT_FOUND\", \"Replacement must belong to the same listing.\");\n+  }\n+  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({ where: { id }, data: { deprecatedAt: new Date(), replacementReleaseId: replacementReleaseId ?? null } });\n+    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_DEPRECATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, { replacementReleaseId });\n+    await audit(tx, actor, replacementReleaseId ? \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_REPLACEMENT_SET\" : \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_DEPRECATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, { replacementReleaseId });\n+    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\n+  });\n+}\n+export async function quarantineRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\n+  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\") throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\n+  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({ where: { id }, data: { moderationStatus: \"QUARANTINED\" } });\n+    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\n+    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\n+    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\n+  });\n+}\n+export async function restoreRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\n+  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\") throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\n+  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({ where: { id }, data: { moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n+    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\n+    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\n+    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\n+  });\n+}\n+export async function verifyReleaseIntegrity(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\n+  const release = await getOwnerRelease(actor, id);\n+  const stored = await db.communityRelease.findUnique({ where: { id } });\n+  if (!stored) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_NOT_FOUND\", \"Release not found.\");\n+  const manifest = releaseManifestSchema.parse(JSON.parse(stored.manifest));\n+  return { ...release, valid: manifestChecksum(manifest) === stored.manifestChecksum };\n+}\n*** End Patch";

function registerStagedAsset(actor: CommunityActor, storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider, input: { name: string; bytes: Uint8Array; declaredMimeType: string; detectedMimeType?: string }) {\n+  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\n+  if (!profile || profile.moderationStatus !== \"ACTIVE\") throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"An active Community Profile is required.\");\n+  const stored = await storage.putStagedObject(profile.id, input.name, input.bytes);\n+  return db.communityAssetReference.create({ data: { ownerProfileId: profile.id, checksum: stored.checksum, declaredMimeType: input.declaredMimeType, detectedMimeType: input.detectedMimeType ?? null, fileSize: input.bytes.byteLength, storageProvider: \"local\", storageKey: stored.key, scanStatus: \"SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED\", processingStatus: \"UPLOADED\" } });\n+}\n+export async function attachAssetToRelease(actor: CommunityActor, storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider, assetId: string, releaseId: string) {\n+  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\n+  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findFirst({ where: { id: assetId, ownerProfileId: profile?.id } });\n+  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id: releaseId, listing: { ownerProfileId: profile?.id } } });\n+  if (!asset || !release) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot attach this asset.\");\n+  const copied = await storage.copyToImmutableRelease(asset.storageKey, releaseId);\n+  if (copied.checksum !== asset.checksum) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_STORAGE_ERROR\", \"Asset checksum changed during immutable copy.\");\n+  return db.communityAssetReference.update({ where: { id: asset.id }, data: { releaseId, storageKey: copied.key, processingStatus: \"READY\" } });\n+}\n+export async function readAuthorizedCommunityAsset(actor: CommunityActor, storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider, assetId: string) {\n+  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\n+  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findFirst({ where: { id: assetId, ownerProfileId: profile?.id, removedAt: null } });\n+  if (!asset) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Asset not found.\");\n+  return storage.readObject(asset.storageKey);\n+}\n+export async function quarantineAsset(actor: CommunityActor, storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider, assetId: string) {\n+  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\") throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\n+  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findUnique({ where: { id: assetId } });\n+  if (!asset) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_STORAGE_ERROR\", \"Asset not found.\");\n+  const moved = await storage.moveToQuarantine(asset.storageKey);\n+  return db.communityAssetReference.update({ where: { id: asset.id }, data: { storageKey: moved.key, scanStatus: \"SUSPICIOUS\", processingStatus: \"QUARANTINED\", visibility: \"PRIVATE\" } });\n+}\n*** End
