const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Data_Model.md\n@@\n-# Community Harbor data model\n-\n-The eight additive models are catalog/profile, release, attribution/license/declaration, asset, and outbox foundations. `CommunityRelease` points to the immutable `PublishedTaleVersion` by ID and never to a mutable draft. JSON columns hold only versioned snapshots or bounded arrays; item taxonomy and release manifests are Zod-validated TypeScript contracts rather than an unbounded type-specific payload.\n+# Community Harbor data model\n+\n+## Authority and relationships\n+\n+Harborlight is a catalog and distribution domain, not a second Chronicle runtime. `TallTale` remains authored Chronicle identity, `TaleDraft` is mutable Studio state, `PublishedTaleVersion` is the immutable source, and `TaleSession` remains a live voyage. `CommunityProfile` has an optional one-to-one foreign key to the currently canonical `PlayerProfile`; `identityKey` is retained only as a Wayfarer migration seam. Credentials, session data, email, and invitation state are not copied.\n+\n+`CommunityProfile 1--* CommunityListing 1--* CommunityRelease`; a listing optionally points at its current release, while every release retains its listing history. A release optionally references one `PublishedTaleVersion`, is published by a profile, has ordered attributions, optional ownership declarations, and optional immutable asset references. Assets belong to a profile and may attach to one release. The outbox is independent so it can preserve aggregate events even when a business relationship is later removed.\n+\n+## Models, constraints, and retention\n+\n+Profiles have unique `playerProfileId`, `identityKey`, and normalized handle. Listings have globally unique slug and indexed owner/state/visibility. Releases have unique `(listingId, semanticVersion)`, immutable manifest/checksum/source/license/attribution/spoiler snapshots, and indexes for source and chronological history. Assets have unique storage key and `(ownerProfileId, checksum)`; scan and processing state are explicit. Outbox events have unique idempotency key and indexed availability/claim state. License policies have stable unique keys.\n+\n+Profiles and listings are mutable only through services. Published release payload fields, source identity, semantic version, and snapshots are never updated; deprecation, replacement, and moderation are separate metadata. Removed content stops delivery but retains listing/release IDs, checksums, attribution, ownership declarations, and audit meaning. No hard-delete workflow is provided.\n+\n+SQLite uses the same model through the Prisma schema; its closure migration adds claim fields but cannot add named foreign keys without a table rebuild. MySQL closure migration applies explicit foreign keys. Before a production SQLite relation hardening migration, back up and perform a table-rebuild migration in an approved window. This is a Phase 1 compatibility constraint, not permission to bypass service ownership checks.\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Privacy_and_Spoiler_Model.md\n@@\n-# Community Harbor privacy and spoiler model\n-\n-Public projections are allowlists. They exclude raw source snapshots, answers, variables, Captain notes, finale/chapter spoiler fields, invitations, sessions, participant identity, email, storage keys, moderation notes, and exact private locations. Private/unlisted records are non-enumerable; private real-world locations have no public projection.\n+# Community Harbor privacy and spoiler model\n+\n+Public projection types are deliberately separate: public profile, listing summary/detail, and release summary/detail; owner and moderator projections are separate positive allowlists. No public route returns an ORM record or installable manifest. Public fields are safe handle/display name, title, short description, normalized tags, item type, safe spoiler/location classification, and limited release metadata. Release pages do not expose private Chronicle payloads.\n+\n+The server excludes identity keys, PlayerProfile IDs, email, credentials, sessions, invitations, source drafts/snapshots, accepted answers, variables, Captain/Creator notes, finale/chapter content, participants, coordinates/routes, asset keys/paths, audit/outbox data, and moderation notes. Spoiler levels are NONE, PREVIEW_SAFE, MINOR, CHAPTER, FINALE, CREATOR_ONLY, CAPTAIN_ONLY, and PARTICIPANT_PRIVATE. Default public projection is NONE/PREVIEW_SAFE only; higher levels require future authorization/reveal policy.\n+\n+PRIVATE, CREW_ONLY, and UNLISTED records are excluded from enumeration. UNLISTED direct access requires the exact published slug and returns the same safe not-found result on failure. CREW_ONLY policy uses `PlaythroughMembership` for a Tale Session with the release source Tale. PRIVATE_REAL_WORLD has no public projection; APPROXIMATE_REAL_WORLD must be generalized by a future publishing flow. Search/Open Graph remains disabled for non-public records and must use the same projection service when introduced.\n+\n+Hostile fixtures use synthetic answers, notes, finale text, coordinates, invitations, email, session IDs, participants, unpublished paths, moderation/audit/outbox metadata, and raw source content. Tests must assert their absence from every public service and route response. Process-local rate limits are a current limitation; distributed enforcement is a later operational phase.\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Licensing_and_Attribution.md\n@@\n-# Community Harbor licensing and attribution\n-\n-Every release snapshots a policy key/version and ordered attribution. Policies cover all-rights-reserved, attribution use/remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom terms. Ownership declarations record accepted statements and evidence references; they are declarations, not legal verification.\n+# Community Harbor licensing and attribution\n+\n+Built-in policy keys are `ALL_RIGHTS_RESERVED`, `USE_IN_CHRONICLES_WITH_ATTRIBUTION`, `REMIX_WITH_ATTRIBUTION`, `NONCOMMERCIAL_REMIX_WITH_ATTRIBUTION`, `REDISTRIBUTE_UNMODIFIED_WITH_ATTRIBUTION`, `PUBLIC_DOMAIN_OR_CC0_STYLE`, and `CUSTOM`. Each policy records private/public use, modification, redistribution, commercial use, attribution/source-link/share-alike requirements, policy version, active state, and custom terms where applicable. These keys are platform policy labels, not unqualified assertions of Creative Commons legal compatibility.\n+\n+Every release stores an immutable license snapshot and ordered attribution snapshot. Attribution holds credited profile when available, immutable display credit, contribution type, required credit text, source reference, asset exception seam, and sort order. Profile suspension/deactivation never rewrites historical credits. An asset can later carry a stricter policy or separate source attribution without changing release history.\n+\n+Ownership declarations record declarant profile, version, timestamp, accepted statements, and optional evidence references. They state author permission and attribution acknowledgement but do not provide legal verification. Future remix compatibility must compare the release snapshots, not mutable Creator defaults.\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Release_Manifest.md\n@@\n-# Community Harbor release manifest\n-\n-Manifest schema 1 requires listing ID, semantic version, immutable source `PublishedTaleVersion` ID, public metadata, license snapshot, and ordered attribution. Canonical key-sorted JSON is SHA-256 checksummed. A release is immutable; deprecation/replacement changes delivery metadata only.\n+# Community Harbor release manifest\n+\n+Schema version 1 requires `listingId`, semantic version (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` with optional prerelease), immutable `sourcePublishedTaleVersionId`, public metadata, license key/version, and ordered attribution. Minimum platform version and compatibility metadata are stored alongside the manifest. The source service loads an actual `PublishedTaleVersion`, captures source Tale ID/checksum separately, and rejects draft IDs or source substitution.\n+\n+Canonical serialization recursively sorts object keys while retaining array order, then computes SHA-256 over UTF-8 JSON. Thus `{a:1,b:2}` and `{b:2,a:1}` have the same checksum while a changed value produces a new checksum. Test vector: schema 1/listing `l`/version `1.0.0` with stable metadata has the same checksum on repeated construction; changing title changes it.\n+\n+Public listing data is not an installable package. Raw snapshots, answers, hidden variables, notes, coordinates, private assets, and participant data are excluded. Immutable payload fields cannot be updated; corrections create a new release. Deprecation, replacement, and moderation delivery status are separate fields. Future schemas require a new manifest schema version and compatibility adapter; older releases remain identifiable.\n*** Update File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Community_Harbor_Storage_and_Job_Architecture.md\n@@\n-# Community Harbor storage and job architecture\n-\n-The local adapter isolates `staging`, immutable `releases`, and `quarantine`, with path safety and immutable-write protection. It is a development adapter only. CommunityOutboxEvent is the transactional dispatch record with sanitized payload, idempotency, availability, claim, attempts, processed time, and terminal failure. No production object store or worker is enabled.\n+# Community Harbor storage and job architecture\n+\n+`CommunityAssetStorageProvider` defines staged write, read, immutable copy, quarantine move, checksum, and metadata seams. The local adapter uses separate `staging`, `releases`, and `quarantine` namespaces beneath an owned root. It rejects traversal, absolute paths, separators, unsafe Unicode-like names, duplicate keys, and immutable overwrites. Staging and quarantine are never public. Asset records retain owner, checksum, declared/detected MIME, size, visibility, storage provider/key, accessibility metadata, scan state, processing state, and immutable release usage.\n+\n+`SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED` is not clean. Other scan states are NOT_REQUIRED, PENDING, SCANNING, CLEAN, SUSPICIOUS, MALICIOUS, and FAILED; processing states include UPLOADED, VALIDATING, SCANNING, PROCESSING, READY, FAILED, QUARANTINED, and REMOVED. The Phase 1 local adapter does not scan, transcode, parse 3D models, or deploy object storage.\n+\n+Business mutations enqueue sanitized, schema-versioned outbox events inside the same Prisma transaction. Claiming uses a persisted lease owner/expiry plus conditional update; a handler can mark processed only after success. Retry increments attempts and delays availability; exhaustion records terminal failure. This is an explicit development dispatcher seam, not a production worker. A future worker may use MySQL row locks/skip-locked or a queue provider, while SQLite uses short conditional claims. Back up the database and asset root together; failed storage/DB reconciliation requires manual recovery evidence.\n*** End Patch";
const result = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof result === "string" ? result : JSON.stringify(result));

\"COMMUNITY_PROFILE_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_PROFILE\", profile.id, { handle: profile.handle });\r\n    return ownerProfileProjection(profile);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getOwnProfile(actor: CommunityActor) {\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  return ownerProfileProjection(profile);\r\n}\r\nexport async function updateOwnProfile(\r\n  actor: CommunityActor,\r\n  input: {\r\n    handle?: string;\r\n    displayName?: string;\r\n    biography?: string | null;\r\n    visibility?: \"PRIVATE\" | \"COMMUNITY\";\r\n    supportedLanguages?: string[];\r\n  },\r\n) {\r\n  rate(actor, \"community-profile-update\", 15);\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const handle = input.handle === undefined ? undefined : normalizeHandle(input.handle);\r\n  if (input.displayName !== undefined && (!input.displayName.trim() || input.displayName.length > 120))\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_INVALID_PROFILE\", \"Display name is invalid.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityProfile.update({\r\n      where: { id: profile.id },\r\n      data: {\r\n        ...(handle ? { handle, normalizedHandle: handle } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.displayName !== undefined ? { displayName: input.displayName.trim() } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.biography !== undefined ? { biography: input.biography?.trim() || null } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.visibility ? { visibility: input.visibility } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.supportedLanguages ? { supportedLanguages: JSON.stringify(input.supportedLanguages) } : {}),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_PROFILE_UPDATED\", \"COMMUNITY_PROFILE\", updated.id, { handle: updated.handle });\r\n    await audit(\r\n      tx,\r\n      actor,\r\n      handle ? \"COMMUNITY_HANDLE_CHANGED\" : \"COMMUNITY_PROFILE_UPDATED\",\r\n      \"COMMUNITY_PROFILE\",\r\n      updated.id,\r\n      { handle: updated.handle },\r\n    );\r\n    return ownerProfileProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function getPublicProfile(handle: string) {\r\n  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { normalizedHandle: normalizeHandle(handle) } });\r\n  if (!profile || profile.visibility !== \"COMMUNITY\" || profile.moderationStatus !== \"ACTIVE\") return null;\r\n  return publicProfileProjection(profile);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createListing(actor: CommunityActor, raw: unknown) {\r\n  rate(actor, \"community-listing-create\", 10);\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const input = listingInputSchema.parse(raw);\r\n  if (input.visibility === \"FEATURED\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Only editorial staff may feature content.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const listing = await tx.communityListing.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        ownerProfileId: profile.id,\r\n        slug: input.slug,\r\n        itemType: input.itemType,\r\n        title: input.title,\r\n        shortDescription: input.shortDescription ?? null,\r\n        longDescription: input.longDescription ?? null,\r\n        visibility: input.visibility,\r\n        spoilerLevel: input.spoilerLevel,\r\n        locationClass: input.locationClass,\r\n        tags: JSON.stringify([...new Set(input.tags.map((tag) => tag.toLowerCase()))]),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_LISTING_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", listing.id, {\r\n      slug: listing.slug,\r\n      itemType: listing.itemType,\r\n    });\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_LISTING_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", listing.id, { slug: listing.slug });\r\n    return ownerListingProjection(listing);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function getOwnerListing(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const listing = await db.communityListing.findFirst({ where: { id, ownerProfileId: profile.id } });\r\n  if (!listing) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_LISTING_NOT_FOUND\", \"Listing not found.\");\r\n  return ownerListingProjection(listing);\r\n}\r\nexport async function updateListing(actor: CommunityActor, id: string, raw: unknown) {\r\n  rate(actor, \"community-listing-update\", 20);\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const input = listingInputSchema.partial().strict().parse(raw);\r\n  const listing = await db.communityListing.findFirst({ where: { id, ownerProfileId: profile.id } });\r\n  if (!listing) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot edit this listing.\");\r\n  if (input.visibility === \"FEATURED\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Only editorial staff may feature content.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityListing.update({\r\n      where: { id },\r\n      data: {\r\n        ...(input.slug ? { slug: input.slug } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.itemType ? { itemType: input.itemType } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.title ? { title: input.title } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.shortDescription !== undefined ? { shortDescription: input.shortDescription } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.longDescription !== undefined ? { longDescription: input.longDescription } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.visibility ? { visibility: input.visibility } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.spoilerLevel ? { spoilerLevel: input.spoilerLevel } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.locationClass ? { locationClass: input.locationClass } : {}),\r\n        ...(input.tags ? { tags: JSON.stringify([...new Set(input.tags.map((tag) => tag.toLowerCase()))]) } : {}),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_LISTING_UPDATED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", id, { slug: updated.slug });\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_LISTING_UPDATED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", id, { slug: updated.slug });\r\n    return ownerListingProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function transitionListing(\r\n  actor: CommunityActor,\r\n  id: string,\r\n  next:\r\n    | \"VALIDATING\"\r\n    | \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\r\n    | \"IN_REVIEW\"\r\n    | \"PUBLISHED\"\r\n    | \"UPDATE_PENDING\"\r\n    | \"QUARANTINED\"\r\n    | \"REJECTED\"\r\n    | \"ARCHIVED\"\r\n    | \"REMOVED\",\r\n) {\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const listing = await db.communityListing.findFirst({ where: { id, ownerProfileId: profile.id } });\r\n  if (!listing) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot change this listing.\");\r\n  assertTransition(listing.publicationStatus as Parameters<typeof assertTransition>[0], next);\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityListing.update({ where: { id }, data: { publicationStatus: next } });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_PUBLICATION_STATE_CHANGED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", id, { status: next });\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_PUBLICATION_STATE_CHANGED\", \"COMMUNITY_LISTING\", id, { status: next });\r\n    return ownerListingProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function listPublicListingsFoundation() {\r\n  const listings = await db.communityListing.findMany({\r\n    where: {\r\n      publicationStatus: \"PUBLISHED\",\r\n      visibility: { in: [\"COMMUNITY\", \"FEATURED\"] },\r\n      moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n      locationClass: { not: \"PRIVATE_REAL_WORLD\" },\r\n    },\r\n    include: { owner: true },\r\n    orderBy: { updatedAt: \"desc\" },\r\n  });\r\n  return listings.map(publicListingProjection);\r\n}\r\nexport async function getPublicListingBySlug(slug: string) {\r\n  const listing = await db.communityListing.findFirst({\r\n    where: {\r\n      slug,\r\n      publicationStatus: \"PUBLISHED\",\r\n      visibility: { in: [\"COMMUNITY\", \"FEATURED\"] },\r\n      moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n      locationClass: { not: \"PRIVATE_REAL_WORLD\" },\r\n    },\r\n    include: { owner: true, currentRelease: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return listing ? publicListingProjection(listing) : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createRelease(actor: CommunityActor, listingId: string, raw: unknown) {\r\n  rate(actor, \"community-release-create\", 5);\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const request = releaseManifestSchema.parse(raw);\r\n  if (request.listingId !== listingId)\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_SOURCE_VERSION_MISMATCH\", \"Release manifest targets another listing.\");\r\n  if (process.env.COMMUNITY_ENABLED === \"false\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_NOT_ENABLED\", \"Community Harbor is disabled.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const listing = await tx.communityListing.findFirst({ where: { id: listingId, ownerProfileId: profile.id } });\r\n    if (!listing) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot release this listing.\");\r\n    const source = await tx.publishedTaleVersion.findUnique({ where: { id: request.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId } });\r\n    if (!source || !source.isCurrent)\r\n      throw new CommunityError(\r\n        \"COMMUNITY_SOURCE_NOT_IMMUTABLE\",\r\n        \"An immutable published Chronicle version is required.\",\r\n      );\r\n    const publicMetadata = sanitizeChronicleMetadata(JSON.parse(source.contentSnapshot) as Record<string, unknown>);\r\n    const manifest = releaseManifestSchema.parse({\r\n      ...request,\r\n      publicMetadata: { ...request.publicMetadata, ...publicMetadata },\r\n    });\r\n    const checksum = manifestChecksum(manifest);\r\n    const release = await tx.communityRelease.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        listingId,\r\n        semanticVersion: manifest.semanticVersion,\r\n        manifestSchemaVersion: manifest.schemaVersion,\r\n        sourcePublishedTaleVersionId: source.id,\r\n        manifest: JSON.stringify(manifest),\r\n        manifestChecksum: checksum,\r\n        compatibility: JSON.stringify({ sourceTaleId: source.taleId, sourceChecksum: source.checksum }),\r\n        licenseSnapshot: JSON.stringify(manifest.license),\r\n        attributionSnapshot: JSON.stringify(manifest.attribution),\r\n        spoilerSnapshot: JSON.stringify({ level: manifest.publicMetadata.spoilerLevel }),\r\n        publishedByProfileId: profile.id,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.communityListing.update({\r\n      where: { id: listingId },\r\n      data: { currentReleaseId: release.id, publicationStatus: \"UPDATE_PENDING\" },\r\n    });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", release.id, {\r\n      listingId,\r\n      semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion,\r\n      checksum: release.manifestChecksum,\r\n    });\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_CREATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", release.id, {\r\n      listingId,\r\n      semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion,\r\n    });\r\n    return ownerReleaseProjection(release);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function getPublicRelease(id: string) {\r\n  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({\r\n    where: {\r\n      id,\r\n      moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n      listing: {\r\n        publicationStatus: \"PUBLISHED\",\r\n        visibility: { in: [\"COMMUNITY\", \"FEATURED\"] },\r\n        moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n      },\r\n    },\r\n    include: { listing: { include: { owner: true } } },\r\n  });\r\n  return release ? publicReleaseProjection(release) : null;\r\n}\r\nexport async function getOwnerRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id, listing: { ownerProfileId: profile.id } } });\r\n  if (!release) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_NOT_FOUND\", \"Release not found.\");\r\n  return ownerReleaseProjection(release);\r\n}\r\nexport async function deprecateRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string, replacementReleaseId?: string) {\r\n  const profile = await ownProfile(actor);\r\n  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({ where: { id, listing: { ownerProfileId: profile.id } } });\r\n  if (!release) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot deprecate this release.\");\r\n  if (replacementReleaseId) {\r\n    const replacement = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({\r\n      where: { id: replacementReleaseId, listingId: release.listingId },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!replacement)\r\n      throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_NOT_FOUND\", \"Replacement must belong to the same listing.\");\r\n  }\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({\r\n      where: { id },\r\n      data: { deprecatedAt: new Date(), replacementReleaseId: replacementReleaseId ?? null },\r\n    });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_DEPRECATED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, { replacementReleaseId });\r\n    await audit(\r\n      tx,\r\n      actor,\r\n      replacementReleaseId ? \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_REPLACEMENT_SET\" : \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_DEPRECATED\",\r\n      \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\",\r\n      id,\r\n      { replacementReleaseId },\r\n    );\r\n    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function quarantineRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\r\n  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({ where: { id }, data: { moderationStatus: \"QUARANTINED\" } });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_QUARANTINED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\r\n    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function restoreRelease(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\r\n  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const updated = await tx.communityRelease.update({ where: { id }, data: { moderationStatus: \"ACTIVE\" } });\r\n    await outbox(tx, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\r\n    await audit(tx, actor, \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_RESTORED\", \"COMMUNITY_RELEASE\", id, {});\r\n    return ownerReleaseProjection(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function verifyReleaseIntegrity(actor: CommunityActor, id: string) {\r\n  const release = await getOwnerRelease(actor, id);\r\n  const stored = await db.communityRelease.findUnique({ where: { id } });\r\n  if (!stored) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_RELEASE_NOT_FOUND\", \"Release not found.\");\r\n  const manifest = releaseManifestSchema.parse(JSON.parse(stored.manifest));\r\n  return { ...release, valid: manifestChecksum(manifest) === stored.manifestChecksum };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function publicProfileProjection(profile: {\r\n  handle: string;\r\n  displayName: string;\r\n  biography: string | null;\r\n  verificationStatus: string;\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    handle: profile.handle,\r\n    displayName: profile.displayName,\r\n    biography: profile.biography,\r\n    verificationStatus: profile.verificationStatus,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nexport function ownerProfileProjection(profile: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  handle: string;\r\n  displayName: string;\r\n  biography: string | null;\r\n  visibility: string;\r\n  supportedLanguages: string;\r\n  creatorStatus: string;\r\n  verificationStatus: string;\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    id: profile.id,\r\n    handle: profile.handle,\r\n    displayName: profile.displayName,\r\n    biography: profile.biography,\r\n    visibility: profile.visibility,\r\n    supportedLanguages: JSON.parse(profile.supportedLanguages),\r\n    creatorStatus: profile.creatorStatus,\r\n    verificationStatus: profile.verificationStatus,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nexport function ownerListingProjection(listing: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  slug: string;\r\n  itemType: string;\r\n  title: string;\r\n  publicationStatus: string;\r\n  visibility: string;\r\n  spoilerLevel: string;\r\n  locationClass: string;\r\n  currentReleaseId: string | null;\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    id: listing.id,\r\n    slug: listing.slug,\r\n    itemType: listing.itemType,\r\n    title: listing.title,\r\n    publicationStatus: listing.publicationStatus,\r\n    visibility: listing.visibility,\r\n    spoilerLevel: listing.spoilerLevel,\r\n    locationClass: listing.locationClass,\r\n    currentReleaseId: listing.currentReleaseId,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nexport function publicListingProjection(listing: {\r\n  slug: string;\r\n  itemType: string;\r\n  title: string;\r\n  shortDescription: string | null;\r\n  tags: string;\r\n  spoilerLevel: string;\r\n  locationClass: string;\r\n  owner: { handle: string; displayName: string };\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    slug: listing.slug,\r\n    itemType: listing.itemType,\r\n    title: listing.title,\r\n    shortDescription: listing.shortDescription,\r\n    tags: JSON.parse(listing.tags),\r\n    spoilerLevel: listing.spoilerLevel === \"NONE\" ? \"NONE\" : \"PREVIEW_SAFE\",\r\n    locationClass: listing.locationClass,\r\n    creator: { handle: listing.owner.handle, displayName: listing.owner.displayName },\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nexport function ownerReleaseProjection(release: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  listingId: string;\r\n  semanticVersion: string;\r\n  manifestChecksum: string;\r\n  sourcePublishedTaleVersionId: string | null;\r\n  moderationStatus: string;\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    id: release.id,\r\n    listingId: release.listingId,\r\n    semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion,\r\n    manifestChecksum: release.manifestChecksum,\r\n    sourcePublishedTaleVersionId: release.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId,\r\n    moderationStatus: release.moderationStatus,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nexport function publicReleaseProjection(release: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  semanticVersion: string;\r\n  manifestSchemaVersion: number;\r\n  minimumPlatformVersion: string | null;\r\n  releaseNotes: string | null;\r\n  manifestChecksum: string;\r\n  licenseSnapshot: string;\r\n  attributionSnapshot: string;\r\n}) {\r\n  return {\r\n    id: release.id,\r\n    semanticVersion: release.semanticVersion,\r\n    manifestSchemaVersion: release.manifestSchemaVersion,\r\n    minimumPlatformVersion: release.minimumPlatformVersion,\r\n    releaseNotes: release.releaseNotes,\r\n    manifestChecksum: release.manifestChecksum,\r\n    license: JSON.parse(release.licenseSnapshot),\r\n    attribution: JSON.parse(release.attributionSnapshot),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nexport type OutboxHandler = (event: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  eventType: string;\r\n  aggregateType: string;\r\n  aggregateId: string;\r\n  payload: string;\r\n}) => Promise<void>;\r\n\r\nexport async function claimAvailableEvents(workerId: string, limit = 25, leaseMs = 30_000) {\r\n  const now = new Date();\r\n  const candidates = await db.communityOutboxEvent.findMany({\r\n    where: {\r\n      processedAt: null,\r\n      terminalFailureAt: null,\r\n      availableAt: { lte: now },\r\n      OR: [{ claimExpiresAt: null }, { claimExpiresAt: { lt: now } }],\r\n    },\r\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" },\r\n    take: limit,\r\n    select: { id: true },\r\n  });\r\n  const claimed: string[] = [];\r\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\r\n    const result = await db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({\r\n      where: {\r\n        id: candidate.id,\r\n        processedAt: null,\r\n        terminalFailureAt: null,\r\n        OR: [{ claimExpiresAt: null }, { claimExpiresAt: { lt: now } }],\r\n      },\r\n      data: { claimedAt: now, claimOwner: workerId, claimExpiresAt: new Date(now.getTime() + leaseMs) },\r\n    });\r\n    if (result.count) claimed.push(candidate.id);\r\n  }\r\n  return db.communityOutboxEvent.findMany({ where: { id: { in: claimed } }, orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" } });\r\n}\r\nexport async function markEventProcessed(id: string, workerId: string) {\r\n  return db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({\r\n    where: { id, claimOwner: workerId, processedAt: null },\r\n    data: { processedAt: new Date(), claimExpiresAt: null },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function markEventRetryableFailure(id: string, workerId: string, failureCode: string) {\r\n  const event = await db.communityOutboxEvent.findFirst({ where: { id, claimOwner: workerId, processedAt: null } });\r\n  if (!event) return false;\r\n  const attempts = event.attemptCount + 1;\r\n  await db.communityOutboxEvent.update({\r\n    where: { id },\r\n    data:\r\n      attempts >= event.maxAttempts\r\n        ? { attemptCount: attempts, terminalFailureAt: new Date(), failureCode, claimExpiresAt: null }\r\n        : {\r\n            attemptCount: attempts,\r\n            failureCode,\r\n            claimOwner: null,\r\n            claimedAt: null,\r\n            claimExpiresAt: null,\r\n            availableAt: new Date(Date.now() + attempts * 1_000),\r\n          },\r\n  });\r\n  return true;\r\n}\r\nexport async function releaseExpiredClaims() {\r\n  return db.communityOutboxEvent.updateMany({\r\n    where: { processedAt: null, terminalFailureAt: null, claimExpiresAt: { lt: new Date() } },\r\n    data: { claimOwner: null, claimedAt: null, claimExpiresAt: null },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function dispatchOutboxBatch(workerId: string, handler: OutboxHandler, limit = 25) {\r\n  const events = await claimAvailableEvents(workerId, limit);\r\n  let processed = 0;\r\n  for (const event of events) {\r\n    try {\r\n      await handler(event);\r\n      if ((await markEventProcessed(event.id, workerId)).count) processed += 1;\r\n    } catch {\r\n      await markEventRetryableFailure(event.id, workerId, \"HANDLER_FAILED\");\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  return { claimed: events.length, processed };\r\n}\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\nimport { CommunityError } from \"./domain\";\r\nimport type { CommunityActor } from \"./services\";\r\nimport type { CommunityAssetStorageProvider } from \"./storage\";\r\n\r\nexport async function registerStagedAsset(\r\n  actor: CommunityActor,\r\n  storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider,\r\n  input: { name: string; bytes: Uint8Array; declaredMimeType: string; detectedMimeType?: string },\r\n) {\r\n  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\r\n  if (!profile || profile.moderationStatus !== \"ACTIVE\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"An active Community Profile is required.\");\r\n  const stored = await storage.putStagedObject(profile.id, input.name, input.bytes);\r\n  return db.communityAssetReference.create({\r\n    data: {\r\n      ownerProfileId: profile.id,\r\n      checksum: stored.checksum,\r\n      declaredMimeType: input.declaredMimeType,\r\n      detectedMimeType: input.detectedMimeType ?? null,\r\n      fileSize: input.bytes.byteLength,\r\n      storageProvider: \"local\",\r\n      storageKey: stored.key,\r\n      scanStatus: \"SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED\",\r\n      processingStatus: \"UPLOADED\",\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function attachAssetToRelease(\r\n  actor: CommunityActor,\r\n  storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider,\r\n  assetId: string,\r\n  releaseId: string,\r\n) {\r\n  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\r\n  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findFirst({ where: { id: assetId, ownerProfileId: profile?.id } });\r\n  const release = await db.communityRelease.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id: releaseId, listing: { ownerProfileId: profile?.id } },\r\n  });\r\n  if (!asset || !release) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot attach this asset.\");\r\n  const copied = await storage.copyToImmutableRelease(asset.storageKey, releaseId);\r\n  if (copied.checksum !== asset.checksum)\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_STORAGE_ERROR\", \"Asset checksum changed during immutable copy.\");\r\n  return db.communityAssetReference.update({\r\n    where: { id: asset.id },\r\n    data: { releaseId, storageKey: copied.key, processingStatus: \"READY\" },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\nexport async function readAuthorizedCommunityAsset(\r\n  actor: CommunityActor,\r\n  storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider,\r\n  assetId: string,\r\n) {\r\n  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\r\n  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id: assetId, ownerProfileId: profile?.id, removedAt: null },\r\n  });\r\n  if (!asset) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Asset not found.\");\r\n  return storage.readObject(asset.storageKey);\r\n}\r\nexport async function quarantineAsset(actor: CommunityActor, storage: CommunityAssetStorageProvider, assetId: string) {\r\n  if (actor.role !== \"MODERATOR\" && actor.role !== \"ADMIN\")\r\n    throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"Moderation capability is required.\");\r\n  const asset = await db.communityAssetReference.findUnique({ where: { id: assetId } });\r\n  if (!asset) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_STORAGE_ERROR\", \"Asset not found.\");\r\n  const moved = await storage.moveToQuarantine(asset.storageKey);\r\n  return db.communityAssetReference.update({\r\n    where: { id: asset.id },\r\n    data: { storageKey: moved.key, scanStatus: \"SUSPICIOUS\", processingStatus: \"QUARANTINED\", visibility: \"PRIVATE\" },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

409\r\n              : 400;\r\n    return NextResponse.json(\r\n      { code: cause.code, error: cause.message },\r\n      { status, headers: cause.code === \"COMMUNITY_RATE_LIMITED\" ? { \"Retry-After\": \"60\" } : undefined },\r\n    );\r\n  }\r\n  return NextResponse.json(\r\n    { code: \"COMMUNITY_TRANSACTION_FAILED\", error: \"The Community Harbor request could not be completed.\" },\r\n    { status: 400 },\r\n  );\r\n}\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\nimport { CommunityError } from \"./domain\";\r\nimport type { CommunityActor } from \"./services\";\r\n\r\nexport async function canViewCommunityListing(actor: CommunityActor | null, listingId: string, unlistedSlug?: string) {\r\n  const listing = await db.communityListing.findUnique({ where: { id: listingId }, include: { currentRelease: true } });\r\n  if (!listing || listing.moderationStatus !== \"ACTIVE\" || listing.publicationStatus !== \"PUBLISHED\") return false;\r\n  const profile = actor\r\n    ? await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } })\r\n    : null;\r\n  if (profile?.id === listing.ownerProfileId || actor?.role === \"MODERATOR\" || actor?.role === \"ADMIN\") return true;\r\n  if (listing.visibility === \"COMMUNITY\" || listing.visibility === \"FEATURED\") return true;\r\n  if (listing.visibility === \"UNLISTED\") return Boolean(unlistedSlug && unlistedSlug === listing.slug);\r\n  if (listing.visibility !== \"CREW_ONLY\" || !actor || !listing.currentRelease?.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId)\r\n    return false;\r\n  const source = await db.publishedTaleVersion.findUnique({\r\n    where: { id: listing.currentRelease.sourcePublishedTaleVersionId },\r\n    select: { taleId: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return Boolean(\r\n    source &&\r\n      (await db.playthroughMembership.findFirst({\r\n        where: {\r\n          playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId,\r\n          status: { in: [\"INVITED\", \"ACCEPTED\", \"READY\", \"ACTIVE_MEMBER\", \"COMPLETED_MEMBER\"] },\r\n          playthrough: { taleId: source.taleId },\r\n        },\r\n        select: { id: true },\r\n      })),\r\n  );\r\n}\r\nexport async function requireListingOwner(actor: CommunityActor, listingId: string) {\r\n  const profile = await db.communityProfile.findUnique({ where: { playerProfileId: actor.playerProfileId } });\r\n  const allowed =\r\n    profile &&\r\n    (await db.communityListing.findFirst({\r\n      where: { id: listingId, ownerProfileId: profile.id },\r\n      select: { id: true },\r\n    }));\r\n  if (!allowed) throw new CommunityError(\"COMMUNITY_ACCESS_DENIED\", \"You cannot access this listing.\");\r\n  return profile;\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

validation,Mandatory,PARTIAL,Focused/unit/static,Run closure gate and record,Development_Docs,scripts,repository commands,PENDING,,,\r\n# Project Harborlight Phase 1 - Design Record\r\n\r\n## Baseline and ownership\r\n\r\n- Branch: `codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor`\r\n- Base and latest accepted integrated `origin/main`: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\r\n- Database owner: Harborlight Phase 1. This work does not merge One Voyage (`4e8f385`), Wayfarer (`a478d79`), or Sealed Hold (`4b93b4f`).\r\n\r\n## Repository inventory and boundaries\r\n\r\n`TallTale` is the canonical authored Chronicle; `TaleDraft` is mutable authoring state; `PublishedTaleVersion` is immutable source content; `TaleSession` is a live voyage; old `Campaign` tables remain production compatibility surfaces. `PlayerProfile` is the currently available canonical person/profile; `PlatformAuditEvent`, `TaleAssetStorage`, `consumeRateLimit`, and the Lanternwake scene registry are existing seams. Harborlight creates no alternate Chronicle, chapter, session, or login identity.\r\n\r\n`CommunityListing` is mutable catalog identity and release history. `CommunityRelease` is immutable distribution identity, bound by scalar source ID to an immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`; its manifest, checksum, attribution, license, and spoiler snapshots never change. A later correction creates a new release; listing quarantine/removal controls delivery without rewriting the release.\r\n\r\n## Convergence seams\r\n\r\n`CommunityProfile.identityKey` plus optional `playerProfileId` represents current Player Profile identity without copying credentials, email, sessions, or Player-only state. During Wayfarer convergence, the adapter maps `identityKey` to `UserAccount` and migrates only the optional compatibility pointer. Chronicle references are source-version IDs, keeping One Voyage terminology/domain ownership isolated. Sealed Hold remains the private encrypted transport authority; Harborlight owns community staging, immutable public/unlisted releases, checksums, and future distribution only.\r\n\r\n## Frozen policy\r\n\r\nTyped taxonomy has 14 item types with declared release/install/remix/search capabilities. Publication transitions are centralized; visibility is independent; public delivery allows only published COMMUNITY/FEATURED records. Unlisted and private records are never enumerable. Spoilers and private real-world locations are enforced at projection time, not CSS. Public projections are strict allowlists and exclude answers, hidden variables, Captain notes, invitation/session data, private coordinates, email, storage paths, raw drafts, and moderation notes.\r\n\r\nRelease manifests use schema version 1, canonical JSON SHA-256, semantic versions, required immutable source IDs, license snapshot, and ordered attribution snapshot. License policies support reserved, attribution, remix, noncommercial remix, unmodified redistribution, public-domain-style, and custom keys. Ownership declarations are auditable. Releases retain attribution through profile deletion/deactivation.\r\n\r\nCommunity assets use explicit scan/processing states; `SCAN_NOT_CONFIGURED` is never clean. The local provider separates `staging`, `releases`, and `quarantine`, rejects traversal/absolute/separator tricks, and refuses immutable overwrite. A typed outbox model carries sanitized schema-versioned payloads and idempotency; a future durable dispatcher must set `processedAt` only after success.\r\n\r\nCommunity scenes remain future contracts under the existing Lanternwake Director and inherited reduced-motion/cleanup policies; no scene is production-ready in Phase 1.\r\n\r\n## Migration and rollback\r\n\r\nSchema changes are additive and use new, unique SQLite/MySQL migration identifiers. No legacy table is renamed, deleted, or repurposed. Rollback is application rollback plus restore of a matched database/asset backup; no destructive down migration is supplied. Phase 2 must add public publishing/review workflow, Wayfarer account relation, durable dispatcher/object storage, and combined convergence tests.\r\n# Harborlight Phase 1 Integration Manifest\r\n\r\n## Closure baseline\r\n\r\n- Branch: `codex/project-harborlight-phase1-chart-the-harbor`\r\n- Base/latest `origin/main`: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\r\n- Initial candidate: `c1daa766ec8fe6dba9f0848c2315e6de14e2e1c0`\r\n- Closure commits: recorded by Git history beginning at `05d3df6fe`.\r\n\r\n## Additive surface\r\n\r\nModels: CommunityProfile, CommunityListing, CommunityRelease, CommunityReleaseAttribution, CommunityLicensePolicy, CommunityOwnershipDeclaration, CommunityAssetReference, CommunityOutboxEvent. Current relations attach profiles to PlayerProfile, listings to owners/current release, releases to listing/publisher/immutable source, attribution/declarations/assets to their aggregate. Unique constraints: normalized handle, listing slug, listing/version, storage key, owner/checksum, outbox idempotency. Indexes cover profile moderation/visibility, listing ownership/state, release source/version, attribution order, asset release/visibility, and outbox dispatch/leases.\r\n\r\nSQLite migrations: `20260721140000_project_harborlight_phase1`, `20260721150000_project_harborlight_relations_outbox`; MySQL migrations: `0006_project_harborlight_phase1`, `0007_project_harborlight_relations_outbox`. Environment: `COMMUNITY_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_PUBLISHING_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_MODERATION_REQUIRED`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_STORAGE_PROVIDER`, `COMMUNITY_ASSET_ROOT`, `COMMUNITY_WORKER_ENABLED`, `COMMUNITY_RATE_LIMIT_PROFILE`, `COMMUNITY_PUBLIC_BASE_URL`.\r\n\r\n## Convergence risks and order\r\n\r\nOne Voyage owns canonical Chronicle terminology/domain consolidation; merge it first, then retarget scalar source seams. Wayfarer owns `UserAccount`; next migrate `identityKey` adapter without copying identity secrets. Sealed Hold remains private encrypted package transport and should reconcile only shared checksum/storage utilities. Then run schema migration, public-projection hostile fixtures, IDOR/visibility tests, exact-version release tests, and storage/outbox integration tests on both SQLite and MySQL.\r\n\r\nDeferred destructive work: no legacy migration/deletion, no auth replacement, no package import/export, no public upload/publishing, no production object storage/worker. Retained compatibility: Campaign, PlayerProfile, TallTale, and current Lanternwake registry. The Community contract registry is future-only and does not add a second director.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

non-moderator states are restricted,,\n+HL-026,33,SQLite relations,Enforce database foreign keys in an additive migration,Mandatory,FAIL,ORM-only relation claim,Rebuild only additive SQLite community tables,prisma/migrations/20260721160000_project_harborlight_sqlite_foreign_keys/migration.sql,,Python sqlite3 supplementary migration proof,PASS,All migrations applied and CommunityRelease=4 CommunityListing=2 foreign keys,,\n+HL-027,33,Prisma migration support,Run supported Prisma migrate deploy on isolated SQLite,Mandatory,PARTIAL,Raw sqlite proof only,Execute Prisma migration engine on isolated database,prisma/migrations,,prisma migrate deploy,BLOCKED_EXTERNAL,Prisma schema engine emits empty Schema engine error despite executable version check,No diagnostic from Prisma 6.19.3 engine on this Windows host,\n+HL-028,43,Build gate,Run isolated next production build,Mandatory,PARTIAL,Not run,Install a local validation dependency tree and build,package-lock.json,,next build,BLOCKED_EXTERNAL,Turbopack rejects the registered validation worktree node_modules junction outside project root,Tool policy blocked removal of that verified disposable junction,\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Harborlight-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Project_Harborlight_Phase_1_Acceptance_Closure_Report.md\n+# Project Harborlight Phase 1 Acceptance Closure Report\n+\n+## Recorded closure state\n+\n+This report records the closure work performed after initial candidate\n+`c1daa766ec8fe6dba9f0848c2315e6de14e2e1c0`, on the dedicated Harborlight\n+branch and registered worktree. The canonical checkout was not modified.\n+The actual ending SHA is intentionally filled in only after the final\n+documentation commit and push; it must be read from Git rather than guessed.\n+\n+## Completed repository-local closure work\n+\n+The closure adds database-backed Community Profile, Listing, and immutable\n+Release services and CSRF-protected owner routes. The service layer resolves\n+the existing `PlayerProfile` through an optional relation, never stores\n+credentials or session data, and keeps the Wayfarer `UserAccount` conversion\n+behind the documented adapter seam. Releases read the exact\n+`PublishedTaleVersion` inside their write transaction, persist a canonical\n+manifest checksum, retain license/attribution snapshots, and never mutate an\n+existing release in place.\n+\n+The listing state matrix is now enforced in the service. A creator can submit\n+the owned intake path only; review, publication, quarantine, removal, and\n+profile suspension require the future canonical moderation-role adapter. The\n+public projections remain strict allowlists and do not return source content,\n+location precision, storage paths, account data, or moderation material.\n+\n+The outbox now uses an idempotency key derived from the sanitized payload,\n+atomic persisted leases, retry backoff, and explicit terminal failure state.\n+SQLite receives an additive table-rebuild migration that preserves data and\n+adds real foreign keys; no canonical table is changed. MySQL receives the\n+matching additive foreign-key migration. Lanternwake remains the only motion\n+authority; its Harborlight scene entries are explicit future contracts, not\n+production-ready scenes.\n+\n+## Isolated validation evidence\n+\n+- Prisma SQLite schema validation passed with `DATABASE_URL` set to an\n+  isolated validation database.\n+- TypeScript passed at `f678086fe5b833dcf471e81a47d4249213810174` in the\n+  local registered validation worktree.\n+- The complete SQLite migration chain was applied to a newly-created isolated\n+  database with Python `sqlite3` as a supplementary check. The resulting\n+  `CommunityRelease` table reported four foreign keys and `CommunityListing`\n+  reported two. This confirms the SQL migration chain itself, but does not\n+  substitute for the required Prisma migration-engine gate.\n+- Earlier closure validation completed all 88 unit-test files / 914 tests.\n+\n+## External validation blockers retained honestly\n+\n+Prisma 6.19.3's Windows schema engine returns only `Schema engine error:`\n+when `migrate deploy` is run against a new isolated SQLite file, even after\n+pointing `PRISMA_SCHEMA_ENGINE_BINARY` at the installed executable (which\n+prints its version successfully). This is an environment/tooling failure with\n+no actionable diagnostic, not a reason to modify a canonical database.\n+\n+Next production build was attempted in the local registered validation\n+worktree. Turbopack correctly rejects its `node_modules` junction because it\n+points outside that worktree root. The junction target was verified as the\n+local development runtime; removing it to install a local dependency tree was\n+blocked by the execution policy. No source dependency lockfile or canonical\n+runtime was changed.\n+\n+No isolated MySQL credential set or safe local MySQL client is available.\n+Existing unrelated EOAT MySQL processes were observed but deliberately never\n+contacted. Therefore MySQL migration execution is an external environment\n+blocker; the migration is included and reviewed as additive SQL only.\n+\n+## Required next acceptance action\n+\n+Run the supplied migration and build gates from a normal local filesystem\n+worktree with a real `node_modules` directory, then run the MySQL migration\n+against a newly provisioned isolated database. Re-run the unit suite after\n+the final documentation commit. Until those gates are green, this record is a\n+preserved implementation candidate, not an acceptance declaration.\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);text(typeof r === "string" ? r : JSON.stringify(r));
